instructions: Start on hands and knees with core braced. Lift one bent leg out to the side, keeping hips level. Lower leg with control, avoiding jerky movements. Repeat on the other side.

name: Windmills
description: Stand with feet wide and arms extended to the sides at shoulder height, then hinge at the hips and rotate your torso to reach one hand toward the opposite foot while the other arm stays stacked above, alternating sides to stretch the hips, hamstrings, and torso.
instructions: Stand with feet wide and arms extended to the sides at shoulder height. Hinge at the hips and rotate your torso to reach one hand toward the opposite foot. Keep the other arm stacked above, then alternate sides to stretch the hips, hamstrings, and torso. Repeat on both sides, maintaining control and balance throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Side Leg Raises
description: Lie on your side with legs straight and stacked. Keeping your core engaged and hips stacked, slowly lift the top leg upward in a controlled motion, then lower it back down without letting it rest completely. Repeat for reps, then switch sides. This exercise targets the outer hips and thighs while helping improve hip mobility and lateral stability.
instructions: Lie on your side with legs straight and stacked. Engage your core and lift the top leg upward in a controlled motion. Lower the leg back down without resting it completely, then repeat for reps. Switch sides to complete the exercise.

name: Lateral Hop
description: The lateral hop is an explosive two-legged side-to-side jump that targets the quadriceps, glutes, and hamstrings while providing a strong cardiovascular challenge. It is typically performed for time or repetitions and works well in fat-loss or athleticism-focused circuits.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart. Jump sideways to one side, landing on both feet. Immediately jump sideways to the other side, repeating the motion. Keep knees slightly bent to absorb the impact and maintain balance.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Monster Walk
description: A lateral stepping exercise performed in a partial squat position to strengthen the hip abductors and glutes while improving hip stability.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart in a partial squat position. Take a large step to one side, keeping the back straight and knees bent. Bring the other foot to meet the first foot, while maintaining the squat position. Repeat on the other side, alternating steps to continue the exercise.

name: Side Lying Groin Stretch
description: Lie on your side with the bottom leg straight and the top leg bent at the hip and knee, placing the top foot flat on the floor in front of your bottom thigh. Gently let the bent knee drop toward the floor while keeping your hips stacked and core engaged, feeling a stretch along the inner thigh of the bottom leg. Hold the position without bouncing and breathe steadily throughout the stretch.
instructions: Lie on your side with the bottom leg straight and the top leg bent. Place the top foot flat on the floor in front of your bottom thigh. Gently let the bent knee drop toward the floor, keeping your hips stacked and core engaged, to feel the stretch in your bottom leg. Hold the position and breathe steadily.

name: IT Band and Glute Stretch
description: From a lying or seated position, cross one leg over the other and gently pull the knee across your body to stretch the outer hip and IT band while also lengthening the glute muscles. Hold the position without bouncing and breathe steadily, focusing on a mild to moderate stretch along the outside of the thigh and hip.
instructions: Lie on your back or sit on the floor with your legs straight. Cross one leg over the other, forming an "X" shape. Gently pull the knee across your body to stretch the outer hip and thigh, holding for a few seconds. Breathe steadily and focus on a mild stretch along the outside of your hip and leg.

name: Single-leg Lying Cross-over Stretch
description: The single-leg lying cross-over stretch is a beginner-friendly bodyweight stretch performed while lying on your back. One leg is lifted and gently rotated across the body to the opposite side, creating a twisting stretch through the lower back, hips, and glutes. It is commonly used in warm-ups or cooldowns to improve mobility and relieve tension in the posterior chain.
instructions: Lie on your back with arms at your sides. Lift one leg and gently rotate it across your body to the opposite side, keeping your knee straight. Hold the stretch for a few seconds, feeling the stretch in your lower back, hips, and glutes. Return to the starting position and repeat with the other leg.

name: Carioca Quick Step
description: The carioca quick step is a lateral, agility-focused bodyweight drill that improves hip mobility, coordination, and overall conditioning through quick crossover steps.
instructions: Start in an athletic stance with feet shoulder-width apart. Take a quick step to one side with one foot, then bring the other foot to meet it, crossing over with a lateral movement. Immediately repeat on the other side, alternating feet in a rapid, rhythmic pattern. Keep your knees slightly bent and weight light on your feet to maintain balance and agility.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Standing Hip Circle
description: The standing hip circle is a bodyweight mobility exercise that targets the hips and surrounding muscles by moving the leg in controlled circular motions. It is commonly used to limber up after sitting or as part of a warm-up before lower-body training or sports.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hands on hips. Lift one leg off the ground, keeping it straight, and move it in a large circle, first clockwise and then counterclockwise. Focus on controlled, smooth movements, targeting your hip joint and surrounding muscles. Repeat on the other leg to maintain balance and overall hip mobility.

name: Lateral Cone Hop
description: The lateral cone hop is a plyometric drill where you jump side to side over a low cone or marker, focusing on quick ground contact, explosive lateral power, and coordination. It is typically performed for time or high-rep sets to develop agility and lateral explosiveness for sport performance.
instructions: Stand with feet together beside a low cone or marker. Jump sideways over the cone, landing on both feet with knees bent to absorb impact. Immediately jump back to the starting side, focusing on quick ground contact and explosive power. Repeat for desired time or reps, alternating sides.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Side-To-Side Adductor Stretch
description: From a wide stance, shift your weight side to side, bending one knee while keeping the other leg straight to stretch the inner thigh of the straight leg. Move slowly and under control, pausing briefly at each side to feel the stretch in the adductors while keeping your chest up and core engaged.
instructions: Stand with a wide stance, feet facing forward. Shift your weight to one side, bending the knee while keeping the other leg straight, stretching the inner thigh. Move slowly and pause briefly, feeling the stretch in the adductors, then return to the starting position and repeat on the other side. Keep your chest up and core engaged throughout the movement.

name: Dynamic Pigeon Stretch
description: A dynamic mobility drill inspired by yoga pigeon pose, moving in and out of the stretch to open the hips and glutes while reducing stiffness in the lower body.
instructions: Start on your hands and knees. Bring one knee forward and place your foot on the ground in front of the other knee. Slowly lower your hips down towards the ground, stretching the back leg, then lift back up to the starting position, repeating the motion for several repetitions. Switch legs and repeat on the other side.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Cross-over Jack
description: The cross-over jack is a dynamic jumping jack variation where the feet cross over each other on each rep instead of simply coming together. It is performed in a continuous, rhythmic fashion to elevate heart rate, improve coordination, and enhance overall conditioning, making it a useful option for warm-ups or cardio-focused circuits.
instructions: Stand with feet together and hands by your sides. Jump your feet out to the sides while raising your arms above your head. Quickly cross one foot over the other, then switch and cross the other foot over, repeating in a continuous motion. Land softly on the balls of your feet and continue the rhythmic pattern.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Traveling Thigh Killa
description: The traveling thigh killa is a challenging lower-body bodyweight drill combining repeated hops forward and backward with bodyweight squats to build muscular endurance and conditioning. It is typically performed for time or high reps in circuits or as a lower-body finisher.
instructions: Start in a standing position with feet shoulder-width apart. Perform a bodyweight squat, then immediately hop forward a few feet. Quickly hop backward to the starting position and repeat, continuing to alternate squats with forward and backward hops. Keep your core engaged and landing softly on the balls of your feet to maintain control throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Foam Roll Adductor
description: Lie face down with one leg extended to the side and a foam roller placed under the inner thigh (adductor). Support your upper body on your forearms, then slowly roll the inner thigh from just above the knee toward the groin, pausing on tender spots and breathing steadily. Repeat on the other leg.
instructions: Lie face down with one leg extended to the side and a foam roller under the inner thigh. Support your upper body on your forearms. Slowly roll the inner thigh from above the knee toward the groin, pausing on tender spots. Repeat on the other leg.

name: MetaBurn Groiner
description: From a high plank, step one foot up outside the same-side hand, dropping the hips toward the floor to open the hips and groin, then return to plank and repeat on the other side. This dynamic movement targets the hip region while engaging the core and upper body for stability.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands under shoulders and toes on ground. Step one foot up outside the same-side hand, lowering hips toward floor to open hips and groin. Return to plank position and repeat on the other side, alternating legs with each repetition. Keep core and upper body engaged for stability throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Side-lying Leg Adduction Scissor
description: A side-lying core exercise where you lie on your side and perform scissoring leg adduction movements, keeping the core braced to challenge the abs and inner thighs.
instructions: Lie on your side with feet touching and hands under your forehead for support. Lift your legs off the ground, keeping them straight, and engage your core. Slowly lift your top leg up and over your bottom leg, then quickly switch legs in a scissoring motion, squeezing your inner thighs together. Repeat for desired repetitions, keeping your core braced throughout the exercise.

name: Groin and Back Stretch
description: A gentle floor-based stretch that opens the inner thighs while lengthening the lower back and hips. Sit or lie in a comfortable position, bring the soles of your feet together or widen your knees, and hinge or round forward slightly to feel a stretch through the groin and along the lower back. Hold the position while breathing slowly, avoiding any bouncing or sharp pain.
instructions: Sit on the floor with the soles of your feet together. Let your knees widen apart, feeling a gentle stretch in your groin. Hinge forward slightly, rounding your back to stretch your lower back and hips. Hold the position, breathing slowly and avoiding any sharp pain.

name: Adductor/Groin Stretch
description: A beginner-friendly stretch targeting the inner thigh muscles by gently opening the hips and lengthening the adductors while seated or standing.
instructions: Sit on floor with legs bent and feet together. Slowly let knees fall apart, stretching inner thighs. Keep back straight and hold stretch for 30 seconds. Breathe deeply and release slowly.

name: Incline Dumbbell Biceps Curl
description: The incline dumbbell biceps curl is performed lying face up on an incline bench, allowing the arms to hang down vertically. This position increases the stretch on the biceps and reduces shoulder involvement, helping to better isolate the biceps. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8-12 reps per set, in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Lie face up on an incline bench with arms hanging down. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward. Curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Banded Biceps Curl in Squat Position
description: The banded biceps curl in squat position targets the biceps while engaging the core and lower body to maintain a stable squat stance throughout the curling motion.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart in a squat position. Hold a resistance band with both hands at shoulder height, palms facing forward. Perform a biceps curl by bending elbows and keeping upper arms still, then return to starting position. Maintain a stable squat stance throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Band Standing Concentration Curl
description: The band standing concentration curl is a unilateral biceps exercise performed with a resistance band. Standing on the band and curling with one arm at a time allows you to isolate the biceps and emphasize a strong peak contraction through the top of the movement. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout.
instructions: Stand on the middle of the resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold one end of the band in each hand with palms facing forward. Curl one arm up towards your shoulder, keeping upper arm still, then lower it back down to the starting position. Alternate arms to complete the exercise.

name: Brachialis SMR
description: Brachialis self-myofascial release (SMR) is a soft-tissue technique for the upper arm, using a foam roller or similar tool to apply pressure to tight areas of the biceps and brachialis. It is typically performed slowly, pausing on tender spots to encourage the muscle to relax and reduce tension before or after workouts.
instructions: Lie on floor with foam roller under upper arm. Position roller on tight area of biceps or brachialis. Slowly roll back and forth, pausing on tender spots to release tension. Hold pressure for 20-30 seconds to allow muscle relaxation.

name: Wide-Grip Barbell Curl
description: The wide-grip barbell curl is a barbell curl variation performed with a grip wider than shoulder width to emphasize the short (inner) head of the biceps. Standing tall with the barbell held in an underhand grip, curl the bar up while keeping the elbows close to the torso, then lower under control. Commonly used for 8-12 reps per set to build biceps size and strength.
instructions: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the barbell with an underhand grip, wider than shoulder width. Curl the bar up, keeping elbows close to the torso, then lower it back down under control. Breathe naturally and repeat for the desired number of reps.

name: EZ-Bar Spider Curl
description: The EZ-bar spider curl is an isolation biceps exercise performed face down on an incline or preacher bench. Using an EZ bar places the wrists in a more comfortable, semi-supinated position while the prone angle minimizes shoulder involvement and keeps constant tension on the biceps. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8-12 or more per set, within upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Lie face down on an incline or preacher bench. Grasp the EZ-bar with a semi-supinated grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Curl the bar up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the bar back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Barbell Curl
description: The barbell curl is a classic strength exercise targeting the biceps, allowing heavier loading than many curl variations and typically performed for moderate to high reps (8–12) in arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, gripping the barbell with palms facing forward. Keep your back straight and core engaged, with a slight bend in your knees. Curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still, and then lower it back down to the starting position. Breathe naturally and avoid swinging the weight, focusing on slow and controlled movements.

name: Close-Grip EZ-Bar Curl
description: The close-grip EZ-bar curl is a biceps-focused strength exercise performed with a narrow grip on an EZ bar. Bringing the hands closer together emphasizes the long head of the biceps, which can contribute to building the outer portion and peak of the muscle. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8-12 reps per set, with controlled form and minimal body swing.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold an EZ-bar with a close grip, hands about 6-8 inches apart. Curl the bar up towards your shoulders, keeping upper arms still, then lower it back down to the starting position. Breathe naturally and maintain control throughout the movement.

name: Preacher Curl
description: The preacher curl is an isolation exercise for building bigger biceps by curling a barbell with the upper arms braced against a preacher bench pad to minimize cheating and maximize tension on the biceps.
instructions: Sit at a preacher curl bench with upper arms resting on the pad. Grasp a barbell with hands shoulder-width apart and palms facing forward. Curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement. Lower the barbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Barbell Curls Lying Against An Incline
description: Lie face down on an incline bench with your chest supported and feet firmly on the floor. Hold a barbell with an underhand grip, arms extended straight toward the floor. Keeping your upper arms fixed and elbows close to your sides, curl the barbell up toward your shoulders, squeezing the biceps at the top. Lower the barbell under control until your arms are fully extended and repeat for the desired reps.
instructions: Lie face down on an incline bench with your chest supported and feet on the floor. Hold a barbell with an underhand grip, arms extended straight down. Curl the barbell up toward your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still and squeezing your biceps. Lower the barbell back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Seated Close-Grip Concentration Barbell Curl
description: Sit on a bench with your feet flat and hold a barbell with a close underhand grip. Lean slightly forward, keeping your upper arms close to your torso, and curl the barbell up toward your shoulders while squeezing your biceps. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the bar under control to full extension without swinging your body.
instructions: Sit on a bench with feet flat and hold a barbell with a close underhand grip. Lean forward slightly, keeping upper arms close to your torso. Curl the barbell up toward your shoulders, squeezing your biceps, and pause briefly at the top. Lower the barbell under control to full extension, avoiding body swing.

name: Close-Grip Barbell Curl
description: The close-grip barbell curl is a biceps-focused curl variation performed with the hands placed closer than shoulder width on a barbell. This narrower grip emphasizes the outer portion of the biceps and can help develop biceps peak. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8–12 reps per set.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a barbell with hands closer than shoulder-width apart and palms facing forward. Curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the barbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Drag Curl
description: The drag curl is a biceps-focused barbell curl variation where the bar is kept close to the body and "dragged" up the torso, minimizing front deltoid involvement and emphasizing elbow flexion. It is typically performed for 8-15 reps per set in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with palms facing forward. Keep the bar close to your body, with your elbows at your sides. Drag the bar up your torso, keeping it close to your body, and curl up to a comfortable height. Lower the bar back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Reverse-grip Barbell Curl
description: The reverse-grip barbell curl is a curl variation performed with an overhand (pronated) grip, shifting emphasis from the biceps to the forearms and brachialis. It is typically done standing, curling the bar from arms-extended to elbows-flexed while keeping the elbows close to the torso and wrists neutral. An EZ bar can be used if a straight bar is uncomfortable on the wrists.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an overhand grip. Keep your elbows close to your torso and wrists neutral. Curl the bar up by bending your elbows, avoiding swinging or jerking motions. Lower the bar back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Lying High Bench Barbell Curl
description: Lie face up on a high flat bench with your upper arms hanging straight down and a barbell held in an underhand grip. Keeping your upper arms perpendicular to the floor and elbows fixed, curl the barbell up toward your shoulders, squeezing the biceps at the top, then lower under control to full extension.
instructions: Lie face up on a high flat bench with a barbell in an underhand grip. Keep your upper arms still and perpendicular to the floor. Curl the barbell up toward your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the barbell back down to the starting position under control.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Seated Wide-Grip Curl to Close-Grip Curl
description: A seated barbell curl variation where you alternate between wide-grip and close-grip hand positions to emphasize different areas of the biceps while maintaining strict form.
instructions: Sit on a bench with back support, holding a barbell with a wide grip. Curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still, then lower it back down. Switch to a close grip and repeat the curl, focusing on squeezing your biceps. Alternate between wide and close grip curls, maintaining strict form throughout the exercise.

name: Seated Straight-Bar Curl Superset
description: A seated barbell curl variation performed with alternating wide-grip and close-grip positions. While seated, curl the bar with a wider grip for a more traditional biceps emphasis, then switch to a closer grip to change the angle of pull and recruit different fibers of the biceps. Alternate grips in a superset fashion to increase time under tension and arm pump.
instructions: Sit on a bench with feet on floor. Hold a straight-bar with a wide grip, curl the bar up towards shoulders, squeezing biceps. Switch to a close grip and repeat, alternating between wide and close grips in a superset fashion. Keep core engaged and control the return to starting position.

name: Dave Draper Forehead Curl
description: An intermediate barbell curl variation where you bring the bar toward your forehead to emphasize the biceps through a shortened range of motion and increased peak contraction.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with palms facing forward. Curl the barbell up toward your forehead, keeping upper arms still and only moving your forearms. Focus on squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement, holding for a brief moment. Lower the barbell back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Reverse-grip EZ-bar Curl
description: The reverse-grip EZ-bar curl targets the biceps and forearms using a pronated (palms-down) grip on an EZ bar. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions to build arm strength and muscular endurance, often as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout.
instructions: Hold an EZ-bar with a pronated grip, palms facing down. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, engage your core, and keep your back straight. Curl the bar up towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still, and then lower it back down to the starting position. Breathe naturally and avoid swinging the bar.

name: Standing Band Biceps Curl
description: The standing band biceps curl is an arm-focused exercise that targets the biceps using elastic resistance. Performed standing, you curl the resistance against the band’s tension, typically for moderate to high repetitions as part of an arms-focused or upper-body workout.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a resistance band with both hands at shoulder height. Keep your upper arms still, then bend your elbows to curl the band towards your shoulders. Keep control throughout the movement, avoiding swinging or jerking the band. Lower the band back to the starting position, stretching your biceps and repeating the curl.

name: Seated Barbell Curl
description: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the floor, holding a barbell with an underhand grip and arms fully extended. Keeping your upper arms still and elbows close to your sides, curl the barbell up toward your shoulders by contracting your biceps, then lower it under control to the starting position.
instructions: Sit on a bench with back straight and feet flat on floor. Hold a barbell with underhand grip and arms extended. Curl the barbell up toward shoulders by bending elbows, keeping upper arms still. Lower the barbell under control to the starting position.

name: AM Standing Barbell Curl
description: The standing barbell curl is a popular exercise targeting the biceps. It is typically performed standing with a shoulder-width grip, curling the bar up toward the shoulders and lowering under control for moderate to high repetitions in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, gripping the barbell with an underhand grip. Keep your back straight and core engaged, curling the bar up toward your shoulders. Lower the bar back down to the starting position in a controlled motion, avoiding swinging or jerking. Breathe naturally and maintain proper form throughout the exercise.

name: EZ-bar preacher curl
description: The EZ-bar preacher curl is a classic isolation exercise for building bigger biceps. With the back of the upper arms braced against the preacher pad, momentum is minimized, forcing the biceps to work harder through the full range of motion.
instructions: Sit at the preacher curl bench with the back of your upper arms resting on the pad. Grasp the EZ-bar with your hands shoulder-width apart and palms facing forward. Curl the bar up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement. Lower the bar back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the entire range.

name: 30 Arms Standing Barbell Curl
description: Stand tall holding a barbell with an underhand grip, hands about shoulder-width apart. Keeping your elbows close to your sides and upper arms stationary, curl the bar up toward your shoulders by contracting your biceps. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the bar under control to the starting position. Avoid swinging your torso or using momentum.
instructions: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a barbell with an underhand grip. Keep your elbows close to your sides and upper arms still, curling the bar up toward your shoulders. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the bar under control to the starting position. Avoid swinging or using momentum, focusing on slow and controlled movements.

name: 30° Arms Incline Biceps Curl
description: Sit on a 30-degree incline bench holding a barbell with an underhand grip, arms fully extended and hanging straight down. Keeping your upper arms fixed and elbows close to your sides, curl the barbell up toward your shoulders by contracting your biceps. Squeeze at the top, then slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position under control. Maintain a neutral spine against the bench and avoid swinging or using momentum.
instructions: Sit on a 30-degree incline bench with a barbell and underhand grip. Keep your upper arms still and elbows close to your body. Curl the barbell up toward your shoulders by bending your elbows, squeezing at the top. Lower the barbell back down slowly, controlling the movement and keeping your spine neutral against the bench.

name: Incline Hammer Curls
description: Sit on an incline bench with your back supported and a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging straight down with neutral (hammer) grip. Keeping your upper arms still and elbows close to your sides, curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, squeezing the biceps at the top. Lower the weights under control to the starting position and repeat.
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with back support and hold a dumbbell in each hand. Keep your upper arms still and elbows close to your sides. Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the weights under control to the starting position.

name: Hammer Curls
description: A dumbbell curl variation performed with a neutral grip, keeping the palms facing each other throughout the movement to emphasize the biceps and forearms.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold dumbbells with palms facing each other and arms at sides. Curl dumbbells up towards shoulders, keeping upper arms still and only moving forearms. Lower dumbbells back to starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Biceps Curl to Shoulder Press
description: A compound dumbbell movement combining a biceps curl with an overhead shoulder press to efficiently train the arms and shoulders in one sequence.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells with palms facing forward. Curl the dumbbells up to shoulder height, keeping upper arms still and only bending elbows. From the curled position, press the dumbbells straight up over your head. Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Concentration Curl
description: The concentration curl is a single-arm biceps exercise typically performed seated, focusing on isolating the biceps and emphasizing the peak. It is commonly used as a finishing movement in a biceps workout and usually done for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Sit on a bench or chair with your arm resting on your thigh. Hold a dumbbell with your palm facing forward. Curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm still and only moving your forearm. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Flexor Incline Dumbbell Curls
description: Sit back on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging straight down and palms facing forward. Keeping your upper arms still, curl the dumbbells up by flexing at the elbows until they reach shoulder level, squeezing the biceps at the top. Lower the weights under control to the starting position and repeat for the desired reps.
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms straight and palms facing forward. Curl the dumbbells up by bending your elbows until they reach shoulder level. Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement. Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position under control.

name: Dumbbell Bicep Curl
description: A single-joint dumbbell exercise performed seated or standing to build size and strength in the biceps, typically done for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward. Curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping upper arms still, then lower them back to the starting position. Breathe naturally and avoid swinging the dumbbells.

name: Cross-body Hammer Curl
description: The cross-body hammer curl is a dumbbell curling variation performed with a neutral grip, bringing the dumbbell across the torso toward the opposite shoulder. This position emphasizes the brachialis and forearms while still heavily involving the biceps. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8–12 or more, as part of an arm-focused workout.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing towards body. Curl the dumbbell across your torso, bringing it towards the opposite shoulder, keeping upper arms still. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, repeating with the other arm.

name: Dumbbell Alternate Bicep Curl
description: The dumbbell alternate bicep curl is a single-joint arm exercise performed one arm at a time to build size and strength in the biceps. It can be done seated or standing and is typically used with moderate to high repetitions as part of an arm-focused workout.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells at sides with palms facing forward. Curl one dumbbell up towards shoulder, keeping upper arm still and only moving forearm. Lower the dumbbell back to starting position and repeat with the other arm. Alternate arms with each rep, keeping core engaged and maintaining good posture.

name: Single-arm Dumbbell Preacher Curl
description: The single-arm dumbbell preacher curl is an isolation exercise that targets the biceps by eliminating momentum and emphasizing the peak contraction through a supported arm position. It is typically performed with lighter weight for moderate to high repetitions in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Sit at a preacher curl bench with arm support. Hold a dumbbell in one hand with palm facing forward. Curl the dumbbell upwards, keeping upper arm still, and squeeze biceps at the top. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Alternate Incline Dumbbell Curl
description: The alternating incline dumbbell curl is performed lying face up on an incline bench, allowing the arms to hang vertically to better isolate the biceps while minimizing shoulder involvement. Curl one dumbbell at a time through a full range of motion, focusing on control and squeeze at the top, typically for moderate to high reps per arm.
instructions: Lie face up on an incline bench with arms hanging straight down. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward. Curl one dumbbell up towards your shoulder, squeezing your bicep at the top, then lower it back down and repeat with the other arm. Alternate arms for the desired number of reps, focusing on control and proper form.

name: Standing Concentration Curl
description: The standing concentration curl is a dumbbell biceps isolation exercise performed while standing and bracing the working arm against the inner thigh. It emphasizes strict form and a controlled tempo to target the biceps peak, often used as an alternative to preacher curls when a preacher bench is not available. Typically done for moderate to high repetitions, it helps build size and definition in the biceps.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand. Brace the working arm against the inner thigh, with upper arm still and only forearm moving. Curl the dumbbell up towards the shoulder, squeezing the biceps at the top. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Alternating Incline Dumbbell Biceps Curl
description: The alternating incline dumbbell biceps curl is performed lying face up on an incline bench, allowing the arms to hang down vertically. This position increases the stretch on the biceps and reduces shoulder involvement, helping to better isolate the biceps. Alternate curling one dumbbell at a time for controlled reps, typically in the moderate to high rep range.
instructions: Lie face up on an incline bench with arms hanging down. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward. Curl one dumbbell up towards your shoulder, squeezing your bicep, then lower it back down and repeat with the other arm. Alternate arms for the desired number of reps.

name: Alternate Hammer Curl
description: The alternating hammer curl is a dumbbell curl variation performed with a neutral wrist position throughout the movement, emphasizing the biceps and forearms by avoiding wrist supination and curling one arm at a time.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold dumbbells with palms facing towards body, keeping wrists neutral. Curl one dumbbell up towards shoulder, keeping upper arm still, then lower it back down. Alternate with the other arm, curling and lowering in a slow and controlled motion.

name: Seated Dumbbell Biceps Curl
description: The seated dumbbell biceps curl is an isolation exercise targeting the biceps. Sitting helps reduce body momentum and encourages stricter form and a stronger mind-muscle connection. Typically performed for moderate to high reps, such as 8-12 per set.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the floor. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward. Curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement. Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the entire range.

name: Preacher Hammer Dumbbell Curl
description: Sit at a preacher bench holding a dumbbell in a neutral (hammer) grip with your upper arm supported on the pad. Keeping your elbow fixed on the pad, curl the dumbbell up toward your shoulder, squeezing the biceps at the top. Lower the weight under control until your arm is almost fully extended, then repeat.
instructions: Sit at a preacher bench with upper arm on the pad. Hold a dumbbell in a neutral grip with palm facing towards body. Curl the dumbbell up towards shoulder, squeezing biceps at the top. Lower the weight under control to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Preacher Curl
description: The dumbbell preacher curl is an isolation exercise that targets the biceps, emphasizing the peak by eliminating momentum and shoulder involvement. Performed seated with the upper arm braced on a preacher bench, it is typically done for moderate to high repetitions in arm or upper-body workouts.
instructions: Sit on a preacher bench with upper arms braced on the pad. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward. Curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Lying Supine Dumbbell Curl
description: Lie on your back on a flat surface holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms extended toward the ceiling, palms facing forward. Keeping your upper arms stable and elbows close to your sides, curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders by flexing at the elbows. Squeeze the biceps at the top, then slowly lower the weights back to the starting position with control.
instructions: Lie on your back with a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended and palms facing forward. Keep your upper arms stable and close to your body. Curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders by bending your elbows, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control.

name: Standing Dumbbell Reverse Curl
description: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with your arms at your sides and palms facing down in a reverse (overhand) grip. Keeping your elbows close to your torso, curl the dumbbells up by bending at the elbows without swinging your body. Squeeze at the top, then slowly lower the weights back to the starting position under control. Can also be performed seated to reduce body momentum.
instructions: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing down. Keep your elbows close to your torso and curl the dumbbells up by bending your elbows. Squeeze at the top, then slowly lower the weights back down to the starting position. Keep your body still and controlled throughout the movement.

name: Palms-Out Incline Biceps Curl
description: The palms-out incline biceps curl is a dumbbell curl variation performed on an incline bench with the palms facing forward throughout the movement. This position emphasizes the long head of the biceps, contributing to the biceps peak, and is typically done for moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout.
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad. Hold dumbbells with your palms facing forward and arms extended. Curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still, then lower them back down to the starting position. Breathe naturally and focus on squeezing your biceps at the top of the curl.

name: Straight-arm Plank with Biceps Curl
description: The straight-arm plank with biceps curl is a hybrid movement that combines a high-tension plank position with a unilateral dumbbell curl. While maintaining a rigid, straight-arm plank, you curl the dumbbell with one arm at a time, forcing the core to resist rotation and the biceps to work under instability. This makes it useful for time-efficient workouts that target the biceps and core while adding a mild cardiovascular challenge.
instructions: Start in a straight-arm plank position with hands under shoulders and toes on ground. Hold a dumbbell in one hand with arm extended. Curl the dumbbell up towards shoulder, keeping upper arm still, then lower it back down. Alternate arms with each rep, maintaining a stable plank position throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Face-down Incline Dumbbell Biceps Curl
description: The face-down incline dumbbell biceps curl is performed lying chest-down on an incline bench while holding dumbbells with arms hanging straight toward the floor. From this position, you curl the weights up by flexing the elbows, keeping the upper arms vertical and close to perpendicular to the floor. This angle reduces shoulder involvement and emphasizes the biceps throughout the range of motion. It is typically used with moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout.
instructions: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with arms hanging straight and holding dumbbells. Keep upper arms vertical and close to the bench. Curl the dumbbells up by flexing the elbows, squeezing the biceps at the top of the movement. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Seated Dumbbell Inner Biceps Curl
description: Sit upright on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging at your sides with palms facing outward to emphasize the inner portion of the biceps. Keeping your upper arms close to your torso and your back straight, curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders while squeezing the biceps at the top. Lower the weights under control to the starting position and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing outward. Keep your upper arms close to your torso and back straight. Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the weights under control to the starting position.

name: Standing Inner-Biceps Curl
description: A dumbbell curl variation performed standing with a supinated grip and elbows kept close to the torso to emphasize the inner portion of the biceps.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells with palms facing forward. Keep elbows close to your torso and shoulders relaxed. Curl the dumbbells upwards, squeezing your biceps, then slowly lower them back to the starting position. Keep control throughout the movement to target the inner biceps.

name: Seated Hammer Curl
description: The seated hammer curl is a dumbbell curling variation performed while seated, emphasizing the biceps and forearms with a neutral (hammer) grip. Sitting helps reduce body swing and encourages stricter form and a stronger mind-muscle connection. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8–12 reps per set or more.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet flat on the floor. Hold dumbbells with a neutral grip, palms facing each other. Curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still, and then lower them back down to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl to Upright Row
description: A compound dumbbell exercise that combines a biceps curl with an upright row to train the biceps, shoulders, and traps in a time-efficient sequence. Perform a controlled curl to shoulder height, then transition into an upright row by lifting the dumbbells along the torso toward chest level, keeping elbows high and core braced.
instructions: Hold dumbbells with palms facing forward. Perform a controlled biceps curl to shoulder height, keeping upper arms still. Transition into an upright row by lifting dumbbells along torso toward chest level, keeping elbows high and core braced. Lower dumbbells back to starting position in a slow and controlled manner.

name: Dumbbell Overhead Hold with Single-Arm Curl
description: Hold one dumbbell locked out overhead while performing a biceps curl with the opposite arm. This combines an isometric shoulder hold with a dynamic curl, challenging the biceps, shoulders, and core stability even with relatively light weight.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold one dumbbell overhead with arm locked, keeping core engaged. With the opposite arm, hold a dumbbell and perform a biceps curl, keeping upper arm still. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position and repeat with the other arm.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Kneeling Concentration Curl
description: The kneeling concentration curl isolates the biceps by limiting body momentum, helping you focus on strict form and controlled lifting with a dumbbell.
instructions: Kneel on a mat with one knee bent and the other foot in front, keeping your back straight. Hold a dumbbell with your arm resting against your inner thigh, palm facing forward. Curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm still and only moving your forearm. Lower the dumbbell back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Lunge Hold Dumbbell Curl
description: An isometric lunge hold combined with a dumbbell curl to train the biceps while challenging leg strength and core stability.
instructions: Stand with feet together, holding dumbbells at sides. Take a large step forward with one foot, lowering into a lunge position with back knee almost touching ground. Hold the lunge position while curling dumbbells up towards shoulders, keeping upper arms still. Lower dumbbells back to starting position and repeat with opposite leg.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Alternating Dumbbell Snatch
description: The alternating dumbbell snatch is an explosive full-body power exercise that primarily targets the hips, back, and shoulders. By switching arms each rep, it allows brief recovery for each side, enabling more total quality work. It can be performed for low reps to emphasize power and speed, or for higher reps to build muscle and conditioning as part of a metabolic circuit.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand. Explosively lift the dumbbell from the ground to overhead in one swift motion, switching arms with each rep. Keep your core engaged and back straight throughout the movement. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position and immediately repeat with the opposite arm.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Reverse-Grip Dumbbell Curl
description: A curl variation performed with palms facing down to emphasize the forearms and grip while still targeting the biceps. Can be done standing or seated, typically for moderate to high reps.
instructions: Hold dumbbells with palms facing down. Stand or sit with back straight, engaging core for stability. Curl dumbbells up towards shoulders, keeping upper arms still, then lower them back down to starting position. Focus on slow and controlled movements to target biceps and forearms.

name: Hammer Curl
description: The hammer curl is a dumbbell exercise performed with a neutral grip to target the biceps and forearms, especially the brachialis. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8-12 or more, as part of an arm-focused workout.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold dumbbells with a neutral grip, palms facing each other. Curl dumbbells up towards shoulders, keeping upper arms still, then lower them back down to the starting position. Keep control throughout the movement to target the biceps and forearms.

name: Single-arm Seated Dumbbell Curl
description: The single-arm seated dumbbell curl is an isolation exercise for building the biceps. Performed one arm at a time while seated, it allows you to focus on the working arm, potentially use slightly heavier weight, and emphasize the mind-muscle connection. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8-12 reps per set or more.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and one arm resting on your thigh. Hold a dumbbell in the working hand with palm facing forward. Curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder, squeezing your bicep at the top. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Dumbbell Biceps Curl
description: The dumbbell biceps curl is a single-joint arm exercise performed seated or standing to build size and strength in the biceps, typically done for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward. Curl the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping upper arms still, then lower them back down to the starting position. Keep your core engaged and back straight throughout the exercise.

name: Seated Biceps Hammer Curl
description: Sit upright on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging at your sides with neutral (hammer) grip and feet flat on the floor. Keeping your elbows close to your torso and upper arms stationary, curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders while maintaining the neutral grip. Squeeze the biceps at the top, then slowly lower the weights back to the starting position under control.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, holding them with a neutral grip. Keep your elbows close to your torso and upper arms still, then curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders. Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement. Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position slowly and under control.

name: Seated Alternating Biceps Curl
description: The seated alternating biceps curl is a dumbbell exercise performed while seated, curling one arm at a time. Alternating sides allows brief rest between reps, helping build bigger and stronger biceps with controlled form. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8-12 or more, in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your back straight and feet on the floor. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward. Curl one arm up towards your shoulder, squeezing your bicep, then lower it back down and repeat with the other arm. Alternate arms with each rep, controlling the movement and keeping your upper arms still.

name: Alternating Hammer Curl
description: The alternating hammer curl is a dumbbell curl variation performed with a neutral wrist position throughout the movement, mimicking the grip of a hammer. One arm curls at a time while the other remains at the side, emphasizing the biceps and forearms. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8-12 or more, in the arm-focused portion of a workout.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing towards your body. Curl one arm up towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm still and your wrist neutral, then lower it back down and repeat with the other arm. Alternate arms with each rep, focusing on squeezing your biceps at the top of the curl.

name: Alternating Biceps Curl
description: The alternating biceps curl is a single-joint dumbbell exercise performed one arm at a time to build size and strength in the biceps. It can be done seated or standing and is typically used with moderate to high repetitions as part of an arm-focused workout.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand. Curl one dumbbell up towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm still and only moving your forearm. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position and repeat with the other arm. Alternate arms with each repetition, keeping control throughout the movement.

name: Holman Slow Biceps Curl
description: A controlled dumbbell curl variation emphasizing slow tempo to increase time under tension in the biceps. Performed standing with strict form, focusing on a smooth, deliberate lifting and lowering phase without swinging the weights.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells with palms facing forward. Slowly lift the dumbbells up towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still and only moving your forearms. Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position with the same slow and controlled tempo. Keep your core engaged and avoid swinging the weights throughout the exercise.

name: Holman Biceps Curl to Chest Punch
description: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing forward. Curl the weights up toward your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your torso. At the top of the curl, rotate your palms to face each other and punch one dumbbell forward at chest height, extending the arm without locking the elbow. Retract the punch, rotate back, and lower the weights under control. Alternate punching arms each rep while maintaining a stable core and upright posture.
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at your sides and palms facing forward. Curl the weights up toward your shoulders, keeping elbows close to your torso. At the top, rotate palms to face each other and punch one dumbbell forward at chest height. Lower the weights under control and alternate punching arms with each rep.

name: Holman Right Arm Heavy Biceps Curl
description: A unilateral heavy dumbbell curl focusing on the right arm to build biceps strength and size. Performed standing with strict form to minimize momentum and maximize biceps engagement.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a heavy dumbbell in your right hand. Keep your upper arm still and close to your body, with your palm facing forward. Curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid swinging or jerking.

name: Holman Left Arm Heavy Biceps Curl
description: A unilateral heavy dumbbell curl focusing on the left arm to build biceps strength and size. Performed standing with strict form to minimize momentum and isolate the biceps.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a heavy dumbbell in your left hand. Keep your upper arm still and close to your body, with your palm facing forward. Curl the dumbbell up towards your left shoulder, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid momentum.

name: Holman Alternating Front Lunge Biceps Curl
description: From a standing position holding dumbbells at your sides, step forward into a lunge while simultaneously curling the dumbbells up toward your shoulders. Push back to the starting position and lower the weights, then repeat on the opposite leg, alternating sides each rep while maintaining an upright torso and controlled movement.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding dumbbells at your sides. Step forward into a lunge with one foot, curling the dumbbells up toward your shoulders as you lower into the lunge. Push back to the starting position, lowering the dumbbells, and repeat on the opposite leg. Maintain an upright torso and controlled movement throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Weighted Double-Time Biceps Curl
description: A dumbbell curl variation where you alternate between controlled and faster tempo reps to increase time under tension and metabolic stress in the biceps.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells with palms facing forward. Alternate arms to curl the dumbbells up to shoulder height, starting with a controlled tempo rep. Immediately follow with a faster tempo rep on the same arm, then switch to the other arm and repeat the sequence. Keep core engaged and focus on proper form throughout the exercise.

name: Holman Heavy Right Arm Biceps Curl
description: A unilateral heavy dumbbell curl focusing on the right arm to build biceps strength and size. Performed standing with strict form to minimize momentum and isolate the biceps.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a heavy dumbbell in your right hand. Keep your upper arm still and close to your body, with your palm facing forward. Curl the dumbbell up towards your right shoulder, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid swinging or jerking.

name: Holman Heavy Left Arm Biceps Curl
description: A unilateral heavy dumbbell curl focusing on the left arm to build biceps strength and size. Performed standing with strict form to minimize momentum and emphasize elbow flexion.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a heavy dumbbell in your left hand. Keep your upper arm still and curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder, focusing on elbow flexion. Avoid swinging or using momentum, and squeeze your biceps at the top of the curl. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position in a controlled manner.

name: MetaBurn Dumbbell Drag Curl
description: A biceps-focused curl variation where the dumbbells are dragged close to the torso as the elbows move slightly back, increasing tension on the biceps through the range of motion.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells with palms facing forward. Curl the dumbbells up, keeping them close to your torso as your elbows move slightly back. Focus on squeezing your biceps as you lift, then slowly lower the dumbbells to the starting position. Keep your core engaged and back straight throughout the exercise.

name: MetaBurn Hammer Curl to Overhead Press
description: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with a neutral (hammer) grip. Curl the weights up toward your shoulders, keeping your elbows close to your body. At the top of the curl, rotate or maintain the neutral grip and press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are fully extended without locking out. Lower the weights back to shoulder level, then reverse the curl to return to the starting position. Maintain a braced core and avoid using momentum throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with a neutral grip. Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, keeping elbows close to your body. Press the dumbbells overhead with arms fully extended, then lower back to shoulder level and reverse the curl to the starting position. Maintain a braced core throughout the movement.

name: MetaBurn Biceps Curl To Lateral Raise
description: Stand holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing forward. Curl the weights up toward your shoulders, keeping elbows close to your torso and avoiding swinging. Lower the dumbbells back to your sides with control, then rotate your palms to face inward and raise the dumbbells out to the sides until your arms are about parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in the elbows. Lower back down slowly and repeat, maintaining a stable core and neutral spine throughout.
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at your sides and palms facing forward. Curl the weights up toward your shoulders, keeping elbows close to your torso. Lower the dumbbells back down, then rotate your palms inward and raise the dumbbells out to the sides until arms are parallel to the floor. Lower back down slowly, maintaining a stable core and neutral spine throughout.

name: 30 Arms Standing Dumbbell Hammer Curl 21s
description: Stand tall holding a pair of dumbbells at your sides with a neutral (hammer) grip. Keeping your elbows close to your torso, perform the 21s protocol: first complete 7 partial reps from the bottom position up to about halfway, then 7 partial reps from halfway up to the top, and finally 7 full-range hammer curls from bottom to top. Control the weight on the way down, avoid swinging your torso, and keep your core braced throughout.
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells at your sides, using a neutral grip. Perform 7 partial curls from bottom to halfway, then 7 from halfway to top. Next, complete 7 full-range hammer curls from bottom to top, controlling the weight on the way down. Keep your core braced and avoid swinging your torso throughout the exercise.

name: 30 Arms Standing Biceps Curl
description: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms extended at your sides and palms facing forward. Keeping your elbows close to your torso and upper arms stationary, curl both dumbbells up toward your shoulders by contracting your biceps. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the weights under control to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining a stable torso and avoiding swinging.
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells at your sides and palms facing forward. Keep your elbows close to your torso and curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the weights under control to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, keeping your torso stable and avoiding swinging.

name: 30 Arms Standing Alternating Hammer Curl
description: Stand tall holding dumbbells at your sides with a neutral grip (palms facing in). Keeping your elbows close to your torso, curl one dumbbell up toward your shoulder without rotating your wrist, squeezing the biceps and forearms at the top. Lower under control, then alternate to the other arm. Maintain a stable torso and avoid swinging the weights.
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells at your sides and a neutral grip. Keep your elbows close to your torso and curl one dumbbell up toward your shoulder without rotating your wrist. Lower the dumbbell under control, then alternate to the other arm. Maintain a stable torso throughout the exercise.

name: 30 Arms Standing Alternating Biceps Curl
description: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing in. Brace your core and keep your elbows close to your torso. Curl one dumbbell up by rotating your palm to face your shoulder as you bend the elbow, squeezing the biceps at the top without swinging your body. Lower the weight under control, then alternate to the other arm. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of reps while maintaining a stable torso and controlled tempo.
instructions: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing in, and core braced. Curl one dumbbell up by bending your elbow and rotating your palm to face your shoulder, squeezing your biceps. Lower the weight under control and alternate to the other arm. Continue alternating sides, maintaining a stable torso and controlled tempo.

name: Single-Arm Hammer Preacher Curl
description: An isolation dumbbell curl performed with a neutral (hammer) grip on a preacher bench to target the biceps and forearms one arm at a time.
instructions: Sit at a preacher bench with arm resting on pad. Hold a dumbbell with a neutral grip, palm facing towards body. Curl the dumbbell up towards shoulder, keeping upper arm still, then lower it back down to starting position. Repeat with the other arm to complete the exercise.

name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Preacher Curl
description: An isolation biceps exercise performed one arm at a time while the upper arm is supported on a preacher bench or inclined surface, emphasizing the peak contraction and strict form.
instructions: Sit at a preacher bench with your arm resting on the pad. Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your palm facing forward. Curl the dumbbell up towards your shoulder, keeping your upper arm still, then slowly lower it back down to the starting position. Switch arms to complete the exercise on both sides.

name: Seated Dumbbell Concentration Curl
description: Sit on a bench with your feet flat and knees apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand. Hinge forward slightly and rest the back of your upper arm against the inside of your thigh. Keeping your upper arm fixed, curl the dumbbell toward your shoulder, squeezing the biceps at the top, then lower under control to full extension. Repeat for reps, then switch arms.
instructions: Sit on a bench with feet flat and knees apart, holding a dumbbell in one hand. Rest the back of your upper arm against the inside of your thigh, hinging forward slightly. Curl the dumbbell toward your shoulder, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the dumbbell under control to full extension, then repeat for reps and switch arms.

name: Incline Hammer Curl
description: Sit back on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging straight down and palms facing each other. Keeping your upper arms still and elbows close to your sides, curl the dumbbells up in a hammer grip until they reach shoulder level, then lower under control to the starting position.

name: Incline Dumbbell Triceps Extension
description: Lie back on an incline bench holding dumbbells with arms extended above your shoulders, then bend at the elbows to lower the weights behind your head and extend back up to target the triceps.
instructions: Lie back on an incline bench with dumbbells in hand. Hold the dumbbells with arms extended above your shoulders. Lower the dumbbells behind your head by bending at the elbows, then extend back up to the starting position, squeezing your triceps. Control the movement throughout to avoid injury.

name: 30° Arms Hammer Curl
description: Perform a hammer curl while seated on a bench set to roughly a 30-degree incline. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with a neutral grip (palms facing each other), arms hanging straight down. Keeping your elbows close to your sides and upper arms stable, curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, squeezing the biceps and forearms at the top. Lower the weights under control to the starting position and repeat for the desired reps.
instructions: Sit on a 30-degree incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Keep your elbows close to your sides and upper arms stable. Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders, squeezing your biceps and forearms at the top. Lower the weights under control to the starting position.

name: Reverse Plate Curls
description: Hold a weight plate with both hands at the 3 and 9 o’clock positions using an overhand (palms-down) grip. Stand tall with your arms extended down in front of your thighs. Keeping your elbows close to your sides and wrists straight, curl the plate upward by flexing your elbows until your forearms are roughly vertical. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the plate under control to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions, focusing on controlled movement and forearm engagement.
instructions: Hold a weight plate with both hands at the 3 and 9 o'clock positions using an overhand grip. Stand tall with arms extended down in front of thighs. Curl the plate upward by flexing elbows, keeping them close to sides, until forearms are vertical. Lower the plate under control to the starting position.

name: Standing Biceps Stretch
description: Stand tall with your arm extended behind you at shoulder height, palm facing up or out, and gently rotate your arm and torso until you feel a stretch along the front of your upper arm and shoulder. Hold without bouncing and breathe steadily, then repeat on the other side.
instructions: Stand tall with your arm extended behind you at shoulder height. Palm should face up or out, with your arm in a straight line. Gently rotate your arm and torso until you feel a stretch in your upper arm and shoulder. Hold the stretch without bouncing, breathing steadily, then repeat on the other side.

name: Suspended Curl
description: A bodyweight biceps curl performed with the hands holding suspended straps or rings, where you lean back and pull your body toward the anchor point by flexing the elbows.
instructions: Hold suspended straps or rings with hands shoulder-width apart. Lean back slightly, keeping arms straight, with feet flat on the ground. Bend elbows to pull your body toward the anchor point, curling hands up toward shoulders. Slowly return to the starting position, straightening arms and controlling the movement.

name: Plate Hammer Curl
description: The plate hammer curl is a biceps-focused strength exercise performed by holding a weight plate with a neutral grip and curling it toward the shoulders. This variation emphasizes the long head of the biceps as well as the brachialis and forearms, similar to a traditional hammer curl.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a weight plate with a neutral grip, palms facing each other. Curl the plate toward your shoulders, keeping upper arms still, and squeeze your biceps at the top. Lower the plate back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Barbell Spider Curl
description: The barbell spider curl is an isolation biceps exercise performed face down on an incline or preacher bench, allowing the arms to hang straight down while curling the barbell. The vertical torso position minimizes shoulder involvement and emphasizes strict elbow flexion to target the biceps through a full range of motion, typically done for moderate to high reps in arm or upper-body workouts.
instructions: Lie face down on an incline or preacher bench with arms hanging straight down. Grasp a barbell with hands shoulder-width apart and palms facing forward. Curl the barbell up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement. Lower the barbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the entire range of motion.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Barbell Forehead Curl
description: The barbell forehead curl is a classic biceps isolation exercise where the bar is curled up toward the forehead instead of the chest, increasing emphasis on the squeeze and peak contraction. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8-12 reps per set.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, gripping the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Curl the bar up toward your forehead, keeping upper arms still and only moving your forearms. Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement, holding for a brief moment. Lower the bar back down to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid swinging.

name: Overhead Cable Curl
description: Also known as a crucifix curl, the overhead cable curl is a biceps-focused exercise performed between two cable stacks using a pair of D-handles. With the arms extended out to the sides and elbows held at shoulder height, both hands curl in toward the ears. This position minimizes shoulder involvement and momentum while emphasizing the biceps peak. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8-12 reps per set, as part of arm-focused training.
instructions: Stand between two cable stacks with D-handles at shoulder height. Hold a handle in each hand with arms extended out to the sides and palms facing forward. Curl hands in toward your ears, keeping elbows still and upper arms at shoulder height. Lower hands back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Standing One-Arm Cable Curl
description: The standing one-arm cable curl is a single-joint isolation exercise targeting the biceps. Performed with strict, controlled form, it minimizes momentum to maximize tension on the biceps throughout the entire range of motion.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the cable at shoulder height. Hold the cable handle with one hand and keep your upper arm still. Curl the handle towards your shoulder, keeping your elbow close to your body, then slowly lower it back to the starting position. Switch arms to work the other biceps.

name: Reverse Cable Curl
description: Stand facing a cable machine with a straight bar attachment set to the low pulley. Grasp the bar with an overhand (pronated) grip, hands about shoulder-width apart. Keeping your elbows close to your sides and upper arms stationary, curl the bar up by flexing at the elbows until your forearms are vertical. Squeeze at the top, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with control.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a straight bar attachment at the low pulley. Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Curl the bar up by bending your elbows, keeping upper arms still, until your forearms are vertical. Lower the bar back down with control, squeezing at the top of the movement.

name: Standing Biceps Cable Curl
description: The standing biceps cable curl uses a straight bar attached to a low pulley to target the biceps through constant tension. Stand facing the cable stack, grasp the bar with an underhand grip, and curl it toward your shoulders while keeping your elbows close to your sides. Control the weight on the way down. Typically performed for moderate to high reps (8-12) in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand facing the cable stack with feet shoulder-width apart. Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, keeping elbows close to your sides. Curl the bar toward your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top. Lower the bar back down to the starting position, controlling the weight throughout the movement.

name: Cable Rope Hammer Curl
description: The cable rope hammer curl is an arm-focused exercise using a rope attachment on a cable machine with a neutral grip to emphasize the biceps and forearms. The constant cable tension challenges the brachialis and grip strength, making it well-suited to moderate to high rep ranges.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the rope attachment at chest height. Hold the rope with a neutral grip, palms facing each other. Curl the rope towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still, then lower it back to the starting position in a controlled motion. Keep your core engaged and maintain tension throughout the exercise.

name: Lying Cable Biceps Curl
description: The lying cable biceps curl is performed lying on your back with your feet toward a cable stack, curling the handle toward your shoulders to isolate the biceps through a long range of motion. It is typically done for moderate to high reps to build size and endurance in the biceps.
instructions: Lie on your back with your feet toward a cable stack. Grasp the cable handle with your palms facing forward. Curl the handle toward your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still, and then lower it back to the starting position. Breathe naturally and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Cable Rope Preacher Hammer Curl
description: The cable rope preacher hammer curl is a single-joint arm exercise that targets the biceps using a cable stack, preacher bench, and rope attachment. The neutral hammer grip emphasizes the brachialis and forearms for added arm thickness, while the preacher bench angle minimizes shoulder involvement to better isolate the biceps. Typically performed for moderate to high reps (8–15 per set) to build strength and size.
instructions: Sit at a preacher bench with your arms resting on the pad. Grasp a rope attachment connected to a cable stack with a neutral hammer grip, palms facing towards your body. Curl the rope up towards your shoulders, keeping your upper arms still, and then lower it back down to the starting position. Control the movement throughout the entire range of motion.

name: Lying Close-Grip Bar Curl On High Pulley
description: The lying close-grip bar curl on a high pulley is a cable-based biceps exercise performed lying prone, pulling the bar down toward the body to place constant tension on the biceps through a unique angle of resistance.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench under a high pulley with a close-grip bar. Grasp the bar with hands shoulder-width apart and palms facing up. Pull the bar down toward your chest, keeping your upper arms still and only moving your forearms. Lower the bar back to the starting position in a slow and controlled manner.

name: Squatting Cable EZ-Bar Biceps Curl
description: A biceps curl performed with an EZ bar attached to a cable machine while holding a static squat position. This variation targets the biceps similarly to a preacher curl while also engaging the lower body isometrically for stability.
instructions: Stand in a squat position with feet shoulder-width apart, holding an EZ-bar attached to a cable machine at chest height. Keep your back straight and core engaged, maintaining the squat position throughout the exercise. Curl the EZ-bar up towards your shoulders, squeezing your biceps at the top of the movement. Lower the bar back down to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid swinging.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Cable Straight-Bar Biceps Curl
description: A cable-based variation of the standing barbell curl using a straight bar attachment. The constant tension from the cable targets the biceps through the full range of motion, making it ideal as a primary arm movement or a high-rep burnout in upper-body workouts.
