instructions: Lie on your back with one knee bent and the other leg extended. Press through the heel of the bent leg to lift your hips, keeping your body in a straight line from shoulder to knee. Squeeze your glute at the top, pause briefly. Lower under control and repeat before switching sides.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Donkey Kick
description: From a hands-and-knees position, extend one leg back and up toward the ceiling in a controlled kicking motion, focusing on squeezing the glutes at the top while keeping the core braced and lower back stable.
instructions: Start on hands and knees with core engaged. Lift one leg back and up toward the ceiling in a controlled motion. Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement, keeping your lower back stable. Lower your leg back down to the starting position, repeating with the other leg.

name: Holman Hip Raise with Alternating Kick
description: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, arms at your sides. Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to lift your hips into a bridge so your body forms a straight line from shoulders to knees. From this position, extend one leg straight out, kicking it forward while keeping hips level and glutes engaged. Return the foot to the floor, then repeat with the opposite leg, alternating kicks while maintaining a strong bridge position. Lower your hips back to the floor under control to complete the set.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Lift your hips into a bridge, squeezing your glutes and bracing your core to form a straight line from shoulders to knees. Alternate kicking one leg straight out while keeping hips level, then return to the starting position and repeat with the other leg. Lower your hips back to the floor under control to complete the set.

name: Holman Left Leg Donkey Kick
description: From an all-fours position, extend your left leg back and up toward the ceiling in a controlled kicking motion, squeezing the glute at the top while keeping your core braced and lower back stable.
instructions: Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips. Lift your left leg, keeping it straight, and extend it back and up toward the ceiling in a controlled motion. Squeeze your glute at the top of the movement, bracing your core and keeping your lower back stable. Lower your leg back down to the starting position, repeating the motion in a slow and controlled manner.

name: Holman Diagonal Right Leg Step-Out to Right Knee-Up
description: From a standing position, step your right leg diagonally out to the side, lowering into a partial squat to load the glutes. Drive through the right foot to return to center while lifting the right knee up toward your chest, engaging the glutes and core for balance. Repeat for reps, then switch sides.
instructions: Stand with feet together. Step your right leg diagonally out to the side, lowering into a partial squat. Drive through your right foot to return to center, lifting your right knee up toward your chest. Repeat on the left side by stepping your left leg diagonally out to the side and lifting your left knee up.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Right Leg Donkey Kick
description: From a hands-and-knees position, extend your right leg back and up toward the ceiling in a controlled kicking motion, squeezing the glute at the top while keeping your core braced and lower back stable.
instructions: Start on your hands and knees with core engaged. Lift your right leg, keeping it straight, and kick backward and upward toward the ceiling. Squeeze your glute at the top of the movement, holding for a brief moment. Lower your leg back down to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the exercise.

name: Holman Butt Tap to Jump
description: A dynamic lower-body plyometric exercise where you squat down to lightly tap your glutes toward a target (such as a box or imaginary seat) and then explode upward into a vertical jump, focusing on powerful hip extension and soft landings.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Squat down, lightly tapping your glutes toward the target, keeping your back straight and knees behind your toes. Explode upward into a vertical jump, extending your hips and pushing through your heels. Land softly on the balls of your feet, immediately preparing to repeat the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Butt Tap to Jump-Forward-Jump-Back
description: From a standing position, hinge at the hips and tap your glutes with your hands as you sit into a partial squat, then explosively jump forward, land softly, and immediately jump back to the starting spot. Maintain a strong core and controlled landings throughout to target the glutes and legs while building power and coordination.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hinge at the hips and sit into a partial squat, tapping your glutes with your hands. Explosively jump forward, landing softly on the balls of your feet. Immediately jump back to the starting spot, maintaining a strong core and controlled landing throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Traveling High Knees to Squat Thrust
description: A dynamic combination exercise that alternates traveling high knees with a squat thrust. Jog forward driving your knees up toward hip height, then drop into a squat, place your hands on the floor, and jump or step your feet back into a plank before returning to standing and continuing forward.
instructions: Jog forward, bringing knees up toward hip height. Drop into a squat, placing hands on the floor, and jump or step feet back into a plank position. Quickly return feet to squat position and stand up, continuing to jog forward. Repeat, alternating between high knees and squat thrusts.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Butt Kicker to High Knees
description: A dynamic lower-body drill that alternates butt kickers with high knees to build hip power, coordination, and conditioning while targeting the glutes and legs.
instructions: Start in a standing position with feet hip-width apart. Begin by kicking your heels back towards your glutes, alternating legs in a rapid motion. Immediately transition to high knees, bringing one knee up towards your chest while quickly switching to the other knee. Continue alternating between butt kickers and high knees, maintaining a dynamic and explosive pace.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Daisy Cutter
description: A lower-body strength exercise targeting the glutes with a hip-hinge and extension pattern, performed using only bodyweight.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hinge at the hips, keeping back straight, and lower torso until nearly parallel to ground. Push through heels to extend hips, squeezing glutes at the top of the movement. Return to starting position, controlling the descent to repeat.

name: Holman Frogger
description: From a high plank position, jump your feet forward to the outside of your hands into a deep squat, then jump or step them back to plank. Keep your core braced and hips low as you transition between positions to target the glutes and lower body while maintaining a strong upper-body support.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands shoulder-width apart. Jump your feet forward to the outside of your hands, lowering into a deep squat. Keep your core braced and hips low, then jump or step your feet back to the plank position. Repeat, maintaining a strong upper-body support throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Hip Raise with Chest Press
description: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, holding a weight over your chest with arms extended. Brace your core and squeeze your glutes to lift your hips into a bridge position while maintaining a neutral spine. At the top of the hip raise, perform a controlled chest press by lowering the weight toward your chest and pressing it back up, then lower your hips back to the floor and repeat.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, holding a weight over your chest with arms extended. Brace your core and lift your hips into a bridge position, squeezing your glutes and maintaining a neutral spine. At the top, lower the weight to your chest and press it back up in a controlled motion. Lower your hips back to the floor and repeat.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Total Fitness Bear Crawl
description: The bear crawl is a bodyweight crawling exercise that challenges the entire body, elevates heart rate, and builds strength and stability in the shoulders, core, and legs. It can be used as a dynamic warm-up, in short bursts for power or circuits, or for longer durations as conditioning.
instructions: Start on hands and toes with knees slightly lifted. Keep back straight and engage core for stability. Crawl forward, moving hands and feet in unison, keeping knees low to the ground. Maintain control and balance throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Pigeon Stretch
description: The pigeon stretch is a lower-body stretch targeting the glutes and hips, often used in warm-ups, cool-downs, or to relieve training-related soreness.
instructions: Start on your hands and knees. Bring one knee forward and place your foot on the ground in front of the other knee. Lower your hips down towards the ground, stretching the back leg and glutes. Hold the stretch for 30 seconds and then switch legs.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Levitating Side Kick-Through
description: The levitating side kick-through is a dynamic full-body bodyweight movement that starts in a bear crawl position and transitions into a partial handstand with a leg kicked out to the side. It challenges strength, coordination, and control while engaging the core, shoulders, and hips, and is often used in circuits, animal-flow style training, or as part of a dynamic warm-up.
instructions: Start in a bear crawl position with hands and toes on the ground. Kick one leg out to the side, lifting your hips and transitioning into a partial handstand. Keep your core engaged and control your body as you lift and lower your leg. Return to the starting bear crawl position and repeat with the other leg.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: 30 Legs Hip Thrust
description: A hip-dominant lower-body exercise performed with bodyweight to target the glutes by driving the hips upward from a bent-knee position, focusing on squeezing the glutes at the top of each repetition.
instructions: Sit on the floor with knees bent and feet flat. Place hands under glutes for support, if needed. Drive hips upward, squeezing glutes at the top of the movement, then slowly lower back down to the starting position. Repeat for 30 repetitions, focusing on controlled movements and glute engagement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Dumbbell Single-Leg Hip Thrust
description: A unilateral hip-dominant exercise where you support your upper back on a bench or elevated surface, plant one foot on the ground, and hold a dumbbell across the working-side hip. Driving through the heel, you extend the hip to lift the pelvis until the torso is in line with the thigh, emphasizing the glutes and hamstrings while also challenging core stability and balance.
instructions: Sit on a bench or elevated surface with your upper back supported. Place one foot on the ground and hold a dumbbell across the hip of the working leg. Drive through your heel to lift your pelvis, extending your hip until your torso is in line with your thigh. Lower your pelvis back to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid injury.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Medicine Ball Duck Walk
description: The medicine ball duck walk is a dynamic lower-body exercise performed in a low squat position while holding a medicine ball, challenging the legs and glutes while elevating the heart rate.
instructions: Hold a medicine ball at chest level with both hands. Lower into a squat position with back straight and knees behind toes. Walk forward in small steps, keeping the squat position and engaging your legs and glutes. Keep the medicine ball stable and control your movements to maintain balance.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Exercise Ball Hip Thrust
description: A hip thrust variation performed with the upper back supported on an exercise ball, using bodyweight or bands to target the glutes and hamstrings while promoting proper hip extension and core stability.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your upper back against an exercise ball. Place your feet flat on the ground, hip-width apart, and knees bent at a 90-degree angle. Lift your hips up towards the ceiling, squeezing your glutes and pushing your heels into the ground, then slowly lower back down to the starting position. Keep your core engaged throughout the movement to maintain stability and control.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Downward Facing Balance
description: From a downward-facing position, extend one leg back and engage your glutes and core to maintain a stable, balanced posture while holding the position for time.
instructions: Start in a downward-facing position with hands under shoulders and toes on ground. Engage your core and lift one leg off the ground, extending it backwards. Keep your glutes and core muscles tight to maintain balance and stability. Hold the position for the desired time, then slowly lower your leg back down.

name: Band Good Morning (Pull Through)
description: A hip-hinge exercise using resistance bands to target the hamstrings and glutes while reinforcing proper posterior chain mechanics and hip extension.
instructions: Stand on the middle of a resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the ends of the band in each hand, with arms straight and palms facing back. Hinge at the hips, keeping back straight, and push hips back to lower your torso, pulling the band apart. Return to standing, squeezing glutes and pushing hips forward.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Standing Hamstring and Calf Stretch
description: A standing stretch where you hinge at the hips with one leg extended in front, lightly dorsiflexing the ankle to lengthen both the hamstrings and calves while maintaining a neutral spine.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Step one foot forward, keeping heel on ground and knee straight. Hinge at hips, leaning forward slightly, and dorsiflex ankle to stretch hamstrings and calves. Keep spine neutral and hold stretch for 15-30 seconds, then switch legs.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Hamstring-SMR
description: Self-myofascial release for the hamstrings using a foam roller. Sit with the back of your thighs on the roller, hands on the floor behind you for support, and slowly roll from just above the back of the knees up toward the glutes, pausing on tender spots to reduce tightness and improve tissue quality.
instructions: Sit on the floor with the back of your thighs on a foam roller. Place your hands behind you for support and balance. Slowly roll the foam roller from just above the knee up towards the glutes, pausing on tender areas to release tension. Roll at a comfortable pace, breathing deeply to help relax the muscles.

name: Barbell Deadlift
description: The barbell deadlift is a compound lift that targets the entire posterior chain, building overall strength and muscle mass. Performed by lifting a loaded barbell from the floor to a standing, locked-out position, it heavily engages the hamstrings, glutes, and lower back while also recruiting the upper back and core for stability. Variations include the conventional stance with hands outside the knees and the sumo stance with a wider foot position and hands inside the knees.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing straight or slightly outward. Grip the barbell with hands outside the knees, keeping back straight and core engaged. Lift the barbell up to hip level, squeezing glutes and pushing hips back, then lower it back down to the starting position. Keep the movement controlled and slow, avoiding jerky or bouncy motions.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Clean Deadlift
description: A barbell deadlift variation performed with the starting position and posture of a clean, emphasizing leg drive and an upright torso to build strength for Olympic lifts.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, gripping the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Keep your back straight and core engaged, with a slight bend in your knees. Lift the barbell up to hip level, using your legs to drive the movement and keeping your torso upright. Lower the barbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the entire range.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Sumo Deadlift
description: The sumo deadlift is a compound barbell lift performed with a wide stance and toes pointed outward, gripping the bar inside the legs. It emphasizes the hamstrings, glutes, and upper back/traps while also heavily engaging the hip muscles due to the wide stance.
instructions: Stand with a wide stance, toes pointed outward, and feet wider than shoulder-width apart. Grip the barbell with hands inside your legs, shoulders relaxed and back straight. Lift the barbell up by pushing hips back and down, then standing up straight, squeezing glutes and pushing hips forward. Lower the barbell back down to the starting position, keeping control throughout the entire movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Romanian Deadlift from Deficit
description: A hip-hinge variation of the Romanian deadlift performed while standing on an elevated surface to increase the range of motion and emphasize the hamstrings and glutes.
instructions: Stand on an elevated surface with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a barbell with hands shoulder-width apart, keeping back straight and core engaged. Hinge at the hips, lowering the barbell down towards the ground, keeping knees slightly bent and stretching the hamstrings. Lift the barbell back up to the starting position, squeezing glutes and pushing hips forward.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Power Snatch
description: The power snatch is an explosive Olympic lift where you pull a barbell from the floor to an overhead position in one powerful movement, catching it above parallel in a partial squat. It develops full-body power, coordination, and speed, with emphasis on the posterior chain.
instructions: Stand over the barbell with feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, keeping back straight and core engaged. Explosively lift the barbell from the floor, pulling it up to an overhead position in one swift motion, catching it above parallel in a partial squat. Lower the barbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Power Clean from Blocks
description: A compound Olympic lifting movement where you pull a barbell from elevated blocks to the shoulders in one explosive motion, emphasizing powerful hip extension and leg drive while reducing the range of motion from the floor.
instructions: Stand in front of blocks with feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, keeping back straight and core engaged. Explosively pull the barbell up, extending hips and legs, and catch it at shoulder height with elbows high. Lower the barbell back to the blocks, controlling the movement throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Snatch Deadlift
description: A deadlift variation using a wide snatch grip to increase range of motion and emphasize the posterior chain, particularly the hamstrings and upper back.
instructions: Stand over the bar with feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the bar with a wide snatch grip, hands wider than shoulder-width apart. Lift the bar up to hip level, keeping back straight and core engaged, then lower it back down to the starting position. Keep the movement controlled and focus on squeezing your hamstrings and upper back throughout the lift.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Power Clean
description: The power clean is a full-body explosive lift where a barbell is pulled from the floor and caught in the front rack position without dropping into a full squat. It is performed in three main phases: the initial pull from the floor, the powerful extension through the hips, knees, and ankles, and the quick turnover to receive the bar in a partial squat with hips higher than a full-depth squat. This movement develops total-body power, strength, and coordination and is often used as a standalone exercise or as part of the clean and press or clean and jerk.
instructions: Stand over the barbell with feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, then lift the bar off the floor by explosively extending hips, knees, and ankles. Receive the bar in a partial squat position, keeping hips higher than a full-depth squat and elbows high to rack the bar. Control the movement throughout, keeping core engaged and back straight.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Hang Snatch
description: From a standing position with the barbell held at hip or mid-thigh level, hinge at the hips, then explosively extend the hips, knees, and ankles to drive the bar upward, pulling it close to the body and catching it overhead in a partial squat before standing tall with arms locked out.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding the barbell at hip level. Hinge at the hips, keeping back straight, then explosively extend hips, knees, and ankles to drive the bar upward. Pull the bar close to the body and catch it overhead in a partial squat. Stand tall with arms locked out, squeezing shoulders and engaging core for balance.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Wide Stance Stiff Legs
description: A barbell deadlift variation performed with a wider-than-shoulder stance and relatively straight legs to emphasize the hamstrings and glutes while maintaining a flat back and hinging at the hips.
instructions: Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width apart. Hold a barbell with hands shoulder-width apart and keep your back flat. Hinge at the hips and lower the barbell down to mid-shin level, keeping your legs relatively straight. Lift the barbell back up to the starting position, squeezing your hamstrings and glutes.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Clean
description: The clean is a full-body Olympic weightlifting movement where you explosively lift a barbell from the floor to the front rack position on your shoulders, using powerful leg drive, hip extension, and an upright catch to develop strength, power, and coordination.
instructions: Stand over the barbell with feet shoulder-width apart. Bend down and grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, keeping back straight and core engaged. Explosively lift the bar up to the front rack position on your shoulders, using powerful leg drive and hip extension to generate force. Catch the bar with elbows high and core tight, standing upright to complete the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Barbell Good Morning
description: A hip-hinge exercise where a barbell rests across the upper back while you bend forward at the hips with a slight knee bend, then return to standing. It primarily strengthens the hamstrings, glutes, and lower back and is often used as an accessory movement for deadlift development.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell across your upper back. Hinge at the hips, keeping your back straight, and bend your knees slightly as you lean forward. Lower your torso until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings, then push through your heels to return to standing. Keep your core engaged and control the movement throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Barbell Stiff-Legged Deadlift
description: The barbell stiff-legged deadlift is a hip-hinge exercise that emphasizes the hamstrings and glutes while also engaging the lower back, upper back, and core. It is commonly used as an accessory movement to build strength and muscle for the conventional deadlift and to develop posterior chain strength.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart behind a barbell. Bend at the hips to grab the barbell with hands shoulder-width apart, keeping knees slightly bent. Lift the barbell up and stand upright, squeezing glutes and pushing hips back, then lower it back down to the starting position. Keep back straight and core engaged throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Romanian Deadlift
description: The Romanian deadlift is a barbell hip-hinge exercise that emphasizes the hamstrings and glutes while also engaging the lower back, upper back, and core. It is commonly used as an accessory lift for the conventional deadlift and as a primary muscle-building movement for the posterior chain.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing straight or slightly outward. Hold a barbell with hands shoulder-width apart, keeping back straight and core engaged. Hinge at the hips, lowering the barbell to just below knee level, squeezing glutes and pushing hips backward. Return to standing position, keeping control throughout the movement.

name: Sumo Deadlift with Chains
description: A wide-stance deadlift variation performed with a barbell and chains. The chains increase resistance as you stand up, emphasizing lockout strength while targeting the posterior chain.
instructions: Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width apart, toes pointing outward. Hold the barbell with an overhand grip, keeping hands shoulder-width apart and chains hanging from the bar. Lift the barbell up, keeping back straight and core engaged, and stand up, squeezing glutes and pushing hips back. Lower the barbell back down to the starting position, controlling the movement and keeping tension in the chains.

name: Sumo Deadlift with Bands
description: A wide-stance deadlift variation using a barbell and resistance bands to increase tension throughout the lift, emphasizing the hamstrings and glutes while also engaging the lower back and full posterior chain.
instructions: Stand over the barbell with a wide stance, toes pointing outward. Grip the barbell with hands outside of legs, keeping back straight and core engaged. Lower your body down, lifting the barbell up with you, keeping the movement slow and controlled, focusing on squeezing glutes and hamstrings. Lift to hip level, then return to starting position.

name: Good Morning off Pins
description: A barbell hip-hinge variation performed with the bar set on safety pins in a rack. Starting from the pins, you hinge at the hips while keeping a neutral spine, then extend the hips to stand more upright, emphasizing the hamstrings and glutes with reduced range from the pins for added control and safety.

Push through your heels to extend your hips and stand more upright, squeezing your glutes. Return to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Stiff-Legged Deadlift
description: The barbell stiff-legged deadlift is a hip-hinge movement that emphasizes the hamstrings and glutes while also engaging the lower back, upper back, and core for stability. It is commonly used as an accessory lift to build strength and muscle in the posterior chain.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes pointing straight ahead. Hold a barbell with hands shoulder-width apart, keeping back straight and core engaged. Lift the barbell by hinging at the hips, keeping knees straight, and squeezing glutes and hamstrings. Lower the barbell back to starting position, maintaining control and proper form.

name: Snatch Pull
description: A barbell power movement that focuses on explosively pulling the bar from the floor to hip and chest height, reinforcing the first and second pull phases of the snatch while building strength in the posterior chain and upper back.
instructions: Stand over the barbell with feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, keeping back straight and core engaged. Explosively pull the bar up to hip and chest height, using legs and hips to generate power. Lower the bar back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Hanging Bar Good Morning
description: Stand with a barbell held in your hands at arm’s length in front of your thighs. With a slight bend in your knees and a neutral spine, hinge at the hips to lower your torso until it is roughly parallel to the floor or as far as comfortable, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings and glutes. Keep the bar close to your legs throughout. Drive your hips forward and contract your hamstrings and glutes to return to the starting position.
instructions: Stand with a barbell held at arm's length in front of your thighs. Hinge at the hips, keeping a slight bend in your knees and a neutral spine, to lower your torso until it's roughly parallel to the floor. Keep the bar close to your legs throughout the movement. Drive your hips forward to return to the starting position, contracting your hamstrings and glutes.

name: Reverse Band Sumo Deadlift
description: A deadlift variation performed with a wide sumo stance and resistance bands attached above the barbell to reduce load off the floor and increase it toward lockout, helping reinforce proper hip drive and lockout strength.
instructions: Stand over the barbell with a wide sumo stance, toes pointing outward. Grip the barbell with hands inside your legs, keeping your back straight and core engaged. Lift the barbell up, driving your hips forward and squeezing your glutes, then lower it back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout. Keep your movements slow and controlled, focusing on proper form and technique.

name: Muscle Snatch
description: From a standing position with a barbell at hip level or from the floor, explosively extend your hips and knees while pulling the bar upward close to your body. As the bar rises, aggressively shrug and pull with the arms, then punch the bar overhead to a locked-out position without re-bending into a deep squat. Stand tall with the bar overhead, then lower it under control and repeat.

Aggressively shrug and pull with your arms, punching the bar overhead to a locked-out position. Lower the bar under control to the starting position and repeat.

name: Split Snatch
description: From the floor, explosively pull the barbell upward, extending hips, knees, and ankles. As the bar travels overhead, quickly drop into a split stance, catching the bar locked out overhead with arms straight and torso upright. Stabilize, then step the feet back together under control to finish standing with the bar overhead before lowering it safely.

As the bar travels overhead, drop into a split stance, catching the bar locked out overhead with arms straight. Step the feet back together under control to finish standing with the bar overhead before lowering it safely.

name: Hang Snatch - Below Knees
description: From a standing position, hold a barbell with a wide grip and lower it to just below the knees by hinging at the hips while keeping the back flat. Explosively extend the hips and knees, shrug the shoulders, and pull the bar upward, then quickly drop under the bar into a partial squat to catch it overhead with locked arms. Stand up fully to complete the rep, then lower the bar back to the hang position under control.

Explosively extend your hips and knees, then quickly drop under the bar into a partial squat to catch it overhead with locked arms. Stand up fully to complete the rep, then lower the bar back to the hang position under control.

name: 30 Legs Sumo Deadlift
description: A deadlift variation with a wide stance and toes turned out, emphasizing the inner thighs, glutes, and hamstrings while keeping the torso more upright than a conventional deadlift.

name: Kettlebell Pass-Through Lunge
description: The kettlebell pass-through lunge is a dynamic lower-body and core exercise where you perform a forward or reverse lunge while passing a kettlebell under the front leg from one hand to the other. This challenges leg strength, core stability, coordination, and shoulder and upper-back engagement, and is often used in circuit or full-body time-efficient workouts.

Pass the kettlebell under the front leg to the other hand, keeping core engaged and back straight. Push back to standing, then repeat with the other leg.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Kettlebell One-Legged Deadlift
description: The kettlebell one-legged deadlift is a unilateral hip-hinge exercise where one foot stays planted while the other leg extends backward as you hinge at the hips. It targets the hamstrings and glutes while also challenging balance, grip, and core stability.

Hinge at the hips, lowering the kettlebell down towards the ground while extending your lifted leg backward, keeping it straight. Return to the starting position by squeezing your glutes and pushing your hips forward.

name: Single-Arm Kettlebell Clean
description: The single-arm kettlebell clean is an explosive hip-dominant movement where you swing a kettlebell from between your legs up to the rack position at shoulder level in one smooth motion. It builds power through the hips and posterior chain while challenging core stability and shoulder control.

Swing kettlebell back between legs, then explosively drive hips forward to generate power, lifting kettlebell up to rack position at shoulder level. Keep elbow close to body and control the movement throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Single-leg kettlebell deadlift
description: The single-leg kettlebell deadlift is a unilateral hip-hinge exercise where one foot remains planted while the other leg extends backward as you hinge at the hips. It targets the hamstrings and glutes while also challenging balance, grip strength, and core stability.

Keep your back straight and core engaged, then push through your heel to return to standing position. Repeat on the other leg.

name: Romanian Deadlift with Kettlebell

Lower the kettlebell toward mid-shin, feeling a stretch in your hamstrings without rounding your spine. Drive your hips forward to return to standing, squeezing your glutes at the top. Move in a controlled manner throughout the entire range of motion.

Feel a stretch in your hamstrings without rounding your spine. Drive your hips forward to return to standing, squeezing your glutes at the top.

name: Single-arm Kettlebell Swing
description: The single-arm kettlebell swing is a hip-hinge power exercise that targets the hamstrings and glutes while also engaging the back and core. Quickly swing the kettlebell forward to chest height, using hip power and keeping arm straight. Control the kettlebell as it swings back down, repeating for desired reps before switching arms.

name: Kettlebell Dead Clean

Drive through your hips to generate power and swing the kettlebell up to the rack position, keeping your arm close to your body. Keep your core engaged and control the kettlebell as you catch it in the rack position.

name: Box Skip

description: The hamstring slide is a bodyweight floor exercise performed lying on your back. name: Lying Cable Leg Curl

name: Standing Cable Leg Curl
description: The standing cable leg curl is a unilateral hamstring isolation exercise performed with an ankle attachment on a cable machine. It targets the hamstrings by flexing the knee against resistance while standing, and is typically done for moderate to high reps (8–12+) as part of lower-body training.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with the ankle attachment secured to one leg. Lift your working leg backwards, curling your heel towards your glutes, keeping your knee bent at a 90-degree angle. Keep your core engaged and your standing leg stable throughout the movement. Lower your working leg back to the starting position, controlling the return to avoid jerky movements.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Lying Leg Curls
description: A machine-based leg exercise performed lying face down, curling the lower legs toward the glutes to target the hamstrings with additional involvement from the glutes and calves. Commonly used for moderate to high reps as a muscle-building movement or pre-exhaust in lower-body workouts.
instructions: Lie face down on the machine with legs extended. Hook your legs under the curling pad, ensuring it's at the correct height for your legs. Curl your legs up towards your glutes, squeezing your hamstrings at the top of the movement. Lower your legs back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.

name: Glute Ham Raise
description: The glute ham raise is a posterior-chain exercise performed on a glute-ham developer (GHD), emphasizing controlled knee flexion and hip extension to strengthen the hamstrings and glutes.
instructions: Sit on the GHD with thighs on the pad and feet secured. Lower your torso by bending at the knees, keeping your back straight. Lift your torso back up by extending your hips and knees, squeezing your glutes and hamstrings. Control the movement throughout the entire range of motion.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Reverse Hyperextension
description: Lie face down on a bench or reverse hyper machine with your hips at the edge and legs hanging straight down. Brace your core and squeeze your glutes and hamstrings to lift your legs until they are in line with your torso, avoiding excessive arching of the lower back. Pause briefly at the top, then lower your legs under control to the starting position.
instructions: Lie face down on a bench or reverse hyper machine with hips at the edge and legs hanging straight down. Brace your core and squeeze glutes and hamstrings to lift legs until in line with torso. Avoid arching your lower back and pause briefly at the top. Lower your legs under control to the starting position.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Seated Leg Curl
description: A machine-based lower-body exercise performed in a seated position to isolate and strengthen the hamstrings. The lifter flexes the knees against a padded lever, curling the weight down and back, then returns under control for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Sit at the machine with your legs extended and feet secured under the padded lever. Lift the lever by curling your heels towards your glutes, squeezing your hamstrings. Lower the weight back down to the starting position under control. Keep your back against the seat and core engaged throughout the exercise.

name: Standing Leg Curl
description: Stand facing a support with the working leg positioned behind you and the pad resting just above the back of your ankle. Keeping your torso upright and core braced, bend your knee to curl your heel toward your glutes without swinging your hips. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the weight under control to the starting position and repeat for the desired reps before switching legs.
instructions: Stand facing a support with your working leg behind you and the pad above your ankle. Keep your torso upright and core braced. Bend your knee to curl your heel toward your glutes without swinging your hips. Lower the weight under control to the starting position and repeat.

name: Smith Machine Stiff-Legged Deadlift
description: A guided variation of the stiff-legged deadlift performed on a Smith machine to target the hamstrings and glutes with added bar path stability. The fixed bar track helps maintain consistent form while you hinge at the hips with a slight knee bend, emphasizing a strong stretch through the hamstrings and controlled extension to stand tall. Commonly used with moderate to high reps in lower-body or full-body strength routines.
instructions: Stand in a Smith machine with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the bar with an overhand grip and slight knee bend. Hinge at the hips, lowering the bar down to mid-shin level, stretching the hamstrings. Lift the bar back up to the starting position, squeezing glutes and pushing hips forward.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Lying Leg Curl
description: A machine-based leg exercise performed lying face down, curling the lower legs toward the glutes to target the hamstrings with additional involvement from the glutes and calves. Commonly used for moderate to high reps as a muscle-building or pre-exhaust movement in lower-body workouts.
instructions: Lie face down on the machine with legs extended. Hook your legs under the curl pad, ensuring it's centered. Lift the pad by curling your legs toward your glutes, squeezing your hamstrings. Lower the pad back down to the starting position in a controlled manner.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Single-Leg Lying Leg Curl
description: The single-leg lying leg curl is a machine-based hamstring exercise performed one leg at a time. Lying face down, you curl the pad toward your glutes, emphasizing the hamstrings while also engaging the glutes and calves. Training each leg separately helps improve mind-muscle connection and address strength imbalances. It is typically used with moderate to high reps as a muscle-building movement or as part of a leg pre-exhaust sequence.
instructions: Lie face down on the machine with one leg secured under the pad. Curl the pad toward your glutes by bending your knee, squeezing your hamstrings. Control the return to the starting position, avoiding momentum. Switch legs to train the other hamstring, repeating the process.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Single-Leg Leg Extension
description: The single-leg leg extension is a machine-based isolation exercise targeting the quadriceps. Performed one leg at a time, it helps improve mind-muscle connection, address strength imbalances between legs, and build muscle in the front of the thighs. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions as part of a lower-body strength or hypertrophy routine.
instructions: Sit at the leg extension machine with one leg under the pad. Lift the weight up by straightening your leg, focusing on squeezing your quadriceps. Lower the weight back down to the starting position in a controlled manner. Repeat with the other leg to maintain balance and symmetry.

name: Machine Hamstring Curl
description: A machine-based leg exercise performed in a seated position to isolate and strengthen the hamstrings. Commonly used with moderate to high repetitions as part of lower-body muscle-building or pre-exhaust work.
instructions: Sit at the machine with your legs extended in front of you. Place your legs under the lever pad, adjusting it to fit your leg length. Lift your heels towards your glutes by curling your legs, squeezing your hamstrings at the top. Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Natural Glute Ham Raise
description: The natural glute ham raise is a bodyweight posterior-chain exercise performed without a dedicated machine. From a kneeling position with your ankles secured, you lower your torso toward the floor by extending at the knees and hips, then contract your hamstrings and glutes to pull your body back up. It strongly targets the hamstrings and glutes while also engaging the lower back and calves for stability.
instructions: Kneel on the floor with ankles secured under a stable object. Lower your torso toward the floor by extending at the knees and hips, keeping your back straight. Contract your hamstrings and glutes to pull your body back up to the starting position. Focus on slow and controlled movements to engage the target muscles effectively.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Standing Leg Swing
description: The standing leg swing is a dynamic warm-up exercise where you stand on one leg and swing the other leg forward and upward in front of you, as if punting an imaginary football, to dynamically stretch the hamstrings and prepare the lower body for activity.
instructions: Stand on one leg with the other foot lifted off the ground. Swing the lifted leg forward and upward, keeping it straight, as if kicking a ball. Lower your leg back down to the starting position and repeat on the other leg. Switch legs after a few repetitions to maintain balance and symmetry.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Linear Acceleration Wall Drill
description: A plyometric sprint drill performed leaning into a wall to train explosive linear acceleration, proper forward body angle, and powerful leg drive while minimizing impact and distance covered.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, leaning into a wall with hands on the wall at shoulder height. Keep your body at a 45-degree angle, engaging your core and keeping your back straight. Push off the wall with your hands and drive one knee forward, quickly switching to the other knee in a sprinting motion. Focus on explosive leg drive and proper body angle, repeating the motion for the desired duration.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Knee Tuck Jump
description: An explosive vertical jump where you drive off the ground and pull your knees toward your chest at the top of the jump, developing lower-body power and hip explosiveness.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Lower your body slightly by bending your knees, then explosively jump upward. As you reach the top of the jump, tuck your knees up toward your chest. Land softly on the balls of your feet to absorb the impact.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Leg-Up Hamstring Stretch
description: Lie on your back and extend one leg straight up toward the ceiling while keeping the other leg flat on the floor. Gently pull the raised leg toward you until you feel a stretch along the back of the thigh, then hold for time before switching sides.
instructions: Lie on your back with one leg extended straight up and the other leg flat on the floor. Gently pull the raised leg toward you until you feel a stretch in the back of your thigh. Hold the stretch for the desired time, then slowly release. Switch legs and repeat to stretch the other side.

name: Inchworm
description: The inchworm is a dynamic bodyweight exercise where you hinge at the hips to touch the floor, walk your hands forward into a straight-arm plank, then walk your feet toward your hands to return to the starting position. It is commonly used as a warm-up to increase core temperature and mobilize the entire body.
instructions: Start in a standing position with feet hip-width apart. Hinge at the hips to touch the floor with your hands, keeping your knees slightly bent. Walk your hands forward into a straight-arm plank position, engaging your core. Walk your feet toward your hands to return to the starting position.

name: Moving Claw Series
description: A dynamic plyometric lower-body drill that targets the hamstrings through repeated claw-like pulling motions while moving forward or laterally. Emphasis is on quick ground contact, controlled knee drive, and powerful hamstring engagement to improve speed, coordination, and athletic performance.
instructions: Start in an athletic stance with feet shoulder-width apart. Explode into a sprint, focusing on quick ground contact and powerful hamstring engagement. As you move forward, drive your knees upward and pull your heels towards your glutes, mimicking a claw-like motion. Keep your core tight and maintain control throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Lying Groin Stretch with Band
description: The lying groin stretch with band is a floor-based stretch targeting the inner thighs and groin. Lying on your back, you loop a band around one foot and gently guide the leg out to the side, keeping the opposite leg relaxed on the floor. This helps improve hip mobility and flexibility and can be used in warm-ups, between strength sets, or as part of a cool-down or recovery routine.
instructions: Lie on your back with one leg relaxed on the floor. Loop a band around the other foot and gently guide the leg out to the side. Keep your knee straight and hold the stretch for a few seconds. Return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.

name: 90/90 Hamstring Stretch
description: A floor-based hamstring stretch performed with one leg extended straight in front and the other leg bent at 90 degrees, helping to lengthen the hamstrings while keeping the spine neutral.
instructions: Sit on the floor with one leg straight out in front of you. Bend the other leg at a 90-degree angle, with your foot tucked in towards your straight leg. Keep your spine neutral and lean forward, stretching the back of your straight leg. Hold the stretch for 30 seconds and then switch legs.

name: World's Greatest Stretch
description: A dynamic, full-body mobility sequence that targets the hips, hamstrings, hip flexors, thoracic spine, and shoulders, making it ideal as a warm-up or general mobility drill.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: The Straddle
description: Sit on the floor with your legs extended wide apart in a straddle position, keeping your knees straight and toes pointed up. With a tall spine, hinge forward from the hips, reaching your hands toward the floor or your feet while maintaining a neutral back. Hold the stretch, breathing steadily, and avoid bouncing as you feel a gentle stretch through the inner thighs and hamstrings.
instructions: Sit on the floor with legs extended wide apart in a straddle position. Keep your knees straight and toes pointed up. Hinge forward from the hips, reaching hands toward the floor or feet, while maintaining a neutral back. Hold the stretch, breathing steadily, and avoid bouncing.

name: Prone Manual Hamstring Curl
description: Lie face down with your legs extended and a partner providing manual resistance at your lower legs. Bend your knees to curl your heels toward your glutes against the resistance, then slowly lower back to the start to strengthen the hamstrings.
instructions: Lie face down with legs extended and a partner holding your lower legs. Bend your knees to curl your heels toward your glutes, lifting against the resistance. Slowly lower your legs back to the starting position, controlling the movement. Keep your core engaged and focus on squeezing your hamstrings throughout the exercise.

name: Knee To Chest
description: Lie on your back with legs extended. Gently pull one knee toward your chest using your hands behind the thigh or on the shin, keeping the other leg straight and your lower back relaxed on the floor. Hold the stretch, then slowly release and switch sides, maintaining smooth breathing throughout.
instructions: Lie on your back with legs extended. Pull one knee toward your chest using your hands behind the thigh or on the shin. Keep the other leg straight and lower back relaxed on the floor. Hold the stretch and then slowly release, switching sides while maintaining smooth breathing.

name: Hurdle Hops
description: Stand with feet hip-width apart facing a series of low hurdles or imaginary barriers. Bend your knees and hips into a quarter squat, swing your arms, and explosively jump forward over the first hurdle, landing softly on the balls of your feet with knees slightly bent. Immediately rebound into the next hop with minimal ground contact time, maintaining an upright torso and controlled landings. Continue for the desired number of hurdles or distance, focusing on quick, powerful jumps and safe, quiet landings.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart facing the hurdles. Bend your knees and hips, then explosively jump forward over the first hurdle, landing softly on the balls of your feet. Immediately rebound into the next hop with minimal ground contact time, keeping your torso upright and knees slightly bent. Continue hopping over the hurdles, focusing on quick and controlled movements.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Hip Stretch With Twist
description: From a half-kneeling lunge position, shift your hips back to straighten the front leg and hinge forward at the hips to stretch the hamstring, then rotate your torso toward the front leg for an added twist. Hold the stretch, breathing steadily, then switch sides.
instructions: Start in a half-kneeling lunge position. Shift your hips back to straighten the front leg and hinge forward at the hips to stretch the hamstring. Rotate your torso toward the front leg, holding the stretch and breathing steadily. Switch sides to repeat the stretch on the other leg.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Linear 3-Part Start Technique
description: A plyometric sprint drill that breaks the linear start into three phases to improve acceleration mechanics, leg drive, and explosive power. Typically performed from a staggered stance, you progress through partial starts to full-speed sprints over a short distance.
instructions: Start in a staggered stance with front foot forward and back foot ready to drive. Explode forward with back foot, focusing on quick turnover and leg drive. Progress through partial starts, increasing speed and distance with each phase. Finish with a full-speed sprint over a short distance, maintaining proper form and explosive power.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Alternating Leg Swing
description: Stand tall and swing one leg forward and backward in a controlled motion, alternating legs to dynamically stretch the hamstrings and hip flexors while warming up the lower body.
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart. Swing one leg forward and backward in a controlled motion, keeping it straight. Alternate legs, swinging the other leg forward and backward. Keep your upper body still and focused on your posture throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Single-Leg Balance and Reach
description: A beginner balance exercise performed standing on one leg while reaching forward or to the side with the opposite hand to improve lower-body stability and control.
instructions: Stand on one leg with the other foot lifted off the ground. Keep your back straight and engage your core for stability. Reach forward or to the side with the opposite hand, keeping your movements slow and controlled. Lower your foot back down to the starting position and repeat on the other leg.

name: High Kick
description: Stand tall and swing one leg straight forward in a controlled kicking motion, aiming to bring your foot toward your outstretched hand to stretch and mobilize the hamstrings and hips.
instructions: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart. Lift one leg straight out in front of you, keeping it straight and controlled. Bring your leg up towards your outstretched hand, stretching your hamstring and hip. Lower your leg back down to the starting position and repeat with the other leg.

name: Boss Everline 10-Yard Sprints
description: Short, explosive 10-yard sprints focused on accelerating as fast as possible from a static start, emphasizing powerful leg drive and quick turnover.
instructions: Start in a static position with feet shoulder-width apart. Explode forward, driving one foot into the ground and quickly alternating legs to generate speed. Focus on powerful leg drive and quick turnover to cover the 10-yard distance as fast as possible. Finish the sprint and walk back to the starting point to repeat.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Standing Hamstring Stretch
description: The standing hamstring stretch is a flexibility exercise targeting the muscles along the back of the thighs. With one foot slightly in front of the other, you hinge at the hips and lean forward to create a strong stretch through the hamstrings, which can be used before lower-body training, sports, or as a restorative stretch anytime.
instructions: Stand with one foot slightly in front of the other. Hinge at the hips and slowly lean forward, keeping your back straight. Keep your front knee straight and let your back leg stretch, feeling the stretch in the back of your thigh. Hold for 15-30 seconds and switch legs.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Reverse Lunge
description: The reverse lunge is a lower-body exercise targeting the quadriceps, glutes, and hamstrings, with slightly greater emphasis on the glutes compared to forward lunge variations.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart. Take a large step backward with one foot, lowering your body until your back knee almost touches the ground. Keep your front thigh parallel to the ground and your front knee behind your toes. Push through your front heel to return to the starting position.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: HM Alternating Cross Lunge
description: From a standing position, step one leg back and across behind the front leg into a curtsy-style lunge, lowering until both knees are bent while keeping the chest upright and core braced. Push through the front heel to return to standing, then alternate sides, crossing the opposite leg behind on each rep to target the glutes and hamstrings.
instructions: Stand with feet together, engaging core and keeping chest upright. Step one leg back and across behind the front leg, lowering into a lunge with both knees bent. Push through the front heel to return to standing, then alternate sides by crossing the opposite leg behind. Repeat, targeting glutes and hamstrings with each rep.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: HM Alternating Front Kick
description: Stand tall and alternately kick one leg forward to hip height or as high as comfortable while keeping your core braced and torso upright. Lower the leg under control and repeat on the opposite side in a rhythmic, marching pattern.
instructions: Stand tall with core braced and torso upright. Alternately kick one leg forward to hip height, keeping knee straight. Lower the leg under control, then repeat on the opposite side in a rhythmic pattern. Continue alternating legs in a smooth, marching motion.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: HM Alternating Sprawl Kick-Through
description: From a high plank, drop into a sprawl by quickly kicking your feet out and lowering your hips toward the floor, then drive one leg underneath your body across the midline in a kick-through while rotating your hips and torso. Return to plank, repeat the sprawl, and kick through with the opposite leg, alternating sides to build strength, coordination, and conditioning.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands under shoulders and toes on ground. Drop into a sprawl by quickly kicking feet out and lowering hips toward floor. Drive one leg underneath body across the midline in a kick-through, rotating hips and torso. Alternate legs with each repetition, returning to plank position after each kick-through.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: HM Alternating Step-Back
description: From a standing position, step one foot back into a staggered stance, lightly tapping the toes behind you while keeping most of your weight on the front leg, then return to the start and alternate legs. Maintain a slight bend in the front knee and hinge the hips back to emphasize the hamstrings and glutes while keeping the torso tall and core braced.
instructions: Stand with feet together, engaging your core and keeping your torso tall. Step one foot back into a staggered stance, lightly tapping your toes behind you while keeping most of your weight on the front leg. Maintain a slight bend in the front knee and hinge your hips back, squeezing your hamstrings and glutes. Alternate legs, returning to the start position after each rep.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: HM Alternating Step-Up With Chair
description: Stand facing a sturdy chair or step. Place one foot fully on the chair, drive through the heel to step up, bringing the opposite knee up or lightly tapping the foot on the surface. Step back down under control and alternate legs each rep, keeping your torso upright and core braced.
instructions: Stand facing a sturdy chair with one foot on the floor and the other on the chair. Drive through the heel to step up, bringing the opposite knee up. Step back down under control and alternate legs. Keep your torso upright and core braced throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: HM Right Knee-Up
description: From a standing position, lift your right knee up toward hip height while maintaining an upright torso and engaged core, then lower it back down under control. Repeat for the desired reps to target the hamstrings and supporting muscles.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and engage your core. Lift your right knee up toward hip height, keeping your torso upright and stable. Lower your right knee back down to the starting position under control. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: HM Low Lunge With External Twist
description: From a low lunge position, lower the back knee toward the floor, keep the front knee stacked over the ankle, and rotate the torso outward away from the front leg, reaching the arm up to open the chest and hips while maintaining balance and control.
instructions: Start in a low lunge position with front knee over ankle. Lower back knee toward floor, keeping front knee bent at 90 degrees. Rotate torso outward, reaching arm up to open chest and hips, maintaining balance and control. Keep core engaged and return to starting position slowly.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: HM Ninja Crawl
description: A bodyweight crawling exercise performed on hands and feet with hips low, moving forward in a controlled, stealth-like pattern to build posterior chain strength, core stability, and coordination.
instructions: Start on hands and feet with hips low and engage core. Keep body in a straight line from head to heels. Move forward in a controlled crawl, stepping with one hand and the opposite foot, then alternating to the other side. Maintain a slow and stealth-like pace to challenge posterior chain and core stability.

name: HM Pigeon Stretch
description: From a tabletop position, bring one leg forward and place the shin across your body with the knee behind the same-side wrist and foot near the opposite hip. Extend the back leg straight behind you with hips squared to the floor. Keeping your torso tall or folding forward over the front leg, hold the stretch while breathing steadily, feeling a deep stretch through the glutes and hip area.
instructions: Start in a tabletop position on your hands and knees. Bring one leg forward, placing the shin across your body with the knee behind the same-side wrist and foot near the opposite hip. Extend the back leg straight behind you, keeping your hips squared to the floor. Hold the stretch, breathing steadily, and fold forward if desired to deepen the stretch in your glutes and hip area.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Single-Leg Deadlift Walk
description: A dynamic variation of the single-leg deadlift where you hinge on one leg, reach toward the floor, then step forward into the next rep, alternating legs as you walk. This targets the hamstrings and glutes while challenging balance and hip stability.
instructions: Stand on one leg with the other foot lifted. Hinge forward at the hips, reaching toward the ground with your hands, keeping your back straight and core engaged. Step forward with your lifted foot and repeat on the other leg, alternating as you walk. Keep your movements slow and controlled to maintain balance and stability.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Total Fitness Hamstring Slide
description: A bodyweight hamstring exercise performed lying on your back on a smooth surface with sliders or a cloth under your heels. From a bridge position, you slide your heels away to extend your legs, then bend your knees to pull your heels back toward your hips, keeping your hips elevated to maintain tension in the hamstrings. Often used as a primer before heavy squats or deadlifts or as an accessory movement in lower-body workouts.
instructions: Lie on your back on a smooth surface with sliders or a cloth under your heels. Lift your hips into a bridge position, squeezing your glutes and lower back. Slowly slide your heels away from you, extending your legs, then bend your knees to pull your heels back toward your hips, keeping your hips elevated. Maintain control and tension in your hamstrings throughout the movement.

name: 30 Legs Walking Lunge
description: Perform alternating walking lunges for 30 steps, focusing on controlled strides, upright posture, and driving through the front heel to target the legs and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet together, taking a large step forward with one foot. Lower your body down into a lunge, keeping your back straight and front knee behind your toes. Drive through the front heel to return to standing, immediately stepping forward with the other foot to repeat the motion. Alternate legs for 30 steps, maintaining controlled strides and upright posture throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Lying Hamstring Stretch with Band
description: The lying hamstring stretch with band is a floor-based stretch targeting the hamstrings. Lying on your back, you loop a band around one foot and gently pull the leg toward you while keeping the knee as straight as comfortable, feeling a stretch along the back of the thigh. It can be used in warm-ups, between exercises, or during cool-downs to improve flexibility and reduce tightness in the hamstrings.
instructions: Lie on your back with a band looped around one foot. Gently pull the leg toward you, keeping the knee as straight as comfortable. Hold the stretch for a few seconds, feeling it along the back of the thigh. Switch legs to stretch the other hamstring.

name: Upper Back-Leg Grab
description: From a standing position, hinge forward at the hips and reach to grab the backs of your legs or ankles, gently pulling your upper body toward your thighs to stretch the hamstrings and upper back. Keep a slight bend in the knees if needed and avoid bouncing, holding the stretch while breathing steadily.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart. Hinge forward at the hips, keeping a slight bend in the knees if needed, and reach to grab the backs of your legs or ankles. Gently pull your upper body toward your thighs, stretching your hamstrings and upper back. Hold the stretch, breathing steadily, and avoid bouncing.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Seated Hamstring Stretch
description: Sit on the floor with one or both legs extended straight in front of you, keeping your back tall. Hinge forward from the hips and reach toward your toes while maintaining a neutral spine, stopping when you feel a gentle stretch along the back of your thighs. Hold the position without bouncing, breathing steadily throughout the stretch.
instructions: Sit on the floor with one or both legs extended straight in front of you. Keep your back tall and hinge forward from the hips. Reach toward your toes, stopping when you feel a gentle stretch along the back of your thighs. Hold the position, breathing steadily, without bouncing.

name: Alternating Lunge Jump
description: The alternating lunge jump is an explosive bodyweight plyometric exercise that targets the quadriceps, glutes, and hamstrings. From a lunge position, you jump upward and switch legs in mid-air, landing in a lunge with the opposite leg forward. It can be performed for time or repetitions to build lower-body power, conditioning, and support fat loss or athletic performance.
instructions: Stand with feet together. Take a large step forward with one foot and lower your body into a lunge position, keeping your back knee almost touching the ground. Jump up and switch legs in mid-air, landing in a lunge with the opposite leg forward. Repeat, alternating legs with each jump.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Runner's Stretch
description: A forward lunge-based stretch that targets the hamstrings and hip flexors by extending the back leg and hinging the hips while maintaining a long spine.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Exercise Ball Leg Curl
description: The exercise ball leg curl is a hamstring-focused movement performed lying on your back with your heels on an exercise ball. By curling the ball toward your hips, it emphasizes the knee-flexion function of the hamstrings and also challenges the glutes and lower back for stability. It is a useful alternative when a leg curl machine is unavailable and can complement deadlift variations for more complete hamstring development.
instructions: Lie on your back with your arms at your sides and heels on the exercise ball. Lift your hips off the ground, squeezing your glutes and lower back for stability. Curl the ball toward your hips by bending your knees, keeping your upper body still. Slowly return the ball to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Ball Hamstring Hat Trick
description: A stability-ball hamstring curl variation performed lying on your back with your heels on a ball. You bridge your hips up, then curl the ball toward your glutes by bending your knees, emphasizing the hamstrings’ knee-flexion role while also engaging the glutes and core for stability.
instructions: Lie on your back with heels on the stability ball. Bridge your hips up towards the ceiling, engaging your glutes and core. Bend your knees to curl the ball towards your glutes, focusing on hamstring contraction. Slowly return to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.

name: Band-assisted Pull-up
description: The band-assisted pull-up is a pull-up variation where an elastic band is looped around the feet or knees to reduce effective bodyweight at the bottom of the movement and provide assistance through the hardest range of motion. It targets the upper back and lats while also engaging the biceps and core, making it an effective progression tool for building strength toward unassisted pull-ups.
instructions: Loop a resistance band around a pull-up bar and secure it under your feet or knees. Grasp the bar with hands shoulder-width apart and hang with arms straight. Pull yourself up, squeezing your lats and pushing your chest to the bar, then slowly lower back down to the starting position. Keep your core engaged throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Assisted Chin-Up
description: A band-assisted reverse-grip chin-up performed with palms facing toward the body and a resistance band looped around the feet or knees. The band reduces effective bodyweight at the bottom of the movement and provides less assistance toward the top, allowing beginners to train the chin-up pattern while building strength in the upper back, lats, and biceps with reduced load.
instructions: Loop a resistance band around your feet or knees and grasp the chin-up bar with a reverse grip, palms facing toward your body. Hang from the bar with your arms fully extended, then lift your body up until your chin clears the bar. Focus on squeezing your lats and pulling with your back muscles. Lower yourself back down slowly, stretching the band and returning to the starting position.

name: Single-Arm Band Low Row
description: A single-arm rowing exercise using a resistance band anchored low, focusing on the lats and mid-back while engaging the core for stability. Typically performed for moderate to high reps toward the end of a back or upper-body workout.
instructions: Anchor the resistance band low, holding one end in your hand. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, engaging your core for stability. Pull the band towards your side, keeping your elbow close to your body, until your hand reaches your hip. Return to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid jerking the band.

name: Latissimus Dorsi SMR
description: Latissimus dorsi self-myofascial release (SMR) uses a foam roller to apply slow, controlled pressure along the lats of the mid-to-upper back. Lying on your side with the roller positioned under the outer back below the shoulder, you roll small distances to locate tender spots and pause on them to encourage the tissue to relax. This drill is typically performed before or after upper-body training or lat stretches to help reduce tightness and improve shoulder and upper-back mobility.
instructions: Lie on your side with the foam roller under your outer back below the shoulder. Position the roller to target the latissimus dorsi muscle. Slowly roll the foam roller in small distances to find tender spots and pause on them for a few seconds to release tension. Breathe deeply and relax the muscle as you apply pressure.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Bent-Arm Barbell Pull-Over
description: The bent-arm barbell pull-over is a classic upper-body strength exercise that targets the chest and lats while also engaging the serratus and core for stability. Performed lying across or along a bench, you lower a barbell in an arc behind your head with bent elbows, then pull it back over your chest using controlled motion.
instructions: Lie on a bench with your feet planted firmly on the ground. Hold a barbell over your chest with bent elbows, then lower it in an arc behind your head. Pull the barbell back over your chest using controlled motion, squeezing your chest and lats. Return to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Barbell Pull-Over to Press
description: The barbell pull-over to press is a compound upper-body movement that combines a barbell pull-over with a barbell press. Starting lying on a bench, you lower the bar in an arc behind your head to work the lats and chest, then bring it back over the chest and press it up to emphasize the chest and triceps. It is typically performed with moderate to high reps (8–12+) as part of an upper-body workout.
instructions: Lie on a bench and grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width. Lower the bar in an arc behind your head, stretching your lats and chest. Bring the bar back over your chest and press it upwards, extending your arms fully. Return to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Single-arm Barbell Bent-over Row
description: The single-arm barbell bent-over row is a unilateral rowing exercise that targets the middle back and lats while also engaging the biceps and grip. Performed in a hinged position with one end of the barbell anchored, it helps build back strength, stability, and muscular balance between sides.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold one end of a barbell with one hand. Bend at the hips and knees, keeping back straight, and lean forward slightly. Lift the barbell to your side until your elbow is at a 90-degree angle, squeezing your back muscles. Lower the barbell back to the starting position and repeat on the other side.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Reverse Barbell Row
description: A barbell rowing variation performed with a reverse (underhand) grip to emphasize the lats and biceps while strengthening the upper back.
instructions: Hold a barbell with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Bend knees slightly and lean forward at the hips, keeping back straight. Lift the barbell up to your chest, squeezing your lats and pulling your elbows close to your body. Lower the barbell back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Side Kick-Through to Kettlebell High Pull
description: A dynamic combination movement that pairs a ground-based side kick-through with an explosive kettlebell high pull. It challenges full-body coordination, core stability, and power while targeting the traps, shoulders, hips, hamstrings, and glutes. Commonly used in circuit-style training for conditioning and fat loss.
instructions: Start in a plank position with a kettlebell on the ground beside you. Perform a side kick-through, rotating your hips and lifting one leg through to the other side. As you kick through, explosively pull the kettlebell up to chest height with both hands. Land softly on the balls of your feet and repeat on the other side.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Double-Kettlebell Standing Clean
description: From a standing position with a kettlebell in each hand, hinge at the hips and swing the bells back between your legs, then explosively extend your hips to drive the kettlebells up and catch them in the rack position at shoulder height. Lower under control and repeat for the desired reps.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in each hand. Hinge at the hips and swing the bells back between your legs. Explosively extend your hips to drive the kettlebells up, catching them in the rack position at shoulder height. Lower the kettlebells under control to the starting position and repeat.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: PJR Pull-Over
description: The PJR pull-over is a triceps-focused variation of the dumbbell pull-over performed on a flat or slightly inclined bench. The bottom position emphasizes a deep stretch on the long head of the triceps, while the top position transitions into a triceps press that recruits all three heads of the triceps. It is typically used in strength or hypertrophy programs in moderate to higher rep ranges.
instructions: Lie on a flat or slightly inclined bench with your feet planted on the floor. Hold a dumbbell over your chest with your arms extended and palms facing each other.
