Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, squeezing your shoulder muscles as you lift and lowering slowly to maintain control.

name: Seated Side Lateral Raise
description: A seated dumbbell exercise targeting the lateral (middle) head of the shoulders. Performed by sitting upright and raising dumbbells out to the sides until arms are roughly parallel to the floor, emphasizing controlled motion to build shoulder size and strength while reducing momentum and lower-body involvement.
instructions: Sit upright with dumbbells at your sides. Lift the dumbbells out to the sides until your arms are parallel to the floor. Keep your core engaged and control the movement to avoid swinging. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with a slow and deliberate motion.

name: Dumbbell Lying Rear Lateral Raise
description: An upper-body strength exercise performed lying face down while raising dumbbells out to the sides to target the rear deltoids and upper back postural muscles. Typically done with light weights for higher repetitions.
instructions: Lie face down on a flat bench with dumbbells in each hand. Hold the dumbbells with palms facing each other and arms extended below your shoulders. Lift the dumbbells out to the sides until they are at a 45-degree angle to your body, squeezing your rear deltoids. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position in a controlled manner.

name: Head-on-bench dumbbell rear delt raise
description: The head-on-bench dumbbell rear delt raise is an isolation exercise for the rear deltoids. Lying face-down with your head supported on a bench helps minimize body swing and momentum, allowing you to better target the posterior shoulder muscles with controlled, light dumbbells for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Lie face-down on a flat bench with your head supported. Hold light dumbbells with your arms extended and palms facing each other. Lift the dumbbells out to the sides until they are at shoulder level, squeezing your rear deltoids. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position in a controlled manner.

name: Cuban Press
description: The Cuban press is a shoulder-focused strength exercise that combines an upright row, external rotation, and overhead press to train the shoulders, rotator cuff, and upper back using relatively light weights, often as part of a dynamic warm-up.
instructions: Hold dumbbells at your sides with your palms facing your body. Perform an upright row by lifting the dumbbells to your sides until your elbows are at shoulder height. Rotate your wrists so your palms face forward, then press the dumbbells straight up over your head. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, reversing the movement to complete the exercise.

name: Reverse Flyes With External Rotation
description: Stand hinged slightly forward with a dumbbell in each hand, arms hanging down and palms facing each other. With a slight bend in the elbows, raise the arms out to the sides until they are in line with your shoulders, then externally rotate so your thumbs point upward or slightly back at the top. Control the motion as you return to the starting position, keeping your shoulders down and core engaged throughout.
instructions: Stand hinged forward with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing each other. Raise arms out to the sides with a slight bend in the elbows until they are in line with your shoulders. Externally rotate your arms so thumbs point upward at the top. Control the return to the starting position, keeping shoulders down and core engaged.

name: Single-Arm Side Lying Rear Fly
description: The single-arm side lying rear fly is an upper-body isolation exercise that targets the rear deltoid of the shoulder. Lying on a bench or flat surface minimizes body momentum, helping you focus on controlled lifting and lowering of the dumbbell. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8-12 reps per set, within an upper-body or shoulder-focused workout.
instructions: Lie on your side on a flat surface with your feet stacked and body in a straight line. Hold a dumbbell in the hand closest to the ceiling, with your arm extended and palm facing your body. Lift the dumbbell upwards, keeping your arm straight, and focus on squeezing your rear deltoid. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Single-arm Dumbbell Upright Row
description: The single-arm dumbbell upright row is a unilateral pulling exercise that targets the traps and shoulders. Performed standing, you pull a dumbbell vertically along the body to shoulder height, focusing on controlled motion and avoiding excessive shrugging. It is typically used with moderate to high reps, such as 8–12 per set, to build size and strength in the upper back and shoulders.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in one hand. Keep your back straight and core engaged, then lift the dumbbell vertically along your body to shoulder height. Focus on squeezing your shoulder blades and avoiding excessive shrugging. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position in a controlled motion, and repeat on the other side.

name: Dumbbell Scaption
description: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing your body. With a slight bend in your elbows and thumbs pointing up, raise the dumbbells in a V shape about 30–45 degrees in front of your body until arms reach shoulder height. Pause briefly, then lower under control. Keep your core braced and avoid shrugging your shoulders or swinging the weights.
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at your sides, palms facing your body. Raise the dumbbells in a V shape, keeping a slight bend in your elbows and thumbs up, to shoulder height. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the dumbbells under control. Keep your core braced and avoid shrugging your shoulders throughout the movement.

name: See-Saw Press (Alternating Side Press)
description: A standing dumbbell shoulder press where you alternate pressing one arm up while lowering the other, creating a see-saw motion that challenges shoulder strength and core stability.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells at shoulder height. Press one dumbbell straight up over your head. Lower the raised dumbbell back to the starting position while simultaneously pressing the other dumbbell up. Alternate arms with each rep, maintaining core stability throughout the exercise.

name: Dumbbell Incline Shoulder Raise
description: Lie back on an incline bench holding dumbbells at your sides with straight arms. With a slight bend in your elbows, raise the dumbbells forward and upward to shoulder height, focusing on lifting with your shoulders rather than your arms. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the weights under control to the starting position.
instructions: Lie back on an incline bench with dumbbells at your sides and straight arms. Raise the dumbbells forward and upward to shoulder height with a slight bend in your elbows. Focus on lifting with your shoulders, not your arms. Lower the weights under control to the starting position.

name: Single-arm Half-kneeling Shoulder Press
description: A single-arm dumbbell shoulder press performed from a half-kneeling position to target the deltoids while challenging core and hip stability. This variation is typically done with lighter loads than standing presses to emphasize strict form, control, and unilateral strength.
instructions: Start in a half-kneeling position with one knee on the ground and the other foot in front. Hold a dumbbell in the hand on the same side as the front foot. Press the dumbbell straight up over your shoulder, extending your arm fully. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Dumbbell Clean And Jerk
description: From a standing position holding dumbbells at your sides, hinge at the hips and slightly bend your knees. In one powerful motion, extend your hips and knees to drive the dumbbells up to shoulder level (clean), then dip and press them overhead to full lockout (jerk). Lower the dumbbells back to the shoulders and then to the starting position under control.
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at your sides. Hinge at the hips and bend your knees, then explosively extend to drive the dumbbells to shoulder level. Dip and press the dumbbells overhead to full lockout, then lower them back to the shoulders and down to the starting position under control. Keep your core tight and movement smooth throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Dumbbell Military Press
description: Press dumbbells overhead from shoulder height while seated or standing, keeping your core braced and wrists stacked over elbows to target the shoulders.
instructions: Sit or stand with dumbbells at shoulder height. Keep your core braced and wrists stacked over elbows. Press the dumbbells straight up over your head, extending your arms fully. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Dumbbell Fix Standing Dumbbell Arnold Press
description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing toward you. Brace your core, then press the dumbbells overhead while simultaneously rotating your palms to face forward at the top. Lower the weights back to the starting position by reversing the motion with control. Maintain a neutral spine and avoid leaning back as you press.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing you. Brace your core, then press the dumbbells overhead while rotating your palms to face forward. Lower the weights back to the starting position with control, reversing the motion. Maintain a neutral spine throughout the exercise.

name: Dumbbell Single-Arm Snatch
description: From a standing position, hinge at the hips to lower a dumbbell between your knees with one hand. In one explosive motion, drive through your legs and hips to pull the dumbbell upward close to your body, then punch it overhead to a locked-out position. Control the descent back to the start and repeat on the same or alternate arm.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell with one hand. Hinge at the hips to lower the dumbbell between your knees. Explosively drive through your legs and hips to pull the dumbbell upward, keeping it close to your body, then punch it overhead to a locked-out position. Control the descent back to the starting position and repeat on the same or alternate arm.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Dumbbell Hang Snatch
description: The dumbbell hang snatch is an explosive full-body power exercise performed from a standing hang position. Starting with the dumbbell held around mid-thigh, you hinge at the hips, then drive explosively through the hips and legs to propel the weight upward in one fluid motion, finishing with the arm locked out overhead. This variation emphasizes hip extension, upper-back and shoulder power, and core stability, and can be used for low-rep power work or higher-rep conditioning.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell at mid-thigh level. Hinge at the hips, keeping your back straight and core engaged. Explosively drive through your hips and legs to propel the dumbbell upward, finishing with your arm locked out overhead. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Surfer Sprawl
description: The surfer sprawl is an explosive, full-body bodyweight-style exercise similar to a burpee, but instead of jumping straight up, you transition from a plank into a low, staggered "surfer" stance, alternating lead sides each rep. It is typically performed for time or high reps in fat-loss or athletic conditioning circuits, emphasizing staying low to challenge the shoulders, core, and legs while building power and agility.
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands under shoulders and toes on ground. From the plank, kick one foot forward and the other back into a low, staggered stance, keeping your back straight and core engaged. Quickly return to the plank position and repeat on the other side, alternating lead feet with each rep. Keep your movements explosive and controlled, staying low to challenge your shoulders, core, and legs.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Dumbbell Clean and Press
description: A full-body compound exercise where you explosively clean the dumbbells from the floor or hang position to the shoulders, then press them overhead. It trains power, strength, and coordination through the hips, legs, back, shoulders, and arms.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells with palms facing your body. Explosively lift the dumbbells to your shoulders, keeping your back straight and core engaged. Press the dumbbells straight up over your head, extending your arms fully. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
description: A shoulder isolation exercise performed by raising dumbbells out to the sides to shoulder height, primarily targeting the middle head of the deltoids. Commonly used as accessory work on upper-body or shoulder-focused training days for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold dumbbells at your sides with palms facing your thighs. Raise the dumbbells out to the sides until they are at shoulder height, keeping your arms straight. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the entire range.

name: Dumbbell Front Raise and Press
description: A compound upper-body exercise that combines a front raise with an overhead press to target the shoulders while elevating heart rate, ideal for time-efficient upper-body or circuit training.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells at thigh level with palms facing forward. Raise the dumbbells to shoulder height, keeping arms straight, then press them overhead. Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height, then return to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout. Keep core engaged and avoid arching the back during the exercise.

name: Rotator Cuff Warm-Up
description: The rotator cuff warm-up is a shoulder external rotation exercise performed with a light dumbbell to prepare the shoulder joint for sports or training and to support rehab. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions (8–12+ per set) with controlled tempo and focus on smooth, pain-free motion.
instructions: Hold a light dumbbell in each hand with arms at sides and palms facing thighs. Bend elbows to 90 degrees, keeping upper arms still, and rotate hands outward. Focus on smooth movement, avoiding any pain or discomfort, and control the rotation back to the starting position. Repeat for 8-12 repetitions, maintaining a steady tempo.

name: Single-arm Dumbbell Arc Row
description: The single-arm dumbbell arc row is a strength exercise targeting the lats and upper back using an arcing pulling path to enhance mind-muscle connection and hit the back from a slightly different angle than a traditional row.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand with arm extended. Bend knees slightly and lean forward at the hips, keeping back straight. Pull the dumbbell upwards in an arcing motion, keeping elbow close to body, until elbow is at a 90-degree angle. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Incline Dumbbell Y Raise
description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench holding dumbbells with arms hanging straight down. With a slight bend in the elbows and thumbs pointing up, raise the arms in a Y shape at about a 45-degree angle from your head until they are in line with your body, focusing on squeezing the upper back and shoulders. Lower the weights under control and repeat.
instructions: Lie chest-down on an incline bench holding dumbbells with arms hanging straight down. Raise the arms in a Y shape at a 45-degree angle from your head, keeping a slight bend in the elbows and thumbs pointing up. Focus on squeezing the upper back and shoulders as you lift. Lower the weights under control to the starting position.

name: 6 Way Shoulder Raise
description: A multi-directional dumbbell raise that combines front, lateral, and overhead movements to target all heads of the shoulders and improve shoulder stability and control.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand. Raise dumbbells forward to shoulder height, then lower to starting position. Next, raise dumbbells out to sides until at shoulder height, and lower again. Finally, raise dumbbells straight up over head, then lower to complete the exercise.

name: Seated Dumbbell Lateral Raise
description: The seated dumbbell lateral raise is a shoulder isolation exercise that emphasizes the middle (lateral) head of the deltoids. Sitting on a bench reduces momentum and swinging, encouraging strict form and better muscle activation. Typically performed for moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or shoulder-focused workout.
instructions: Sit on a bench with feet on floor. Hold dumbbells at sides with palms facing thighs. Raise dumbbells out to sides until at shoulder height, keeping arms straight. Lower dumbbells back to starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: AM Dumbbell Side Raise
description: The dumbbell side (lateral) raise is an upper-body isolation exercise that targets the shoulder muscles, especially the lateral deltoids, to build width and strength. Performed by raising dumbbells out to the sides with controlled motion, it is commonly used as accessory work on upper-body strength days.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells at your sides. Lift the dumbbells out to the sides, keeping arms straight, until they are at shoulder level. Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position with control, avoiding jerky movements. Keep core muscles engaged throughout the exercise to maintain stability.

name: Incline Bench Dumbbell Reverse Fly
description: Lie chest-down on an incline bench holding dumbbells with arms hanging straight down. With a slight bend in the elbows, raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc until your upper arms are in line with your body, squeezing your upper back and rear shoulders, then lower under control.
instructions: Lie chest-down on an incline bench holding dumbbells with arms hanging straight down. Bend your elbows slightly and raise the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc. Lift until your upper arms are in line with your body, squeezing your upper back and rear shoulders. Lower the dumbbells under control to the starting position.

name: Dumbbell Halo
description: Hold a single dumbbell by the ends at chest height, brace your core, and slowly circle it around your head in a controlled halo motion, keeping your elbows close and torso stable. Reverse the direction each rep to evenly work the shoulders and upper back.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell by the ends at chest height. Brace your core and keep your elbows close to your body. Slowly circle the dumbbell around your head in a controlled motion, keeping your torso stable. Reverse the direction with each repetition to work your shoulders and upper back evenly.

name: Standing Dumbbell Scap Raise
description: Stand tall holding dumbbells at your sides with arms straight and shoulder blades relaxed. Without bending your elbows, shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears, focusing on squeezing the shoulder blades upward, then slowly lower back down under control. Keep your neck neutral and avoid rolling the shoulders forward or backward.
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells at your sides and arms straight. Keep your shoulder blades relaxed and your neck neutral. Shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears, squeezing your shoulder blades together, then slowly lower back down under control. Avoid bending your elbows or rolling your shoulders forward or backward.

name: Holman Overhead Press
description: A dumbbell overhead press variation emphasizing controlled shoulder pressing while maintaining a stable core and neutral spine. Performed standing or seated, it targets the deltoids with strict form and reduced momentum.
instructions: Stand or sit with feet shoulder-width apart and core engaged. Hold dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing forward. Press dumbbells straight up over your head, keeping your core stable and spine neutral. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Holman Slow Weighted Half Jack to Overhead Press
description: Stand tall holding dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing in. Step one foot out to the side into a half jumping-jack stance while simultaneously pressing the dumbbells overhead in a controlled motion. Step the foot back to the starting position as you lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height. Alternate sides each rep, moving slowly and maintaining core tension to stabilize the shoulders and torso.
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells at shoulder height and palms facing in. Step one foot out to the side into a half jumping-jack stance while pressing the dumbbells overhead in a controlled motion. Step the foot back to the starting position as you lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height. Alternate sides slowly, maintaining core tension to stabilize the shoulders and torso.

name: Holman Slow Arnold Press
description: A controlled variation of the Arnold press where you rotate the dumbbells from a palms-in position at shoulder height to a palms-out position overhead, emphasizing time under tension for the shoulders.
instructions: Hold dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing your body. Press the dumbbells upwards, rotating your wrists so palms face forward at the top of the movement. Lower the dumbbells back down to the starting position, taking your time to control the movement and emphasize tension in your shoulders. Focus on slow and deliberate movements throughout the entire range of motion.

name: Holman Boat Arnold Press with Feet Push-Out
description: Sit in a boat pose position holding dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing toward you. Engage your core, lean back slightly, and lift your feet off the floor, extending them forward into a push-out position. As you maintain balance, rotate your palms outward and press the dumbbells overhead in an Arnold press motion. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position while keeping your core tight and feet extended or hovering, then repeat for reps.
instructions: Sit in a boat pose position holding dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing you. Engage your core and lift your feet off the floor, extending them forward. Rotate your palms outward and press the dumbbells overhead, then lower them back to the starting position while keeping your core tight and feet extended. Repeat for reps, maintaining balance and control throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Dumbbell Hip Drive to Overhead Press
description: A full-body power movement where you use a hip drive to generate momentum and transition into an overhead dumbbell press, emphasizing shoulders and leg drive.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells at sides. Explosively drive hips forward to generate momentum, keeping back straight and core engaged. As hips extend, transition into an overhead press, extending arms and squeezing shoulders. Lower dumbbells back to starting position, controlling the movement to maintain balance and stability.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Squat Thrust to Alternating Renegade Triceps Press
description: From a standing position, squat down and place the dumbbells on the floor, then jump or step your feet back into a high plank with hands on the dumbbells. In the plank, perform an alternating triceps press by bending one elbow to lower that side toward the floor while keeping the other arm straight, then press back up and repeat on the opposite side. After the press, jump or step your feet back toward your hands and stand up to complete the squat thrust, then repeat for reps.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold dumbbells. Squat down, placing dumbbells on the floor, then jump or step back into a high plank position with hands on dumbbells. Perform an alternating triceps press by bending one elbow, lowering, then pressing back up and repeating on the opposite side. Jump or step feet back toward hands and stand up to complete the exercise, repeating for reps.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Cheer Press
description: A shoulder-focused dumbbell press variation where you press the weights overhead in a controlled, cheer-style motion, emphasizing deltoid strength and stability.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells at shoulder height. Press the dumbbells straight up over your head in a controlled, cheer-style motion. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, keeping control throughout the entire range of motion. Squeeze your deltoids at the top of the movement to emphasize shoulder strength and stability.

name: Holman Left Arm Press with Right Arm Hold
description: From a standing or seated position, hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward. Press the left arm straight overhead while keeping the right arm held in a static position at shoulder height. Lower the left arm back to the starting position under control while maintaining the right arm hold. Complete all reps on the left side, then switch sides so the right arm presses while the left arm holds. Focus on keeping your core braced and torso upright throughout.
instructions: Hold dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing forward. Press your left arm straight overhead, keeping your right arm still. Lower your left arm back to the starting position under control. Switch arms after completing all reps to work the right side.

name: Holman Right Arm Press with Left Arm Hold
description: Perform a single-arm dumbbell shoulder press with the right arm while holding a static 90-degree elbow bend with the left arm. Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height, palms facing forward. Keep your core braced and glutes tight. Press the right dumbbell overhead until the arm is fully extended without shrugging the shoulder, then lower it back to shoulder height under control. At the same time, keep the left arm held in a fixed, bent position at shoulder height throughout the set. Complete all reps on the right side, then switch sides if desired.
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing forward. Hold the left arm bent at a 90-degree angle, keeping it still throughout the exercise. Press the right dumbbell overhead, extending your arm without shrugging your shoulder. Lower the right dumbbell back to shoulder height under control, then repeat for desired reps before switching sides.

name: Holman Left Front Raise with Right Hold
description: Stand holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms down at your sides. Raise the left arm straight in front of you to shoulder height with a slight bend in the elbow and hold the right dumbbell at your side or in a static raised position, depending on variation. Control the movement, pause briefly at the top, then lower the left arm slowly while maintaining tension in the right side. Repeat for reps, then switch sides if desired.
instructions: Stand with dumbbells at your sides. Raise your left arm straight in front of you to shoulder height with a slight elbow bend. Pause briefly at the top, then lower your left arm slowly while keeping your right arm still. Repeat for reps, then switch arms if desired.

name: Holman Right Front Raise with Left Hold
description: Perform a front raise with the right arm while holding the left arm in an isometric front raise position. Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand, arms down in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Raise both arms to shoulder height, then keep the left arm held at shoulder level while you lower and repeatedly raise the right arm under control. Maintain a slight bend in the elbows, keep your core braced, and avoid swinging or leaning back. After completing reps on the right side, switch sides so the right arm holds while the left arm performs the raises.
instructions: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand, arms down in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Raise both arms to shoulder height, then hold the left arm in place. Lower and raise the right arm to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in the elbow and core braced. Switch arms after completing reps to work the left side.

name: Holman Alternating Front Raise to Side Raise
description: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms down at your sides and palms facing your thighs. Keeping a slight bend in your elbows and your core braced, raise one arm straight in front of you to shoulder height, then lower it under control. Next, raise the opposite arm out to the side to shoulder height, then lower it. Continue alternating between front raises and side raises, maintaining controlled tempo and avoiding swinging or leaning back.
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells at your sides and palms facing your thighs. Raise one arm straight in front of you to shoulder height, then lower it. Alternate with the opposite arm, raising it out to the side to shoulder height, then lower it. Continue alternating front and side raises, keeping a controlled tempo and braced core.

name: Holman Heavy Right Arm Overhead Press
description: A single-arm dumbbell overhead press performed with the right arm to build shoulder strength and stability. Press the dumbbell from shoulder height to full extension overhead while keeping the core braced and torso upright.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a dumbbell at shoulder height with your right hand, palm facing forward. Press the dumbbell straight up over your head, extending your arm fully while keeping your core braced and torso upright. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Holman Heavy Left Arm Overhead Press
description: A single-arm dumbbell overhead press performed with the left arm to build unilateral shoulder strength and stability. Press the dumbbell from shoulder height to full extension overhead while keeping the core braced and torso upright.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a dumbbell in your left hand at shoulder height with your palm facing forward. Press the dumbbell straight up over your head, extending your arm fully while keeping your core braced and torso upright. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Holman Right Arm Heavy Overhead Press
description: A single-arm dumbbell overhead press performed standing, focusing on driving a heavy weight straight overhead with the right arm while maintaining a braced core and neutral spine.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in your right hand. Engage your core and maintain a neutral spine. Press the dumbbell straight overhead with your right arm, extending your elbow fully. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Right Diagonal Lunge to Overhead Press
description: From a standing position holding a dumbbell in your right hand at shoulder height, step your right foot diagonally forward to the right into a lunge while keeping your chest tall and core braced. As you drive through your front heel to return toward standing, press the dumbbell overhead in a smooth motion. Lower the weight back to shoulder height as you step into the next diagonal lunge, repeating for the desired reps on the right side.
instructions: Stand with feet together, holding a dumbbell in your right hand at shoulder height. Step your right foot diagonally forward into a lunge, keeping your chest tall and core braced. As you drive through your front heel to return to standing, press the dumbbell overhead in a smooth motion. Lower the weight back to shoulder height and repeat for desired reps.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Left Arm Heavy Overhead Press
description: A single-arm dumbbell overhead press performed with the left arm to build shoulder strength and stability. Press the dumbbell from shoulder height to full extension overhead while keeping the core braced and torso upright.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a dumbbell in your left hand at shoulder height. Press the dumbbell straight up over your head, extending your arm fully and keeping your core braced. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Weighted Palms-In Overhead Press
description: Stand holding dumbbells at shoulder height with your palms facing in toward your head. Brace your core, keep your chest tall, and press the weights straight overhead until your arms are fully extended without locking out your elbows. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height under control, maintaining the palms-in (neutral) grip throughout the movement.
instructions: Stand holding dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing in. Brace your core and keep your chest tall. Press the weights straight overhead until arms are fully extended, avoiding elbow lockout. Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height under control, maintaining the palms-in grip.

name: Dumbbell Racked Carry
description: The dumbbell racked carry involves walking for time or distance while holding a pair of dumbbells in a front-racked position near the shoulders. This exercise challenges the shoulders and core, reinforces an upright posture, and can be used as a conditioning finisher or as accessory work to heavy lifts like the deadlift.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing your body. Keep your shoulders down and away from your ears, engaging your core for stability. Walk forward with a upright posture, taking small steps and avoiding swaying from side to side. Maintain the racked position and controlled breathing throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Dumbbell Clean and Push-Press
description: The dumbbell clean and push-press is a full-body compound movement that combines a powerful lower-body clean with an overhead push-press. The clean phase drives the weight up using the hips, legs, glutes, back, and biceps, while the push-press uses leg drive to assist the shoulders and triceps through the overhead portion. It can be performed for lower reps to build strength or higher reps for muscle building and metabolic conditioning.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells at thighs. Hinge hips and knees to lower body, then explosively drive through heels to clean dumbbells up to shoulder height. Receive dumbbells in a partial squat, then immediately push-press them overhead, using leg drive to assist shoulders and triceps. Lower dumbbells back to starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Total Fitness Dumbbell Farmer's Carry
description: The dumbbell farmer's carry involves walking while holding heavy dumbbells at your sides, building full-body strength, grip, and core stability. It is often used as a finisher, in fat-loss circuits, or as an accessory to heavy lifts like the deadlift.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Single-Arm Dumbbell Racked Carry
description: Carry a single dumbbell in a front-racked position at shoulder height while walking for time or distance. Keep the torso upright, core braced, and shoulder packed down and back to resist leaning or rotation. This exercise builds unilateral shoulder and core stability while challenging grip and overall conditioning.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell at shoulder height with one arm, resting it on your forearm. Keep your torso upright and core braced to maintain balance. Walk forward while keeping the dumbbell stable and your shoulder packed down, avoiding leaning or rotation. Control your pace and breathing to complete the desired distance or time.

name: Jordan Shallow Dumbbell Pull-Over
description: Lie on a bench holding a dumbbell with both hands above your chest, arms slightly bent. Keeping your core braced and elbows softly bent, slowly lower the dumbbell in an arc behind your head until you feel a stretch through your lats and shoulders, then pull it back over your chest using your upper back and shoulder muscles. Maintain control and avoid arching your lower back throughout the movement.
instructions: Lie on a bench holding a dumbbell with both hands above your chest, arms slightly bent. Lower the dumbbell in an arc behind your head, keeping your core braced and elbows softly bent, until you feel a stretch through your lats and shoulders. Pull the dumbbell back over your chest using your upper back and shoulder muscles. Maintain control and avoid arching your lower back throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Dumbbell Shadow Boxing
description: Dumbbell shadow boxing is an intermediate conditioning exercise where you perform boxing-style punches while holding light dumbbells. It increases shoulder endurance and challenges the cardiovascular system through continuous, controlled punching combinations.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding light dumbbells in each hand. Throw controlled boxing-style punches, such as jabs and hooks, in a continuous motion. Keep your arms relaxed and focus on quick, precise punches, rotating your torso for added power. Breathe naturally and maintain a steady pace for the desired duration.

name: 30 Shoulders Alternating Dumbbell Front Raise
description: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms down in front of your thighs, palms facing your body. Keeping your core braced and a slight bend in your elbows, raise one dumbbell straight up in front of you to about shoulder height without swinging your torso. Pause briefly, then lower it under control as you simultaneously begin raising the opposite arm. Continue alternating sides for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining controlled movement and avoiding excessive momentum or leaning back.
instructions: Stand tall with dumbbells in each hand, palms facing your body. Raise one dumbbell straight up to shoulder height, keeping your core braced and a slight bend in your elbow. Lower the dumbbell under control as you raise the opposite arm, alternating sides. Continue alternating for the desired number of repetitions, maintaining controlled movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Bent-Over Dumbbell Reverse Fly
description: Stand hinged at the hips with a flat back, holding dumbbells at arm’s length under your shoulders. With a slight bend in your elbows, raise the dumbbells out to the sides until they reach shoulder height, squeezing your shoulder blades together, then lower under control.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, hinged at the hips with a flat back. Hold dumbbells at arm's length under your shoulders with a slight bend in your elbows. Raise the dumbbells out to the sides until they reach shoulder height, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Lower the dumbbells under control to the starting position.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Dumbbell Shrug
description: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with arms straight. Keeping your arms relaxed, elevate your shoulders straight up toward your ears in a shrugging motion, pause briefly at the top, then lower your shoulders back down under control. Avoid rolling the shoulders and keep your neck neutral throughout.

name: Incline Face-Down Dumbbell Reverse Fly
description: Lie face down on an incline bench holding dumbbells with arms hanging straight down. With a slight bend in the elbows, raise the dumbbells out to the sides until your arms are in line with your shoulders, squeezing the shoulder blades together, then lower under control.
instructions: Lie face down on an incline bench with dumbbells in hand and arms hanging straight down. Raise the dumbbells out to the sides with a slight bend in the elbows until your arms are in line with your shoulders. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top of the movement. Lower the dumbbells back down under control to the starting position.

name: Seated Dumbbell Front Raise
description: Sit upright on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms down in front of your thighs with palms facing your body. Keeping your torso still and core braced, raise the dumbbells straight up in front of you to shoulder height, pausing briefly at the top. Lower the weights back down under control and repeat for the desired reps, avoiding swinging or using momentum.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms down in front of your thighs. Raise the dumbbells straight up in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your torso still and core braced. Lower the weights back down under control, avoiding swinging or using momentum. Repeat for the desired reps, maintaining proper form throughout the exercise.

name: Neutral-Grip Arnold Press
description: Seated or standing dumbbell shoulder press variation starting with palms facing each other at shoulder height, then rotating the wrists outward as you press overhead to target the deltoids through a longer range of motion.
instructions: Sit or stand with dumbbells at shoulder height, palms facing each other. Press the dumbbells straight up, rotating your wrists outward as you extend your arms. Keep your core engaged and control the movement throughout the entire range of motion. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, rotating your wrists back to the neutral grip.

name: MetaBurn 3-Way Shoulder Raise
description: A dumbbell shoulder exercise that combines front, lateral, and rear raises in sequence to target all heads of the shoulders. Performed standing with controlled motion, it challenges shoulder strength and muscular endurance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells at your sides. Raise the dumbbells forward to shoulder height, then lower them back down. Next, raise the dumbbells out to the sides to shoulder height, and lower them again. Finally, raise the dumbbells behind you to shoulder height, squeezing your shoulder blades together, and lower them to the starting position.

name: MetaBurn Dumbbell Overhead Press
description: Press dumbbells overhead from shoulder height while standing or seated, keeping your core braced and wrists stacked over elbows to target the shoulders.
instructions: Stand or sit with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold dumbbells at shoulder height with wrists stacked over elbows. Press dumbbells straight up over your head, keeping core braced and control throughout the movement. Lower dumbbells back to starting position, squeezing shoulders at the top of the movement.

name: MetaBurn Single-Leg Arnold Press
description: Stand on one leg holding dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing toward you. As you press the weights overhead, rotate your palms to face forward while maintaining balance on the single leg. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control, rotating the palms back toward you. Repeat for reps, then switch legs.
instructions: Stand on one leg, holding dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing you. Press the dumbbells overhead, rotating palms to face forward while maintaining balance. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control, rotating palms back toward you. Switch legs after completing the desired number of reps.

name: RG Forward-Leaning Dumbbell Upright Row
description: A shoulder-focused upright row performed while leaning slightly forward, using dumbbells to emphasize the lateral deltoids and upper back while reducing lower back involvement compared to a fully upright stance.
instructions: Hold dumbbells with palms facing body. Lean forward slightly at hips, keeping back straight and core engaged. Lift dumbbells to sides until elbows are at shoulder height, squeezing shoulder blades together. Lower dumbbells to starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: RG Alternating Seated Dumbbell Lateral Raise
description: Sit upright on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, palms facing inward. Keeping your torso still and a slight bend in your elbows, raise one dumbbell out to the side until your arm is roughly parallel to the floor, focusing on lifting with the shoulder rather than shrugging. Lower under control, then repeat on the opposite side, alternating arms for the desired number of repetitions while maintaining a stable, seated posture.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing inward. Raise one dumbbell out to the side until your arm is parallel to the floor, keeping a slight bend in your elbow. Lower the dumbbell under control, then repeat on the opposite side, alternating arms. Maintain a stable posture throughout the exercise.

name: Single-Dumbbell Halo
description: Hold a single dumbbell vertically by one end at chest height, then slowly circle it around your head in a controlled halo motion, keeping your core braced and shoulders stable before reversing the direction.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell vertically by one end at chest height. Keep your core braced and shoulders stable throughout the exercise. Slowly circle the dumbbell around your head in a controlled halo motion, first in one direction. Reverse the direction to complete the exercise, maintaining control throughout the movement.

name: Car Driver
description: The car driver is a shoulder exercise where you hold a weight in front of you with both hands and rotate it side to side like turning a steering wheel. It primarily targets the front (anterior) shoulders while also engaging the core and upper back for stability. It is often used to add variety to shoulder workouts or as part of an upper-body or full-body circuit.
instructions: Hold a weight with both hands in front of you at chest level. Rotate the weight from side to side, mimicking a steering wheel motion. Keep your core and upper back muscles engaged for stability. Maintain control throughout the movement, avoiding jerky or bouncy rotations.

name: Front Plate Raise
description: The front plate raise is a shoulder exercise where you hold a weight plate with both hands and lift it straight out in front of your body to shoulder height, emphasizing the front (anterior) deltoids and helping build shoulder strength and endurance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a weight plate with both hands at thigh level, palms facing your body. Lift the plate straight out in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your arms straight. Lower the plate back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Battle Ropes
description: Battle ropes involve explosively moving heavy ropes in alternating or simultaneous waves while the opposite end is anchored to the ground. This exercise can be used for short, powerful bursts or longer intervals to build cardiovascular conditioning, shoulder endurance, and full-body work capacity.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding one end of the battle ropes in each hand. Anchor the other end of the ropes to a stable object on the ground. Move the ropes up and down in alternating waves, keeping your core engaged and arms relaxed. Explosively switch between waves, using your entire body to generate power and momentum.

name: Circus Bell
description: A strongman-style overhead press where a large, offset-weight dumbbell is cleaned to the shoulder and pressed overhead to build unilateral shoulder strength and stability.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the dumbbell with both hands and clean it to your shoulder, keeping your core engaged. Press the dumbbell overhead, extending your arm fully and keeping your core stable. Lower the dumbbell back to your shoulder, controlling the movement throughout the entire range.

name: Landmine Linear Jammer
description: An explosive single-arm pressing exercise using a barbell anchored in a landmine setup. The lifter drives the bar upward in a powerful pressing motion, then controls and catches it with the opposite hand, alternating sides. This movement primarily targets the shoulders while also engaging the upper back, triceps, and core for stability and power development.
instructions: Stand facing the landmine with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the barbell with one hand and drive it upward in a powerful pressing motion. Control the bar as it comes back down and catch it with your opposite hand, alternating sides with each rep. Keep your core engaged and back straight throughout the exercise.

name: Crucifix Hold
description: Stand tall with arms extended straight out to the sides at shoulder height, holding a weight in each hand. Maintain a slight bend in the elbows and keep the shoulders down and braced while resisting the pull of gravity for a set time. Focus on keeping the torso upright and avoiding shrugging the shoulders toward the ears.
instructions: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart. Extend arms straight out to the sides at shoulder height, holding a weight in each hand with a slight bend in the elbows. Keep shoulders down and braced, avoiding shrugging towards the ears, and maintain a upright torso position. Hold for the specified time, resisting the pull of gravity on the weights.

name: Bar Shoulder Extension Stretch
description: A mobility drill where you hold onto a fixed bar behind you and gently extend your shoulders to open the chest and improve overhead mobility and thoracic extension. Commonly used as part of a warm-up before upper-body training.
instructions: Stand with a fixed bar behind you at shoulder height. Hold the bar with both hands and lean back slightly, keeping your arms straight. Gently extend your shoulders, opening your chest and stretching your upper back. Keep the movement slow and controlled, holding for a few seconds before releasing.

name: Chair Upper Body Stretch
description: Seated stretch performed on a chair to gently open the shoulders and upper back by reaching the arms forward or overhead while maintaining an upright posture.
instructions: Sit in a chair with feet on floor and back straight. Reach arms forward and up, keeping them straight, to stretch shoulders and upper back. Hold for a few seconds, feeling the stretch, and then slowly return to starting position. Keep posture upright throughout the stretch.

name: Sled Reverse Flye
description: Stand in an athletic stance holding the sled handles or straps with arms extended in front of you. With a slight bend in the elbows, pull your arms out and back in an arc, squeezing your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement. Control the return to the start position and repeat, keeping your torso stable and avoiding shrugging your shoulders.
instructions: Stand in an athletic stance holding the sled handles or straps with arms extended. Pull your arms out and back in an arc with a slight bend in the elbows. Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the end of the movement. Control the return to the start position, keeping your torso stable and avoiding shoulder shrugs.

name: Plate Shoulder Circle Big to Small
description: A dynamic warm-up exercise where you hold a weight plate with straight arms and draw large, controlled circles that gradually become smaller, targeting the shoulder complex and rotator cuff muscles.
instructions: Hold a weight plate with straight arms at shoulder height. Draw a large circle with the plate, keeping your arms straight and using your shoulders to control the movement. Gradually decrease the circle size as you continue, maintaining control and focus on your shoulder muscles. Keep your core engaged and repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Sled Overhead Backward Walk
description: Walk backward while holding resistance overhead to strengthen the shoulders and upper body while engaging the core and lower body for stability and control.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding the sled handle overhead with both hands. Walk backward slowly, keeping the handle overhead and engaging your core for stability. Keep your shoulders down and away from your ears, maintaining control throughout the movement. Take small steps to focus on balance and strength.

name: Log Lift
description: A strongman-style overhead press where a large cylindrical log is cleaned from the floor to the chest and then pressed overhead to full arm extension.
instructions: Stand over the log with feet shoulder-width apart. Bend down and grasp the log with hands shoulder-width apart, keeping back straight and core engaged. Lift the log to chest level, then press it straight up over your head to full arm extension. Lower the log back to the starting position, keeping control throughout the entire movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Standing Suspended Rear Delt Fly
description: A bodyweight rear delt fly performed standing with a suspension trainer or rings. Lean back while holding the straps, then open your arms out to the sides to bring your body upright, focusing on squeezing the rear shoulders and upper back. Used for upper-body warm-ups or higher-rep shoulder and upper-back work.
instructions: Stand facing the suspension trainer with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the straps with arms extended and lean back, keeping a straight line from head to heels. Open your arms out to the sides, squeezing your rear shoulders and upper back, to bring your body upright. Control the movement as you return to the starting position.

name: Waiter's Carry
description: The waiter's carry is a loaded carry where a weight is held overhead in one hand while walking, challenging the core, shoulders, and upper back. It can be performed for time or distance to build shoulder stability, grip strength, and overall core control.

name: Towel Dumbbell Front Raise
description: The towel dumbbell front raise is a shoulder isolation exercise where a dumbbell is suspended from a towel held with both hands, then raised to shoulder height in front of the body. The towel grip increases the stability and grip challenge while targeting the front of the shoulders, and it can be used in upper-body or full-body strength circuits.
instructions: Hold a towel with both hands, with a dumbbell suspended from the center of the towel. Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, keeping your core engaged and back straight. Raise the dumbbell to shoulder height in front of you, keeping your arms straight and using your shoulder muscles to control the movement. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the exercise.

name: Reach-Roll-Lift Combo
description: The reach-roll-lift combo is a shoulder and upper-back mobility drill that improves thoracic extension, scapular control, and overhead range of motion. It is commonly used as part of a dynamic warm-up for upper-body workouts or as postural training throughout the day.
instructions: Start on hands and knees. Reach forward with one arm, keeping it straight, and lower your forehead to the ground. Roll onto your side, lifting your reached arm up and over your body, then lift your torso and arm towards the ceiling. Repeat on the other side, alternating arms to maintain balance and control.

name: Plate Lateral Raise
description: The plate lateral raise is a shoulder isolation exercise that targets the side (lateral) deltoids. Holding light weight plates at your sides, you raise your arms out to shoulder height with a slight bend in the elbows, then lower under control. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions in shoulder or upper-body workouts to build width and definition in the shoulders.
instructions: Hold light weight plates at your sides with a slight bend in the elbows. Raise your arms out to shoulder height, keeping the elbows slightly bent. Lower the plates back down to the starting position under control. Maintain a slow and steady tempo to target the lateral deltoids effectively.

name: Bent-over rear delt raise
description: The bent-over rear delt raise is an isolation exercise that targets the rear (posterior) deltoids and upper back. Performed bent at the hips with a neutral spine, you raise light weights out to the sides in a reverse fly motion. It is commonly used as a warm-up or high-rep finisher on shoulder or back days to improve shoulder balance and posture.
instructions: Bend at the hips with a neutral spine, keeping back straight. Hold light weights with arms extended and palms facing your body. Lift the weights out to the sides in a reverse fly motion, squeezing your rear deltoids. Lower the weights back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Single-arm Landmine Press
description: The single-arm landmine press is an angled pressing exercise performed with one end of a barbell anchored to the floor. Pressing the bar along its arc targets the shoulders while also engaging the upper chest and core for stability. It is typically performed for moderate to higher reps to build strength and muscle in the shoulders.
instructions: Stand facing the landmine with your feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the end of the barbell with one hand, keeping your arm at a 45-degree angle to your body. Press the bar away from you, extending your arm and squeezing your shoulder, then lower it back to the starting position under control. Alternate arms with each rep to maintain balance and engage your core.

name: Plate Front Raise
description: The plate front raise is a shoulder isolation exercise where you hold a weight plate with both hands and lift it in front of your body to shoulder height, targeting the front of the shoulders and helping build strength and muscle definition.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a weight plate with both hands at shoulder height, palms facing your thighs. Lift the plate straight out in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your arms straight. Lower the plate back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Holman Slow Overhead Press
description: A controlled overhead pressing variation emphasizing slow tempo to increase time under tension in the shoulders. Performed standing or seated, you press the weight overhead with a deliberate, slow ascent and descent to build strength and stability.
instructions: Stand or sit with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold weight at shoulder height with palms facing forward. Press weight overhead with a slow and deliberate movement, taking 2-3 seconds to reach the top. Lower the weight back down to the starting position with the same slow tempo.

name: 30 Shoulders Plate Front Raise
description: Stand tall holding a weight plate with both hands in front of your thighs, arms extended but not locked. With controlled motion, raise the plate straight up in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your core braced and shoulders down away from your ears. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the plate back to the starting position under control. Avoid swinging or using momentum, and maintain a neutral spine throughout.
instructions: Stand tall holding a weight plate with both hands in front of your thighs. Raise the plate straight up in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your core braced and shoulders down. Lower the plate back to the starting position under control, avoiding swinging or momentum. Keep a neutral spine throughout the movement.

name: Single-arm landmine push-press
description: An explosive single-arm pressing exercise performed with an angled barbell, emphasizing the shoulders while also engaging the triceps, upper chest, and core for power and stability.
instructions: Stand facing the landmine with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the barbell with one hand, keeping your elbow close to your body. Press the barbell upwards, extending your arm and using your legs and core for power, then lower it back down to the starting position. Keep your core engaged and control the movement throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Single-arm cable front raise
description: The single-arm cable front raise is an isolation exercise targeting the anterior and medial deltoids, helping build shoulder size, strength, and symmetry by working each side independently. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions (8-12+ per set) as part of an upper-body or shoulder-focused workout.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the cable at shoulder height. Hold the cable handle in one hand with your arm extended and palm facing your thighs. Lift the handle forward and upward to shoulder height, keeping your arm straight, then lower it back to the starting position. Alternate arms to work both sides evenly.

name: Cable Internal Rotation
description: Stand sideways to a cable machine with the working arm closest to the pulley. Set the pulley at elbow height and attach a single handle. Grasp the handle with your elbow bent to 90 degrees and tucked into your side, forearm across your body. Rotate your forearm inward toward your abdomen while keeping your elbow pinned to your side, then slowly return to the starting position under control. Focus on smooth, controlled motion to target the rotator cuff and shoulder stabilizers.
instructions: Stand sideways to the cable machine with your working arm closest to the pulley. Set the pulley at elbow height and grasp the handle with your elbow bent at 90 degrees, tucked into your side. Rotate your forearm inward toward your abdomen, keeping your elbow pinned to your side. Return to the starting position slowly and under control.

name: Seated Face Pull
description: The seated face pull is a cable-based pulling exercise that targets the rear deltoids and upper back to improve shoulder health, posture, and stability. Performed seated, it emphasizes controlled, moderate-to-high rep sets and is often used in warm-ups or main shoulder sessions.
instructions: Sit at the cable machine with feet flat on the floor. Hold the rope attachment with palms facing each other and arms extended. Pull the rope towards your face, keeping elbows high and squeezing your rear deltoids. Return the rope to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Single-arm bent-over cable rear delt fly
description: The single-arm bent-over cable rear delt fly is an isolation exercise for the rear deltoids, performed in a bent-over position using a cable. It targets the shoulders one side at a time and is typically done for moderate to high reps (8-12+) in upper-body or shoulder-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the cable at shoulder height. Bend your knees slightly and lean forward at the hips, keeping your back straight. Hold the cable handle with one hand and lift it out to the side, keeping your arm straight, until your hand is at shoulder height. Lower the handle back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Seated Cable Shoulder Press
description: The seated cable shoulder press is a multijoint machine-based pressing exercise that targets the shoulder muscles and triceps, typically performed for moderate to higher rep ranges to build muscle and strength.
instructions: Sit at the cable machine with feet flat on floor. Grasp the cable handles at shoulder height with palms facing forward. Press the handles straight up over your head, extending your arms fully. Lower the handles back to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid jerking or swinging.

name: Standing Face Pull
description: The standing face pull is an isolation exercise performed with a rope attachment on a cable machine to target the rear deltoids, traps, and upper back while standing upright and pulling the rope toward the face.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with a rope attachment at chest height. Hold the rope with both hands and keep your arms straight. Pull the rope towards your face, keeping your elbows high and squeezing your rear deltoids and upper back. Return to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid jerking or swinging.

name: Standing Cable Rear Delt Row
description: The standing cable rear delt row is an upper-body pulling exercise that targets the rear deltoids while also engaging the traps, lats, and biceps. Performed standing with a cable machine, it is typically done for moderate to high repetitions (8–12+ per set) as part of a shoulder or upper-back workout.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the cable at shoulder height. Hold a handle in each hand with palms facing your body. Pull the cable towards your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together and targeting the rear deltoids. Return to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid jerking or swinging.

name: Standing Low-Pulley Deltoid Raise
description: A single-arm cable lateral raise that targets the medial deltoid while also engaging the traps and upper back. Performed standing with the cable set low, it allows focused work on each shoulder independently and is typically done for moderate to high repetitions in shoulder or upper-body workouts.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with the pulley at a low position. Hold the handle with one hand and keep your arm straight, with a slight bend in your elbow. Lift the handle out to the side until your arm is at shoulder height, keeping your core engaged and avoiding swing. Lower the handle back to the starting position and repeat with the other arm.

name: Standing Crossed-Cable Rear Delt Fly
description: The standing crossed-cable rear delt fly is an isolation exercise targeting the rear deltoids. Performed standing with cables set at about chest height, you cross the cables in front of your body and pull outward in a reverse fly motion to emphasize the rear shoulders. It is typically done for moderate to high reps (8–12+) in upper-body or shoulder-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with cables set at chest height. Cross the cables in front of your body, holding a handle in each hand. Pull the cables outward to the sides, keeping your arms straight, and squeeze your rear deltoids. Return to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid swinging.

name: Cable Shoulder Press
description: A vertical pressing exercise using a cable machine to target the shoulders with constant tension throughout the movement. Performed by pressing the handles overhead from about shoulder height while maintaining a stable core and controlled tempo.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the cable handles at shoulder height with palms facing forward. Press the handles straight up over your head, extending your arms fully, then lower them back to the starting position in a controlled motion. Keep your core stable and maintain a slow, steady tempo throughout the exercise.

name: Alternating Cable Shoulder Press
description: Press one handle overhead at a time using a cable machine, keeping your core braced and torso upright to build shoulder strength with continuous tension.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a handle in each hand at shoulder height, with palms facing forward. Press one handle straight up overhead, keeping your core braced and torso upright, then lower it back to the starting position and repeat with the other arm. Alternate arms with each press to maintain continuous tension.

name: Cable Seated Lateral Raise
description: Sit upright on a bench facing away from a low cable stack, holding a D-handle in each hand at your sides. With a slight bend in your elbows and your core braced, raise your arms out to the sides until they reach shoulder height, keeping your palms facing down and shoulders away from your ears. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the handles back to the starting position under control, maintaining tension on the cables throughout the movement.
instructions: Sit upright on a bench facing away from a low cable stack, holding a D-handle in each hand. Keep a slight bend in your elbows and engage your core. Raise your arms out to the sides until they reach shoulder height, with palms facing down and shoulders relaxed. Lower the handles back to the starting position under control, keeping tension on the cables.

name: Single-arm Lying Cable Front Raise
description: An isolation shoulder exercise performed lying on your back while raising a single cable handle in front of your body to target the front deltoids. Typically done for moderate to high reps to build shoulder strength and endurance.
instructions: Lie on your back with your knees bent and feet flat on the floor. Hold a cable handle in one hand with your arm extended and palm facing your thighs. Lift the handle forward and upward, keeping your arm straight, until it's at shoulder height. Lower the handle back to the starting position and repeat with the other arm.

name: Lying Cable Front Raise
description: The lying cable front raise is an isolation shoulder exercise emphasizing the front deltoids. Performed lying on your back on a bench or the floor, you raise the cable handle in an arc over your torso, focusing on controlled motion and time under tension. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions to create a strong burn and pump in the shoulders as part of a shoulder-focused workout.
instructions: Lie on a bench or the floor with your back flat. Hold a cable handle over your chest with arms extended. Raise the handle in an arc over your torso, keeping control throughout the movement. Lower the handle back to the starting position, focusing on slow and deliberate motion.

name: Lying Cable Lateral Raise
description: The lying cable lateral raise is an isolation shoulder exercise performed while lying on a bench or the floor, using a cable to lift the arm out to the side and emphasize the medial (middle) deltoid. It is typically used in muscle-building shoulder or upper-body workouts in moderate to high rep ranges.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench or the floor with a cable machine beside you. Hold a cable handle in each hand with arms extended and palms facing thighs. Lift the handles out to the sides until arms are at shoulder height, squeezing deltoids at the top. Lower the handles back to the starting position in a controlled manner.

name: Cable Seated Row
description: The cable seated row is a strength exercise targeting the upper back using a cable stack. Performed from a seated position, you pull the handle toward your torso to work the lats, traps, and other upper-back musculature, with secondary involvement from the biceps and rear shoulders. It is commonly used in muscle-building programs or as an accessory movement in strength routines.
instructions: Sit at the cable machine with feet on the floor. Hold the handle with both hands, keeping knees slightly bent and back straight. Pull the handle toward your chest, squeezing your shoulder blades together and keeping elbows close to your body. Return to the starting position, controlling the release of the handle.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Single-arm cable lateral raise
description: The single-arm cable lateral raise is an isolation exercise targeting the medial deltoid of the shoulder. Using a cable allows constant tension through the range of motion and lets you focus on each side independently, typically performed for moderate to high reps in upper-body or shoulder-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the cable at shoulder height. Hold the cable handle with one hand, keeping your arm straight. Lift the cable out to the side until your arm is at shoulder height, then lower it back to the starting position. Repeat with the other arm, alternating sides to work each shoulder independently.

name: AA Cable Leaning Side Raise
description: Stand sideways to a cable machine, holding the handle with the outside hand. Lean your body away from the machine to increase the range of motion, then raise your arm out to the side until it reaches shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in the elbow. Control the weight back down to the starting position and repeat for the desired reps before switching sides.
instructions: Stand sideways to a cable machine, holding the handle with the outside hand. Lean your body away from the machine to increase range of motion. Raise your arm out to the side until it reaches shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in the elbow. Control the weight back down to the starting position to complete the rep.

name: 30 Shoulders Bent-Over Low Cable Reverse Fly
description: Stand facing away from a low cable pulley with a handle in each hand, hips hinged and torso bent forward. With a slight bend in your elbows and palms facing down or slightly inward, raise your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until they reach shoulder height, squeezing your rear delts and upper back. Control the cables back to the starting position without letting the weights slam, keeping your core braced and back flat throughout.
instructions: Stand facing away from a low cable pulley with a handle in each hand. Hinge your hips and bend your torso forward, keeping a slight bend in your elbows and palms facing down. Raise your arms out to the sides in a wide arc until they reach shoulder height, squeezing your rear delts and upper back. Control the cables back to the starting position, keeping your core braced and back flat throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Cable Rope Face Pull
description: Stand facing a cable machine with a rope attachment set at upper-chest or face height. Grasp the rope with both hands using an overhand or neutral grip, step back to create tension, and pull the rope toward your face, leading with your elbows and externally rotating the shoulders so your hands move to either side of your head. Squeeze your rear delts and upper back at the end of the movement, then slowly return to the starting position under control.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with the rope at upper-chest height. Grasp the rope with an overhand or neutral grip and step back to create tension. Pull the rope toward your face, leading with your elbows and rotating your shoulders, until your hands are beside your head. Slowly return to the starting position, squeezing your rear delts and upper back at the peak of the movement.

name: Cable Straight-Bar Front Raise
description: Stand facing a low cable pulley with a straight bar attached. With arms extended and a slight bend in the elbows, raise the bar in front of you to shoulder height, then lower under control to target the front of the shoulders.
instructions: Stand facing a low cable pulley with a straight bar attached. Hold the bar with arms extended and a slight bend in the elbows. Raise the bar in front of you to shoulder height, keeping control throughout the movement. Lower the bar back down to the starting position under control, targeting the front of the shoulders.

name: 30 Shoulders High Cable Reverse Fly
description: Stand facing away from a high cable attachment with the cables crossed in front of you, arms slightly bent. Keeping your elbows soft and chest lifted, pull the handles out and back in a wide arc until your hands are in line with your shoulders, squeezing the rear delts and upper back. Control the return to the start position without letting the weights slam.
instructions: Stand facing away from a high cable attachment with cables crossed in front of you. Keep your elbows soft and chest lifted. Pull the handles out and back in a wide arc until your hands are in line with your shoulders, squeezing your rear delts and upper back. Control the return to the start position without letting the weights slam.

name: 30 Shoulders Single-Arm Bent-Over Cable Reverse Fly
description: Stand in a staggered stance facing a cable machine with the pulley set just below shoulder height. Hinge at the hips to bend forward while keeping your back flat and core braced. Grasp the handle with one hand, arm slightly bent and across your body. Keeping your torso stable, pull the handle out and up to the side in a reverse fly motion until your hand is roughly in line with your shoulder. Pause briefly, then return under control and repeat for all reps before switching arms.
instructions: Stand in a staggered stance facing the cable machine with the pulley at shoulder height. Hinge at the hips to bend forward, keeping your back flat and core braced. Grasp the handle with one hand and pull it out and up to the side until your hand is in line with your shoulder. Return the handle under control to the starting position and repeat.

name: Single-Arm Cable Front Raise
description: Stand facing away from a cable machine with the pulley set low and a single handle attached. Hold the handle in one hand with your arm down in front of your thigh, palm facing your body. With a slight bend in the elbow and a braced core, raise your arm straight up in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your torso still and avoiding swinging. Pause briefly at the top, then lower the handle under control back to the starting position. Complete all reps on one side before switching arms.
instructions: Stand facing away from the cable machine with the handle in one hand. Hold your arm down in front of your thigh with a slight bend in the elbow. Raise your arm straight up in front of you to shoulder height, keeping your torso still. Lower the handle back to the starting position under control, then repeat on the other side.

name: Single-Arm Leaning Cable Lateral Raise
description: Stand sideways to a cable machine with the handle set at the lowest position. Hold the handle with the outside hand and lightly hold the machine with the inside hand, leaning your body away to create tension. With a slight bend in the elbow, raise your arm out to the side until it reaches shoulder height, keeping your torso still and focusing on the shoulder doing the work. Lower the handle under control and repeat for all reps before switching sides.
