instructions: Stand sideways to a cable machine with the handle at the lowest position. Hold the handle with your outside hand and lightly grasp the machine with your inside hand, leaning away to create tension. Raise your arm out to the side until it reaches shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in the elbow and your torso still. Lower the handle under control to the starting position.

name: RG 3-Way Single-Arm Rear Delt Cable Fly
description: A single-arm cable fly variation targeting the rear delts from three different angles to improve shoulder strength, stability, and posture.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the cable at chest height. Hold the cable handle with one hand and perform a fly, keeping your arm straight, targeting the rear delt from the side. Rotate the cable to shoulder height and repeat, then lower it to hip height for the third angle, squeezing your rear delt with each movement. Switch arms to complete the exercise on both sides.

name: RG Single-Arm Cross-Body Cable Front Raise
description: Stand sideways to a cable machine with the handle set at the lowest position. Grasp the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm across your body. With a slight bend in the elbow and stable core, raise the handle diagonally up and across your body to shoulder height, focusing on the front of the shoulder. Control the weight back down across your body to the start position and repeat before switching arms.
instructions: Stand sideways to the cable machine with the handle at the lowest position. Grasp the handle with the hand farthest from the machine, arm across your body. Raise the handle diagonally up and across your body to shoulder height, keeping a slight bend in the elbow. Lower the handle back down across your body to the start position, then repeat on the other side.

name: Smith Machine Shoulder Press
description: The Smith machine shoulder press is a machine-based overhead pressing exercise that targets the deltoid muscles. The fixed bar path of the Smith machine provides added stability compared to a free barbell, allowing you to focus on driving the weight overhead and controlling the eccentric phase. It can be used with lower reps for strength or moderate to high reps for muscle and strength endurance, commonly 8-12 reps per set, as part of a shoulder or upper-body workout.
instructions: Stand in the Smith machine with feet shoulder-width apart. Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width, then press the bar upwards extending your arms. Lower the bar back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout the entire range. Keep your core engaged and avoid arching your back throughout the exercise.

name: Reverse Machine Flyes
description: Reverse machine flyes target the rear deltoids and upper back by having you sit facing the machine, grasp the handles with arms slightly bent, and open the arms out and back in a wide arc. This isolates the rear shoulders and upper back with controlled, usually lighter loads for higher repetitions.
instructions: Sit facing the machine with feet on floor. Grasp the handles with arms slightly bent and palms facing each other. Open arms out and back in a wide arc, squeezing rear deltoids and upper back, then return to starting position. Keep movements controlled and smooth throughout the exercise.

name: Single-arm Smith machine upright row
description: The single-arm Smith machine upright row is an upper-body strength exercise that targets the traps and shoulders using a guided bar path. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or shoulder-focused workout.
instructions: Stand facing a Smith machine with the bar at chest height. Hold the bar with one hand, keeping your elbow close to your body. Lift the bar upwards, keeping it close to your body, until your elbow is at a 90-degree angle. Lower the bar back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Smith Machine Behind-the-Neck Press
description: The Smith machine behind-the-neck press is a shoulder-focused pressing exercise performed on a guided bar path. The fixed track of the Smith machine provides added stability compared to a free barbell, allowing you to focus on driving the bar upward with the deltoids. From a seated or standing position, the bar is lowered behind the head to about ear level, then pressed overhead. It is typically used with moderate to high reps, such as 8-12 per set, in shoulder or upper-body workouts to build muscle and strength endurance in the shoulders and upper back.
instructions: Sit or stand in the Smith machine with the bar at shoulder height. Lower the bar behind your head to about ear level, keeping your core engaged and back straight. Press the bar upwards, extending your arms fully, then control the return to the starting position. Keep your movements slow and deliberate to maintain proper form.

name: 30 Shoulders Leg Press Shoulder Press
description: A machine-based shoulder press variation performed from a seated position, focusing on pressing the weight overhead to target the shoulder muscles with controlled form.
instructions: Sit at the leg press machine with feet on the platform. Grasp the shoulder press handles at shoulder height. Press the weight straight up over your head, extending your arms fully. Lower the weight back down to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Pec-Deck Reverse Fly
description: Sit on the pec-deck machine facing the pads with the handles set behind you. Keeping a slight bend in your elbows and your chest firmly against the pad, pull the handles outward and back in a wide arc until your arms are in line with your shoulders. Squeeze your rear delts and upper back, then return under control without letting the weights slam.
instructions: Sit on the pec-deck machine with the handles behind you and a slight bend in your elbows. Keep your chest firmly against the pad and pull the handles outward and back in a wide arc. Stop when your arms are in line with your shoulders, squeezing your rear delts and upper back. Return the handles to the starting position under control.

name: Handstand Push-Up
description: The handstand push-up is an advanced bodyweight pressing exercise that primarily targets the shoulders, with assistance from the triceps, upper chest, and core to stabilize the inverted position.
instructions: Start in a handstand position against a wall for support. Kick up into a handstand with hands shoulder-width apart and fingers facing forward. Lower your body until the top of your head nearly touches the ground, then push back up to the starting position. Keep your core engaged and body straight throughout the exercise.

name: Upward Stretch
description: Stand tall and reach both arms overhead, extending through the shoulders and upper back while keeping the core engaged. Hold the stretch at the top, focusing on lengthening through the sides of the torso and shoulders, then gently release.
instructions: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart. Reach both arms overhead, extending through the shoulders and upper back. Hold the stretch at the top, focusing on lengthening through the sides of the torso and shoulders. Gently release to the starting position.

name: Shoulder Circles
description: Stand tall and slowly rotate your shoulders in a circular motion, bringing them up toward your ears, then back, down, and forward. Perform controlled circles in one direction, then reverse, focusing on smooth movement and staying within a comfortable range of motion.
instructions: Stand tall with good posture. Slowly rotate your shoulders in a circular motion, bringing them up, back, down, and forward. Perform controlled circles in one direction, then reverse, focusing on smooth movement. Keep movements within a comfortable range of motion.

name: Cross-body Shoulder Stretch
description: A flexibility exercise where one arm is brought across the chest and gently pulled toward the body with the opposite hand to stretch the shoulder muscles and surrounding tissues.
instructions: Hold your right arm straight out to the side at shoulder height. Cross your right arm over your chest toward your left shoulder, gently pulling it with your left hand. Keep your right elbow slightly bent and hold the stretch for a few seconds, feeling the stretch in your shoulder and surrounding muscles. Repeat on the other side with your left arm crossed over your chest.

name: Arm Circle
description: The arm circle is a bodyweight shoulder exercise commonly used in warm-ups. With arms extended out to the sides at shoulder height, you draw controlled circles in the air, gradually increasing size as needed. It targets the shoulder muscles, promotes blood flow, and prepares the upper body for more intense activity.
instructions: Hold arms straight out to the sides at shoulder height. Make small circles with hands for a few repetitions, gradually increasing circle size as needed. Keep movements controlled and smooth, focusing on shoulder rotation. Breathe naturally and continue for desired number of repetitions.

name: Seated Front Deltoid Stretch
description: Sit upright and gently pull one arm across the front of your body at shoulder height, using the opposite hand to apply light pressure just above the elbow. Hold the stretch while keeping your shoulders relaxed and chest lifted to target the front of the shoulders.
instructions: Sit upright with good posture. Gently pull one arm across the front of your body at shoulder height. Use your opposite hand to apply light pressure just above the elbow to deepen the stretch. Hold the stretch, keeping your shoulders relaxed and chest lifted.

name: Shoulder Raise Stretch
description: An intermediate shoulder mobility stretch performed by slowly raising the arms to shoulder height and overhead to improve shoulder flexibility and control.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Raise your arms out to the sides until they are at shoulder height, then continue upwards towards the ceiling. Keep your arms straight and control the movement as you lower your arms back down to the starting position. Breathe naturally and repeat the movement to improve shoulder flexibility.

name: Wall Walk
description: From a push-up position with feet against a wall, walk your hands backward toward the wall as your feet walk up, moving into a near-handstand position, then reverse the movement back to the starting push-up position. This advanced bodyweight drill challenges the entire body, especially the shoulders and core.
instructions: Start in a push-up position with feet against a wall. Walk hands backward toward the wall as feet walk up, keeping body straight. Continue until nearly in a handstand position, then slowly reverse the movement back to the starting push-up position. Keep core engaged and control the movement throughout.

name: Return Push from Stance
description: From an athletic stance, explosively push yourself backward or forward and then quickly return to the starting stance, focusing on rapid shoulder drive and body control. Maintain a tight core and soft knees to absorb impact and repeat for controlled, powerful reps.
instructions: Stand in an athletic stance with feet shoulder-width apart. Explosively push yourself backward or forward, focusing on rapid shoulder drive. Quickly return to the starting stance, maintaining a tight core and soft knees to absorb impact. Repeat for controlled, powerful reps.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Wall Shoulder Tap
description: The wall shoulder tap is a bodyweight shoulder stability exercise performed in a handstand position with the feet supported on a wall. From a stable handstand, one hand is lifted to tap the opposite shoulder, then returned to the floor, alternating sides for the duration of the set. It challenges shoulder strength, core stability, and balance in an inverted position.
instructions: Start in a handstand position with feet supported on a wall. Engage your core and maintain a straight body line. Lift one hand to tap the opposite shoulder, then return it to the ground, alternating sides. Keep movements controlled and focused to maintain balance and stability throughout the exercise.

name: Bear Crawl Shoulder Tap
description: The bear crawl shoulder tap is a static bear crawl hold where you alternately lift one hand to tap the opposite shoulder, challenging core stability, shoulder strength, and coordination while elevating heart rate.
instructions: Start in a bear crawl position with hands and feet on the ground. Engage your core and maintain a straight line from head to heels. Lift one hand to tap the opposite shoulder, then return to the starting position and repeat with the other hand. Alternate hands in a slow and controlled manner to challenge your core and shoulders.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Shadow Boxing
description: Shadow boxing is a fast-paced, non-contact boxing drill where you throw punches and move your feet in place or around a small area. It is typically performed for timed rounds to elevate heart rate, improve coordination, and build shoulder and upper-body endurance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hands up in a guard position. Throw quick jabs, hooks, and uppercuts, rotating your hips and moving your feet in place or around a small area. Keep your hands up and elbows close to your body, punching in a rhythmic and controlled manner. Move continuously for the timed round, resting briefly between rounds.

name: Bear Crawl Fire Feet
description: Bear crawl fire feet is a static bear crawl hold where you rapidly run your feet in place, creating a high-intensity, full-body cardio challenge that builds shoulder stability, core strength, and coordination.
instructions: Start in a bear crawl position with hands and feet on the ground. Engage your core and keep your shoulders stable. Rapidly run your feet in place, lifting them only a few inches off the ground. Maintain the position and continue running your feet for the desired duration.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Wall Sprawl
description: A dynamic bodyweight drill where you start on all fours facing a wall, kick your feet up to briefly contact or approach the wall in a partial handstand position, then quickly return to the starting position. It challenges the shoulders while demanding full-body coordination and cardiovascular endurance.
instructions: Start on all fours facing a wall. Kick your feet up towards the wall, briefly touching or approaching it in a partial handstand position. Quickly return to the starting position on all fours. Repeat the movement, focusing on explosive power and control throughout the exercise.

name: Bent Over Shoulder Y
description: The bent over shoulder Y targets the rear deltoids by raising the arms in a Y shape relative to the torso, emphasizing the back of the shoulders through a controlled range of motion.
instructions: Hold light dumbbells with arms straight. Bend over at hips, keeping back straight and core engaged. Raise arms in a Y shape, lifting dumbbells out to sides until they are at a 45-degree angle to your body. Lower arms back to starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Shoulder L
description: A bent-over shoulder external rotation that targets the rotator cuff and surrounding shoulder stabilizers. Performed as a controlled movement, often in a dynamic warm-up, using bodyweight or light resistance to improve shoulder stability and mobility.
instructions: Hold a light weight or use bodyweight with arms bent at 90 degrees. Bend over at the hips, keeping back straight and core engaged. Rotate arms outward, keeping elbows close to body, then return to starting position in a controlled movement. Keep movements slow and deliberate to target the rotator cuff and surrounding shoulder stabilizers.

name: Shoulder Dislocation Stretch with Dowel
description: Stand tall holding a dowel or similar stick with a wide overhand grip in front of your thighs. Keeping your elbows straight and core braced, slowly raise the dowel up and over your head, continuing the arc behind your body as far as comfortable without pain. Reverse the motion to return to the starting position. Move in a slow, controlled manner, focusing on opening the shoulders and chest while maintaining good posture. Adjust your grip width to reduce or increase the stretch intensity.
instructions: Stand tall holding a dowel with a wide overhand grip in front of your thighs. Raise the dowel up and over your head, keeping your elbows straight and core braced, then continue the arc behind your body as far as comfortable. Reverse the motion to return to the starting position, moving slowly and controlling the stretch. Adjust your grip width as needed to modify the intensity of the stretch.

name: PVC Shoulder Stretch
description: Stand tall holding a light dowel or PVC pipe with a wide overhand grip in front of your thighs. Keeping your elbows straight, slowly raise the pipe up and over your head, then as far behind you as comfortable without pain, opening the shoulders and chest. Pause briefly, then reverse the motion to return to the start. Move in a slow, controlled manner and adjust your grip width to reduce or increase stretch intensity.
instructions: Stand tall holding a light PVC pipe with a wide overhand grip in front of your thighs. Slowly raise the pipe up and over your head, then behind you as far as comfortable without pain. Keep your elbows straight and move in a slow, controlled manner. Pause briefly before reversing the motion to return to the starting position.

name: Shoulder T
description: A bent-over rear delt raise performed in a T shape to warm up and activate the rear shoulders using bodyweight or light resistance.
instructions: Hold a light weight or use bodyweight and stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Bend at the hips and knees, keeping back straight, and lean forward slightly. Lift arms out to sides until they are at a T shape, squeezing rear delts at the top. Lower arms back down to starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Kipping Clapping Pull-Up
description: An explosive pull-up variation using a kipping swing to generate momentum, pulling the body rapidly above the bar and releasing briefly to clap the hands before re-gripping and controlling the descent.
instructions: Hang from the bar with hands shoulder-width apart. Swing legs back to generate momentum, then quickly pull yourself up and over the bar. Release your grip briefly to clap your hands together before re-grasping the bar. Control your descent back to a dead hang, repeating the process for the desired number of reps.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Kneeling Overhead Press to Donkey Kick
description: From an all-fours kneeling position, perform an overhead press while maintaining a strong core, then extend one leg back and up into a donkey kick. Alternate sides to train shoulders and glutes while challenging core stability and balance.
instructions: Start in an all-fours kneeling position with a strong core. Hold a weight or dumbbell in each hand and press them straight up over your head. Lower the weights back down, then lift one leg up and back into a donkey kick, keeping your core engaged. Alternate legs with each repetition.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Floor Angel
description: The floor angel is a shoulder mobility and activation drill performed lying on the floor, focusing on shoulder stabilizers and postural muscles of the upper back by slowly sliding the arms overhead while maintaining contact with the ground.
instructions: Lie on the floor with arms at your sides. Slowly lift your arms overhead, keeping them straight and in contact with the ground. As you lift, focus on squeezing your shoulder blades together and maintaining a stable upper back. Lower your arms back down to the starting position, repeating the motion in a controlled and deliberate manner.

name: Shoulder Extension Stretch
description: A flexibility drill for the shoulders where the arms are moved behind the body to improve shoulder extension range of motion. Can be used in warm-ups or post-workout mobility work to reduce stiffness and enhance overhead and pressing performance.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold arms straight out to sides at shoulder height. Slowly swing arms backward, keeping them straight, until you feel a stretch in your shoulders and chest. Hold the stretch for a few seconds, then return to the starting position.

name: Backward Medicine Ball Throw
description: Stand holding a medicine ball with both hands, hinge slightly at the hips, and swing the ball between your legs. Explosively extend your hips and drive your arms upward, throwing the ball backward over your head. Reset and repeat for powerful, controlled repetitions.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball with both hands. Hinge slightly at the hips and swing the ball between your legs. Explosively extend your hips and drive your arms upward, throwing the ball backward over your head. Control your movement and reset to starting position.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Medicine Ball Scoop Throw
description: An explosive, full-body plyometric movement starting from a semi-squat while holding a medicine ball between the legs, then driving through the hips and core to scoop the ball upward and throw it overhead in one continuous motion. It can be performed for lower reps to build power or higher reps for conditioning and fat loss.
instructions: Start in a semi-squat position with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold a medicine ball between your legs with both hands. Drive through your hips and core to scoop the ball upward, then throw it overhead in one continuous motion. Land softly on the balls of your feet to absorb the impact and prepare for the next rep.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Standing Two-Arm Overhead Throw
description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a medicine ball at chest level. Brace your core, then swing the ball overhead and explosively throw it forward or down in front of you, following through with your arms and hips. Retrieve the ball and repeat for the desired reps, keeping your core tight and avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a medicine ball at chest level. Brace your core and swing the ball overhead, then explosively throw it forward. Follow through with your arms and hips, keeping your core tight. Retrieve the ball and repeat, avoiding excessive arching of the lower back.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Incline Face-Down Bar Front Raise
description: The incline face-down bar front raise is an upper-body strength exercise targeting the shoulders. Lying chest-down on an incline bench allows a greater range of motion than standing front raises and changes the angle of resistance, emphasizing the front and middle deltoids while also engaging the traps and upper back for stability. It is typically performed with moderate to high repetitions (8–12+ per set) in shoulder or upper-body workouts.
instructions: Lie chest-down on an incline bench with your feet flat on the floor. Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lift the bar forward and up, keeping your arms straight, until it reaches shoulder height. Lower the bar back to the starting position in a controlled motion.

name: EZ-Bar Front Raise
description: Stand holding an EZ bar with an overhand grip at hip level, hands slightly inside shoulder-width. With a slight bend in your elbows and a stable core, raise the bar in front of you to shoulder height without swinging, then lower it under control. Focus on lifting with your shoulders rather than using momentum.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding an EZ bar with an overhand grip at hip level. Raise the bar in front of you to shoulder height with a slight bend in your elbows and stable core. Lift with your shoulders, avoiding momentum and swinging. Lower the bar under control to the starting position.

name: Side Wrist Pull
description: From a standing or seated position, extend one arm straight out to the side at shoulder height with the palm facing down. Use the opposite hand to gently pull the fingers of the extended arm toward the body, creating a light stretch through the forearm and shoulder. Hold the stretch without bouncing, then switch sides.
instructions: Hold one arm straight out to the side at shoulder height with palm facing down. Use your other hand to gently pull the fingers of the extended arm toward your body. Hold the stretch without bouncing for a few seconds. Switch arms to stretch the other side.

name: Elbow Circles
description: Stand tall with your arms lifted to the sides and elbows bent at 90 degrees. Slowly draw small circles with your elbows, moving them forward for several repetitions, then reverse the direction. Keep your shoulders relaxed and core lightly engaged, focusing on smooth, controlled motion to gently warm up and mobilize the shoulder joints.
instructions: Stand tall with arms lifted to the sides and elbows bent at 90 degrees. Draw small circles with your elbows, moving them forward. Reverse direction after several repetitions, keeping shoulders relaxed and core lightly engaged. Maintain smooth, controlled motion to mobilize the shoulder joints.

name: Kneeling Arm Drill
description: From a kneeling position, rapidly drive your arms forward and backward in a running motion to develop shoulder coordination and upper-body conditioning with minimal lower-body impact.
instructions: Start on hands and knees with knees hip-width apart. Drive your arms forward and backward in a rapid, alternating motion, mimicking a running action. Keep your elbows slightly bent and your core engaged to maintain balance. Focus on quick, light arm movements to maintain a consistent pace.

name: Bent-over Shoulder Rotation
description: The bent-over shoulder rotation is an upper-body exercise targeting the rotator cuff and surrounding shoulder stabilizers. Performed in a hinged position, it involves rotating the shoulders through a controlled range of motion, often used as a dynamic warm-up or prehab movement to promote shoulder stability and mobility.
instructions: Hold light dumbbells with arms extended. Bend at the hips, keeping back straight and core engaged. Rotate shoulders in a circular motion, first clockwise and then counterclockwise, maintaining control throughout the range of motion. Keep movements slow and deliberate to target the rotator cuff and surrounding shoulder stabilizers.

name: Band Overhead Triceps Press
description: The band overhead triceps press is an arm exercise performed by pressing a resistance band overhead to isolate and strengthen the triceps. It can be done with a looped band or a band with handles, and is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions to build muscular endurance and definition in the triceps.
instructions: Hold a resistance band with both hands overhead, arms extended. Keep your upper arms still and close to your head. Lower the band behind your head by bending your elbows, then press it back up to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

name: Band Overhead Triceps Extension
description: A single-joint triceps isolation exercise performed with a resistance band in an overhead position to emphasize the long head of the triceps, typically done for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Hold a resistance band in both hands overhead with arms extended. Lower the band behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping upper arms still. Extend your arms to return to the starting position, straightening your elbows. Control the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Band Straight-Arm Pull-Down
description: The band straight-arm pull-down is a home or non-gym variation of the straight-arm lat pull-down. Using resistance bands anchored overhead, you pull your arms down in a straight, slightly arced path while keeping your elbows nearly locked. This movement targets the lats and upper back while engaging the core and triceps isometrically, and is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Stand facing the anchored resistance band with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the band with straight arms at shoulder height, palms facing down. Pull the band down in a straight, slightly arced path, keeping elbows nearly locked, and squeeze your lats at the bottom of the movement. Return to the starting position, controlling the band's resistance throughout the exercise.

name: Band Skullcrusher
description: The band skullcrusher is a triceps isolation exercise performed with resistance bands, typically for moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout.
instructions: Hold a resistance band in both hands with arms extended overhead. Lower the band behind your head, keeping upper arms still, by bending elbows. Extend elbows to return to the starting position, squeezing triceps at the top. Keep control throughout the movement to target the triceps effectively.

name: Single-Arm Band Kick-Back
description: The single-arm band kick-back is an isolation triceps exercise performed with a resistance band. From a hinged or staggered stance, the upper arm stays close to the torso while the forearm extends backward to fully straighten the elbow, emphasizing the triceps. It is typically used as a burnout movement for moderate to high reps in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand in a staggered stance with one hand holding a resistance band. Hold the band with your upper arm close to your torso and your forearm bent at a 90-degree angle. Extend your forearm backward, straightening your elbow, then slowly return to the starting position. Repeat on the other side to complete the exercise.

name: Incline EZ-bar Skullcrusher
description: The incline EZ-bar skullcrusher is a triceps isolation exercise performed on an incline bench. The incline angle increases the stretch on the long head of the triceps, making it especially effective for building size and strength in the back of the upper arm. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad. Hold an EZ-bar with an overhand grip, extending your arms overhead. Lower the bar to your forehead by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms still. Return to the starting position by straightening your arms.

name: Decline Close-Grip Bench To Skull Crusher
description: Lie on a decline bench holding a barbell with a close grip. Lower the bar toward your lower chest, pressing it back up to work the triceps in a close-grip bench motion. Then, without locking out fully, hinge at the elbows and lower the bar toward your forehead or just behind the head, performing a skull crusher. Extend the elbows to return to the start and repeat, keeping your upper arms stable and core braced throughout.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with a close grip on the barbell. Lower the bar to your lower chest, then press it back up. Hinge at the elbows to lower the bar toward your forehead, then extend to return to the start. Keep your upper arms stable and core braced throughout the movement.

name: Bench Press - Powerlifting
description: A heavy barbell bench press variation focused on maximizing strength and power, typically performed with lower reps and higher loads to develop pressing strength for powerlifting.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with feet planted firmly on the ground. Grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, squeezing your shoulder blades together. Lower the bar to your chest, then press upwards extending your arms fully, exhaling at the top of the movement. Return the bar to the starting position, controlling the descent to avoid bouncing off your chest.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Behind-the-head Skullcrusher
description: The behind-the-head skullcrusher is a triceps isolation exercise where you lower a barbell behind your head instead of toward your forehead, increasing the stretch on the triceps. It is typically performed for moderate to high reps (8-12+) as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout. If a straight bar bothers your wrists or elbows, an angled EZ bar can be used as an alternative.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor. Grasp a barbell or EZ bar with an overhand grip, extending your arms straight up over your chest. Lower the bar behind your head, keeping your upper arms still, until you feel a stretch in your triceps. Push the bar back up to the starting position, extending your arms fully.

name: Standing Barbell Overhead Triceps Extension
description: The standing barbell overhead triceps extension is an isolation exercise that targets the long head of the triceps by placing the arms overhead. Performed standing with a barbell held behind the head, the lifter extends the elbows to straighten the arms, then lowers the bar under control. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions in arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a barbell behind your head with your arms extended overhead. Grasp the barbell with an overhand grip and engage your core for stability. Lower the barbell behind your head by bending your elbows, then extend your elbows to return to the starting position, straightening your arms fully. Control the movement throughout the exercise to avoid injury.

name: Reverse-Grip Bench Press
description: The reverse-grip bench press is a barbell pressing variation that uses an underhand grip to emphasize the upper chest and triceps while also engaging the forearms for grip and stabilization. Performed lying on a bench, it can feel awkward at first, so start with lighter weight and consider using a spotter for safety.
instructions: Lie on a bench and grip the barbell with an underhand grip. Lower the bar to your chest, keeping your elbows close to your body. Press the bar upwards, extending your arms fully, then control the return to the starting position. Start with lighter weight and consider using a spotter for safety.

name: Bench Press with Chains
description: A barbell bench press variation where chains are attached to the bar, increasing resistance as the bar is pressed upward to develop lockout strength and power.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor. Grip the barbell with chains attached, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower the bar to chest, then press upward, extending arms fully to develop lockout strength. Control the descent back to starting position, squeezing chest and shoulder muscles throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Floor Press
description: The floor press is a barbell pressing exercise performed while lying on the floor. It reduces range of motion compared to a traditional bench press, emphasizing the triceps and lockout strength while limiting shoulder stress.
instructions: Lie on the floor with knees bent and feet flat. Grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower the barbell to your chest, then press upwards, extending your arms fully, squeezing your triceps at the top. Control the barbell throughout the entire movement, avoiding arching your back.

name: One Arm Floor Press
description: A single-arm barbell press performed while lying on the floor, targeting the triceps and chest while limiting shoulder range of motion for added stability and safety.
instructions: Lie on the floor with your knees bent and feet flat. Hold a barbell over your chest with one hand, arm extended. Lower the barbell to your chest, keeping your elbow close to your body, then press it back up to the starting position. Switch arms to complete the exercise on both sides.

name: JM Press With Bands
description: The JM Press with Bands is a triceps-focused pressing exercise that combines a close-grip barbell press with a partial skull-crusher motion. Resistance bands add accommodating resistance, making the movement harder at lockout and easier at the bottom. Lying on a bench, you lower the bar toward your upper chest/throat with elbows slightly forward, then press back up to full elbow extension to emphasize triceps strength and lockout power.
instructions: Lie on a bench with resistance bands attached to the barbell. Lower the bar toward your upper chest/throat with elbows slightly forward. Press the bar back up to full elbow extension, emphasizing triceps strength and lockout power. Control the movement throughout the entire range of motion.

name: JM Press
description: The JM Press is a hybrid between a close-grip bench press and a triceps extension. Lying on a bench, you lower the bar toward your upper chest or throat with elbows tucked, then press it back up, emphasizing the triceps while minimizing shoulder involvement.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor. Grip the bar with a close grip, about 6-8 inches apart, and lower it toward your upper chest with elbows tucked in. Press the bar back up to the starting position, extending your arms and emphasizing your triceps. Lower and lift the bar in a controlled motion, avoiding jerky movements.

name: Floor Press with Chains
description: Lie on your back on the floor with a barbell loaded with chains held above your chest, arms extended. Lower the bar under control until your upper arms touch the floor, briefly pause, then press the bar back up to full extension. The chains deload slightly at the bottom and add resistance as you press, emphasizing triceps lockout strength while limiting shoulder range of motion.
instructions: Lie on your back on the floor with a barbell loaded with chains held above your chest, arms extended. Lower the bar under control until your upper arms touch the floor, briefly pause. Press the bar back up to full extension, emphasizing triceps lockout strength. Keep the movement slow and controlled throughout the entire range of motion.

name: Reverse Band Bench Press
description: The reverse band bench press is a barbell bench variation where resistance bands are attached above the bar to assist the lift at the bottom and reduce load on the shoulders while still challenging the triceps and chest at lockout.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor. Grip the barbell with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart, and have a spotter attach the resistance bands above the bar. Lower the bar to your chest, then press upwards, extending your arms fully, and control the return to starting position. Focus on squeezing your chest and triceps at the top of the movement.

name: AM Skullcrusher
description: The AM Skullcrusher is an EZ-bar triceps isolation exercise where you lower the bar behind your head while lying on a bench, emphasizing the long head of the triceps through a controlled elbow extension. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout rather than for maximal loads.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor. Grasp an EZ-bar with an overhand grip, extending your arms straight up over your chest. Lower the bar behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms still. Control the bar as you return to the starting position, extending your arms fully.

name: Barbell Skullcrusher
description: Lie on a bench holding a barbell with a narrow overhand grip, arms extended above your chest. Keeping your upper arms fixed, bend your elbows to lower the bar toward your forehead, then extend your elbows to press the bar back up to the starting position, fully engaging the triceps.
instructions: Lie on a bench holding a barbell with a narrow overhand grip. Keep your upper arms fixed and bend your elbows to lower the bar toward your forehead. Extend your elbows to press the bar back up to the starting position, fully engaging your triceps. Breathe naturally and control the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Dumbbell Floor Press
description: The dumbbell floor press is a multijoint pressing exercise performed lying on the floor, typically with knees bent or legs flat. The reduced range of motion compared to a standard dumbbell bench press emphasizes the chest and triceps while often placing less stress on the shoulders.
instructions: Lie on the floor with knees bent and feet flat. Hold dumbbells over chest with arms extended. Lower dumbbells to sides of chest by bending elbows, then press upwards to starting position. Keep core engaged and control the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Seated Triceps Press
description: The seated triceps press is an arm-focused strength exercise performed with a single dumbbell held by both hands. From a seated position, the weight is lowered behind the head and then pressed upward by extending the elbows, emphasizing the triceps. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions (8-12+ per set) and can be loaded relatively heavy, ideally with assistance to get the dumbbell into position when using challenging weights.
instructions: Sit on a bench with feet on the floor. Hold a dumbbell with both hands and lower it behind your head, keeping upper arms still. Press the dumbbell upward by extending your elbows, squeezing your triceps. Lower the dumbbell back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Tricep Dumbbell Kickback
description: The single-arm triceps kick-back is an isolation exercise performed from a bent-over position to increase the size and strength of the triceps.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand with arm bent at 90 degrees. Bend over at the waist, keeping back straight and supporting arm on a stable surface. Extend arm backward, straightening it and squeezing triceps, then slowly lower the dumbbell to the starting position. Repeat with the other arm to complete the exercise.

name: Standing Dumbbell Triceps Extension
description: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell with both hands overhead. Keep your elbows close to your head and slowly lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending at the elbows, then extend your arms to return to the starting position, focusing on squeezing the triceps.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell with both hands overhead. Keep your elbows close to your head and bend them to lower the dumbbell behind your head. Slowly extend your arms to return to the starting position, squeezing your triceps as you straighten. Focus on controlled movements to avoid swinging the dumbbell.

name: Double-Arm Triceps Kick-Back
description: The double-arm triceps kick-back is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps, performed by hinging at the hips and extending both arms backward to straighten the elbows. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions to build triceps size and strength as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing backward. Hinge at the hips and bend knees slightly, leaning forward at the waist. Extend both arms backward, straightening the elbows and lifting the dumbbells. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, controlling the movement to target the triceps.

name: Single-arm Dumbbell Triceps Extension
description: The single-arm dumbbell triceps extension is an isolation exercise performed one arm at a time, typically overhead, to target all three heads of the triceps with emphasis on the long head. It is commonly used in upper-body or arm-focused workouts for moderate to high repetitions (8–12+ reps per set).
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand with arm extended overhead. Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow, keeping your upper arm still. Straighten your arm to return to the starting position, extending the dumbbell overhead again. Switch arms to repeat on the other side.

name: Decline Dumbbell Triceps Extension
description: Lie on a decline bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms extended above your chest, palms facing each other. Keeping your upper arms fixed, bend at the elbows to lower the dumbbells toward the sides of your head, feeling a stretch in the triceps. Extend your elbows to return the weights to the starting position, maintaining control and avoiding flaring the elbows too wide.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended above your chest, and palms facing each other. Lower the dumbbells by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms still, and stretching your triceps. Extend your elbows to return the weights to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement. Avoid flaring your elbows too wide for proper form.

name: Dumbbell Skullcrusher
description: The dumbbell skullcrusher is an isolation triceps exercise performed lying on a bench, lowering dumbbells toward the forehead or behind the head and extending the elbows to lockout. It is typically used with moderate to high reps, such as 8-12 per set, in upper-body or arm-focused workouts to build triceps size and strength.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor. Hold a dumbbell in each hand with your arms extended overhead and palms facing each other. Lower the dumbbells behind your head by bending your elbows, then extend your elbows to return to the starting position. Control the movement throughout to avoid injury.

name: One Arm Pronated Dumbbell Triceps Extension
description: A single-arm triceps isolation exercise performed lying or standing, extending a pronated (palms-down) grip dumbbell to target the triceps through elbow extension.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell with one hand and a pronated grip, palms facing down. Lie on a flat surface or stand with your arm extended overhead and upper arm still. Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow, then extend your arm to return to the starting position. Keep your upper arm still throughout the movement.

name: One Arm Supinated Dumbbell Triceps Extension
description: A single-arm triceps isolation exercise performed lying down with a supinated (palms-up) grip, extending the elbow to target the triceps.
instructions: Lie on a flat surface with your arm extended overhead and a dumbbell in one hand with a supinated grip. Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow. Extend your elbow to return the dumbbell to the starting position, squeezing your triceps. Repeat with the other arm to ensure balanced development.

name: Seated Bent-Over Two-Arm Dumbbell Triceps Extension
description: Sit on the edge of a bench with a dumbbell held in both hands. Hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat and chest slightly up. Start with your elbows bent and the dumbbell near your chest, then extend your arms straight back by squeezing your triceps. Pause briefly at full extension, then slowly return to the starting position with control.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with a dumbbell in both hands. Hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat and chest up. Lower the dumbbell to your chest with elbows bent, then extend your arms straight back by squeezing your triceps. Return to the starting position with control, pausing briefly at full extension.

name: Tate Press
description: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with arms extended above your chest, palms facing your feet. Bend your elbows out to the sides, lowering the dumbbells toward your chest while keeping upper arms relatively still. Press the dumbbells back up by extending your elbows to fully contract the triceps.
instructions: Lie on a bench holding dumbbells with arms extended above your chest, palms facing your feet. Bend your elbows out to the sides, lowering the dumbbells toward your chest. Press the dumbbells back up by extending your elbows to fully contract the triceps. Keep your upper arms still throughout the movement.

name: Standing Bent-Over One-Arm Dumbbell Triceps Extension
description: A single-arm bent-over triceps isolation exercise where you hinge at the hips and extend the working arm backward to target the triceps.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your arm bent and palm facing backward. Hinge at the hips and bend over, keeping your back straight and core engaged. Extend your arm backward, straightening it completely, then lower it back to the starting position. Switch arms to work the other triceps.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Seated Bent-Over One-Arm Dumbbell Triceps Extension
description: Sit on the edge of a bench or box, holding a dumbbell in one hand with a neutral grip. Hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat and core braced, and let the working arm hang straight down with the upper arm close to your torso. Keeping your upper arm fixed, extend the elbow to straighten the arm and squeeze the triceps at the top, then slowly bend the elbow to return to the starting position. Repeat for reps, then switch arms.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with a dumbbell in one hand and a neutral grip. Hinge forward at the hips, keeping your back flat and core braced, with your working arm hanging straight down. Extend your elbow to straighten your arm, squeezing your triceps at the top. Slowly lower the dumbbell back to the starting position and repeat, then switch arms.

name: Dumbbell Tricep Extension - Pronated Grip
description: A triceps isolation exercise performed with a pronated (overhand) grip on a dumbbell to target the back of the upper arm. Typically done standing or seated, you extend the elbow to lift the weight and control it back down.
instructions: Stand or sit with your back straight and hold a dumbbell with a pronated grip. Hold the dumbbell overhead with your arm extended and your palm facing forward. Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow, keeping your upper arm still. Raise the dumbbell back to the starting position by straightening your arm.

name: V-sit with Overhead Triceps Extension
description: The V-sit with overhead triceps extension is a hybrid movement that combines a V-sit core hold with an overhead triceps extension. While holding a V-shaped seated position to engage the abs, you press a dumbbell overhead and bend at the elbows to lower and extend the weight, targeting the triceps while maintaining core tension.
instructions: Sit on floor with legs lifted and torso upright, forming a V-shape. Hold a dumbbell overhead with both hands and extend arms fully. Lower the dumbbell behind head by bending elbows, keeping upper arms still. Return to starting position by straightening arms and extending the dumbbell overhead.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Single-arm Dumbbell Skullcrusher
description: The single-arm dumbbell skullcrusher is an isolation triceps exercise performed lying on a bench, extending one arm at a time to target the triceps through a large range of motion. It is typically done for moderate to high reps, such as 8-12 per set, in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with your back pressed against the pad. Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your arm extended overhead and your upper arm still. Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow, keeping your upper arm still. Return to the starting position by straightening your arm, extending your elbow.

name: Dumbbells-Together Incline Bench Press
description: The dumbbells-together incline bench press is a chest-focused pressing exercise performed on an incline bench while squeezing the dumbbells together throughout the movement. This constant tension emphasizes the upper chest and also engages the shoulders and triceps. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or chest-focused workout.
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with feet on floor. Hold dumbbells together over chest, squeezing them tightly. Press dumbbells upwards, extending arms fully, then lower them back to starting position, keeping constant tension. Breathe naturally and maintain control throughout the movement.

name: Incline Dumbbell Overhead Triceps Extension
description: The incline dumbbell overhead triceps extension is an upper-body strength exercise that targets the triceps, especially the long head, by performing an overhead extension while lying on an incline bench. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions in arm or upper-body workouts.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with your back against the pad. Hold a dumbbell in each hand overhead with arms extended and palms facing forward. Lower the dumbbells behind your head by bending your elbows, then raise them back to the starting position by straightening your arms. Control the movement throughout the exercise to target your triceps effectively.

name: Dumbbell Crush Press
description: Lie on a bench holding two dumbbells together above your chest with palms facing each other. Keep the dumbbells pressed tightly together as you lower them toward the mid-chest, keeping elbows close to your sides. Pause briefly, then press the dumbbells back up while maintaining constant tension by squeezing them together throughout the movement.
instructions: Lie on a bench holding two dumbbells together above your chest with palms facing each other. Lower the dumbbells toward your mid-chest, keeping elbows close to your sides and the dumbbells pressed tightly together. Pause briefly, then press the dumbbells back up to the starting position, maintaining constant tension by squeezing them together. Keep control throughout the movement to target the correct muscles.

name: Holman Hovering Renegade Triceps Press
description: From a high plank position with hands on dumbbells, perform a controlled triceps press by bending the elbows to lower your chest toward the dumbbells while keeping the elbows close to your sides, then press back up, maintaining a strong core and stable hips throughout.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands on dumbbells. Lower your chest toward the dumbbells by bending your elbows, keeping them close to your sides. Press back up to the starting position, maintaining a strong core and stable hips. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Holman Heavy Triceps Press to Overhead Press
description: A compound dumbbell movement that combines a heavy close-grip triceps press with an overhead press. Lying on a bench, press the dumbbells up with elbows tucked to emphasize the triceps, then transition to an overhead press position to drive the weights overhead, engaging the shoulders while maintaining control throughout the range of motion.
instructions: Lie on a bench with dumbbells over your chest and elbows tucked. Press the dumbbells up, extending your arms and emphasizing your triceps. Transition to an overhead press position, driving the weights overhead while engaging your shoulders. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, maintaining control throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Total Fitness Close-Grip Dumbbell Press
description: The close-grip dumbbell press is a variation of the dumbbell bench press where the dumbbells are held closer together to emphasize the triceps while still engaging the chest and shoulders. Performed lying on a bench, you press the dumbbells up from chest level with a narrow grip, keeping elbows close to your sides to increase triceps activation.
instructions: Lie on a bench with dumbbells held close together over your chest. Grip the dumbbells with your hands close to each other, keeping elbows close to your sides. Press the dumbbells upwards, extending your arms fully, then lower them back to the starting position with control. Keep your core engaged and focus on squeezing your triceps throughout the movement.

name: Dumbbell Row to Triceps Kick-Back
description: A compound dumbbell exercise that combines a bent-over row with a triceps kick-back. From a hip-hinged position, you row the dumbbells toward your torso to target the lats and upper back, then extend the elbows to perform a kick-back to work the triceps while the core, glutes, and hamstrings stabilize the body isometrically.
instructions: Hold dumbbells with palms facing your body. Hinge at the hips and bend your knees slightly, keeping your back straight. Row the dumbbells towards your torso, squeezing your lats and upper back, then extend your elbows to perform a kick-back, targeting your triceps. Lower the dumbbells back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Rolling Triceps Press
description: The rolling triceps press is a dynamic dumbbell triceps exercise often used as a bench press accessory by powerlifters. Lying on a bench or the floor, you lower the dumbbells toward your shoulders with an elbow bend and slight roll, then extend the arms to press back up, emphasizing triceps strength and hypertrophy.
instructions: Lie on a bench or floor with dumbbells over your chest. Lower the dumbbells toward your shoulders by bending your elbows and slightly rolling your arms. Extend your arms to press the dumbbells back up, focusing on triceps contraction. Control the movement throughout the exercise to avoid injury.

name: Arms Overhead Dumbbell Triceps Extension
description: Stand or sit with a dumbbell held overhead in both hands, arms fully extended. Keeping your upper arms close to your head, bend at the elbows to lower the dumbbell behind your head, then extend your elbows to return to the starting position, focusing on squeezing the triceps.
instructions: Stand or sit with a dumbbell held overhead in both hands, arms fully extended. Keep your upper arms close to your head and bend at the elbows to lower the dumbbell behind your head. Extend your elbows to return to the starting position, squeezing your triceps as you straighten your arms. Focus on controlled movements and proper form throughout the exercise.

name: Dumbbell Triceps Kickback
description: From a hip-hinged position, hold a dumbbell in one hand with your upper arm close to your torso and elbow bent at 90 degrees. Keeping your upper arm fixed, extend your elbow to straighten the arm fully behind you, squeezing the triceps at the top, then slowly return to the starting position. Repeat for reps and switch arms.
instructions: Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your upper arm close to your torso and elbow bent at 90 degrees. Hinge at the hips and keep your back straight. Extend your elbow to straighten your arm behind you, squeezing your triceps at the top. Slowly return to the starting position and repeat on the other side.

name: 30 Arms BFR Dumbbell Kick-Back
description: Lean forward with a flat back while holding dumbbells, upper arms close to your torso and elbows bent at 90 degrees. Keeping your upper arms fixed, extend your elbows to straighten your arms and squeeze the triceps at the top, then slowly return to the starting position. This variation is performed with blood flow restriction (BFR) bands on the upper arms to increase training stimulus with lighter weights.
instructions: Put BFR bands on your upper arms. Hold dumbbells with your arms bent at 90 degrees and upper arms close to your torso. Lean forward with a flat back and extend your elbows to straighten your arms, squeezing your triceps at the top. Slowly return to the starting position, keeping control throughout the movement.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Dumbbell Saw Triceps Skullcrusher
description: A compound triceps exercise that combines a dumbbell skullcrusher with a dumbbell shoulder press. Lying on a bench, you lower the dumbbells toward your forehead or behind your head to emphasize the triceps, then extend the elbows and transition into a pressing motion to finish overhead, increasing time under tension for the triceps while also engaging the shoulders.
instructions: Lie on a bench with dumbbells over your chest. Lower the dumbbells toward your forehead, stretching your triceps. Extend your elbows, then press the dumbbells upwards, fully extending your arms overhead. Control the movement throughout, focusing on slow and deliberate transitions.

name: Chain Handle Extension
description: A triceps isolation exercise where you extend the elbows against resistance using a chain or cable-style handle, focusing on locking out the arms to build triceps strength and stability.
instructions: Stand facing the chain handle with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold the chain handle with palms facing down and arms extended at a 90-degree angle. Extend the elbows to lock out the arms, keeping upper arms still and only moving the forearms. Lower the handle back to the starting position in a controlled manner.

name: Sled Overhead Triceps Extension
description: Face away from a sled attached to a cable or strap, holding the handles overhead with elbows bent. Keeping your upper arms fixed beside your head, extend your elbows to press your hands forward and up until your arms are straight, squeezing the triceps. Control the return to the starting position and repeat for reps.
instructions: Face away from the sled, holding handles overhead with elbows bent. Keep your upper arms still beside your head. Extend your elbows to straighten your arms, squeezing your triceps as you press your hands forward and up. Control the return to the starting position to repeat for reps.

name: Body Triceps Press Using Flat Bench
description: A bodyweight triceps exercise performed face-down with your hands on a flat bench, lowering your head and chest toward the bench by bending the elbows, then pressing back up to extend the arms. The angle and body position can be adjusted to make the movement easier or harder, effectively targeting all three heads of the triceps.
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands on a flat bench and feet on the ground. Lower your head and chest toward the bench by bending your elbows, keeping your body straight. Push back up to the starting position by extending your arms, squeezing your triceps. Control the movement throughout to target your triceps effectively.

name: Suspended Triceps Press
description: A bodyweight triceps exercise performed with the hands suspended in straps or on a suspension trainer, pressing the body away by extending the elbows while maintaining a rigid plank position.
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands suspended in straps or a suspension trainer. Engage your core and maintain a rigid body position. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground. Press back up to the starting position by extending your elbows, keeping your core engaged and body straight.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Seated Triceps Push-Down
description: The seated triceps push-down is an isolation exercise for the triceps performed with a rope or similar attachment on a cable machine. Sitting reduces momentum and lower-body involvement, helping you focus on controlled elbow extension and consistent tension through the triceps, typically for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Sit at the cable machine with your feet flat on the floor. Hold the rope attachment with an overhand grip, keeping your elbows close to your body. Extend your arms fully, then lower the rope back down to the starting position, keeping control throughout the movement. Focus on squeezing your triceps as you extend your arms.

name: Holman Overhead Weighted Triceps Press
description: An overhead triceps press variation where you extend a weight straight up from behind your head to target the triceps. Keep your elbows close to your ears, core braced, and avoid arching your lower back as you press the weight overhead and lower it under control.
instructions: Hold a weight with both hands behind your head. Keep your elbows close to your ears and core braced. Press the weight straight up overhead, extending your arms fully. Lower the weight back down to the starting position under control, avoiding arching your lower back.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Weighted Triceps Dip
description: A compound bodyweight exercise where additional weight is added to increase resistance while dipping to target the triceps, shoulders, and chest.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench or chair with hands gripping the edge. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground, keeping additional weight secured. Straighten your arms to return to the starting position, extending your elbows fully. Keep your core engaged and control the movement throughout the exercise.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Cable V-bar Push-down
description: The cable V-bar push-down is an isolation exercise that targets the triceps using a cable machine and V-shaped attachment. Performed standing, you extend the elbows to push the bar down, emphasizing the triceps through a controlled range of motion. It is commonly used with moderate to high repetitions as part of upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with the V-bar attachment at chest level. Grasp the V-bar with a shoulder-width overhand grip. Extend your elbows to push the bar down towards your thighs, keeping your upper arms still. Return to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid jerking the cable.

name: Reverse Grip Triceps Pushdown
description: The reverse-grip triceps pushdown is a cable-based isolation exercise for the triceps performed with an underhand (supinated) grip on a straight bar. The palms-up position shifts emphasis to the medial head of the triceps and challenges grip strength, so it is typically done with moderate to high repetitions, such as 8–12 or more per set.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with a straight bar attached. Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, palms facing up. Extend the bar downward by straightening your arms, then return to the starting position in a controlled motion. Keep your elbows close to your body throughout the exercise.

name: Kneeling Cable Triceps Extension
description: The kneeling cable triceps extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a cable stack, typically performed in a kneeling position facing away from the pulley. It emphasizes elbow extension to build triceps size and strength while minimizing assistance from other muscle groups.
instructions: Kneel facing away from the cable pulley with the rope attachment at shoulder height. Hold the rope with both hands and extend your arms fully, keeping your upper arms still. Lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows, then extend your arms to the starting position, squeezing your triceps. Keep your core engaged and back straight throughout the exercise.

name: Single-arm cable triceps extension
description: The single-arm cable triceps extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps by extending the elbow against constant cable resistance, typically performed for moderate to high repetitions in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the cable at chest height. Hold a handle in one hand with your arm bent and upper arm still. Extend your arm straight behind you, keeping your upper arm stable, then lower it back to the starting position. Repeat with the other arm to complete the exercise.

name: Dip Machine
description: The dip machine is a strength exercise that mimics parallel bar dips to primarily target the triceps while also engaging the chest and shoulders. Using a guided machine path, you press the handles down by extending your elbows, keeping your torso relatively upright and arms close to your body. It can be used for moderate to heavy loads for strength or higher repetitions for muscle growth.
instructions: Sit at the dip machine with your feet flat on the floor. Grasp the handles with your hands shoulder-width apart and your arms close to your body. Lower your body by bending your elbows, then press the handles down by extending your elbows to return to the starting position. Keep your torso upright and control the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Triceps Pushdown
description: The triceps pushdown is a cable-based isolation exercise that targets the triceps using a V-bar attachment, typically performed for moderate to high reps in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with a V-bar attachment. Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Extend the bar down towards your thighs, keeping elbows close to your body, then return to the starting position in a controlled motion. Keep your core engaged and focus on squeezing your triceps throughout the movement.

name: Low Cable Overhead Triceps Extension
description: The low cable overhead triceps extension is a single-joint isolation exercise targeting the triceps. Lying on a bench with a low cable and rope attachment, you extend your arms overhead to fully straighten the elbows, emphasizing the long head of the triceps. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout.
instructions: Lie on a bench with a low cable and rope attachment at your feet. Grasp the rope with both hands and extend your arms overhead, straightening your elbows. Lower the rope behind your head, bending your elbows, then extend again to the starting position. Control the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Lying Cable Triceps Extension
description: The lying cable triceps extension is an isolation exercise performed lying on a bench or the floor, targeting the triceps through elbow extension. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions to create a strong burn and pump as part of an arm-focused workout.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench or the floor with your back pressed against the surface. Hold a cable rope attachment above your head with your arms extended. Lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows, then extend your arms to return to the starting position. Control the movement throughout to target your triceps effectively.

name: Low Cable Triceps Extension
description: Stand facing a low cable pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment. Grip the handle with both hands and keep your elbows close to your sides. Extend your arms downward by straightening your elbows until your arms are fully extended, squeezing the triceps at the bottom. Control the weight as you bend your elbows to return to the starting position without letting them flare forward.
instructions: Stand facing a low cable pulley with a straight bar or rope attachment. Grip the handle with both hands, keeping elbows close to your sides. Extend your arms downward by straightening your elbows until fully extended, squeezing your triceps. Control the weight as you bend your elbows to return to the starting position.

name: Cable Overhead Triceps Extension
description: A seated cable exercise where the weight is extended overhead to isolate the triceps, with an emphasis on the long head due to the overhead arm position. Typically performed for moderate to high repetitions to build triceps size and endurance while minimizing momentum.
instructions: Sit at cable machine with feet on floor. Grasp cable rope overhead with arms extended and palms facing forward. Lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows, keeping upper arms still. Return to starting position by straightening your arms, extending the cable overhead.

name: Single-arm low cable triceps extension
description: A single-joint isolation exercise where you drive a low cable handle overhead to fully extend the elbow, targeting the triceps with controlled, moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with the cable at a low height. Hold the handle with one hand and extend your arm overhead, keeping your upper arm still. Lower the handle behind your head by bending your elbow, then drive it back up to the starting position, fully extending your elbow. Repeat with the other arm to complete the exercise.

name: Incline Cable Straight-Bar Triceps Extension
description: The incline cable straight-bar triceps extension is a single-joint arm exercise performed lying on an incline bench facing away from a cable machine. The incline position increases the stretch and range of motion on the triceps, especially the long head, while the cable provides constant tension throughout the movement. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions as part of an arm-focused strength workout.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench facing away from a cable machine. Grasp a straight-bar cable attachment with an overhead grip. Extend the bar downward by straightening your arms, then lower it back to the starting position in a controlled motion. Keep your upper arms still and only move your forearms throughout the exercise.

name: Single-arm cable triceps kick-back
description: The single-arm cable triceps kick-back is an isolation exercise that targets the triceps, typically used as a burnout movement for moderate to high repetitions in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Hold a cable handle with one hand and stand facing the cable machine. Keep your upper arm still and close to your body. Extend your arm straight back, squeezing your triceps at the top of the movement. Return to the starting position, controlling the cable's return to avoid jerky movements.

name: Incline Cable Rope Triceps Extension
description: A single-joint triceps exercise performed lying on an incline bench using a cable and rope attachment. The incline position increases stretch and range of motion, emphasizing the long head of the triceps and allowing controlled, moderate-to-high rep sets for arm development.
instructions: Lie on an incline bench with your face up. Hold a rope attachment connected to a cable machine above you with your arms extended. Lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows, then extend your arms to return to the starting position. Control the movement throughout the exercise to target your triceps.

name: Reverse-Grip Cable Straight-Bar Push-Down
description: An isolation triceps exercise performed on a cable machine using a straight bar with an underhand (palms-up) grip. The lifter keeps elbows close to the torso and extends the arms to push the bar down, emphasizing the triceps while grip strength can be a limiting factor. Typically done for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with a straight bar at chest height. Grasp the bar with an underhand grip, palms facing up, and keep elbows close to your torso. Extend your arms to push the bar down, keeping your upper arms still, and then control the return to the starting position.

name: Single-Arm Incline Triceps Extension
description: The single-arm incline triceps extension is an isolation exercise that targets the triceps, emphasizing the long head due to the incline angle. Performed with a dumbbell or cable on an incline bench, it is typically done for moderate to high repetitions in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Sit on an incline bench with your back against the pad. Hold a dumbbell in one hand with your arm extended overhead and your upper arm still. Lower the dumbbell behind your head by bending your elbow, then extend your arm to the starting position. Switch arms to work the other triceps.

name: Cable Rope Push-Down
description: The cable rope push-down is an isolation exercise that targets the triceps using a cable machine with a rope attachment. Performed with controlled elbow extension, it is typically done for moderate to high repetitions (8–12+ per set) as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with a rope attachment at chest height. Hold the rope with an overhand grip, keeping elbows close to your body. Extend your arms fully, pushing the rope down towards your thighs, then control the return to the starting position. Keep your upper arms still, only moving your forearms during the exercise.

name: Cable Straight-Bar Push-Down
description: The cable straight-bar push-down is an isolation exercise that targets the triceps by extending the elbows against resistance from a cable machine. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with the bar at chest height. Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Extend the bar down towards your thighs, keeping elbows close to your body, then return to the starting position in a controlled manner. Keep your core engaged and focus on extending your elbows to target your triceps.

name: Straight-Arm Bar Pull-Down
description: The straight-arm bar pull-down is a cable exercise targeting the lats and upper back. Performed standing and slightly hinged at the hips, you pull a straight bar from shoulder height down toward the thighs with elbows extended, focusing on driving the movement with the lats rather than the arms. It emphasizes a full stretch and strong contraction of the lats while also engaging the core and triceps isometrically, and is typically done for moderate to higher repetitions.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a slight hip hinge. Grasp the straight bar with an overhand grip, arms extended and shoulders down. Pull the bar down toward your thighs, keeping elbows straight and squeezing your lats. Control the return to the starting position, maintaining tension in your lats and core.

name: AM Rope Triceps Pushdown
description: The cable rope triceps pushdown is an isolation exercise that targets the triceps by extending the elbows against resistance from a cable machine using a rope attachment. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions as part of an upper-body or arm-focused workout.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with a rope attachment at chest height. Hold the rope with an overhand grip and extend your arms fully. Lower the rope by bending your elbows, keeping your upper arms still, then push it back to the starting position. Control the movement throughout the exercise.

name: AM Bar Triceps Push-Down
description: The AM bar triceps push-down is a cable-based isolation exercise that targets the triceps by extending the elbows against resistance, typically performed for moderate to high repetitions in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with the AM bar at chest height. Grasp the bar with an overhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
