Extend the bar down towards your thighs, straightening your arms and squeezing your triceps. Return the bar to the starting position, controlling the movement to avoid jerking or swinging.

name: Rope Overhead Triceps Extension
description: The rope overhead triceps extension is a single-joint cable exercise that targets the triceps, with an emphasis on the long head due to the overhead arm position. Performed standing with a rope attached to a cable machine, it is commonly used near the end of a triceps workout for moderate to high repetitions.
instructions: Stand facing a cable machine with a rope attachment at a high position. Hold the rope with both hands and extend your arms overhead, keeping your elbows close together. Lower the rope behind your head by bending your elbows, then raise it back to the starting position by straightening your arms. Keep control throughout the movement to target your triceps effectively.

name: LD Cable One-Arm Upright Row
description: Stand side-on to a cable machine with the handle set at a low position. Grasp the handle with one hand and stand tall with your core braced. Pull the handle upward along your torso, leading with your elbow and keeping the handle close to your body until your elbow reaches about shoulder height. Pause briefly, then lower the handle under control to the starting position. Perform all reps on one side before switching arms.
instructions: Stand side-on to a cable machine with the handle at a low position. Grasp the handle with one hand and stand tall with your core braced. Pull the handle up along your torso, leading with your elbow, until it reaches shoulder height. Lower the handle under control to the starting position, then repeat on the other side after completing all reps.

name: Underhand Cable Straight-Bar Push-Down
description: Stand facing a cable machine with a straight bar attached to a high pulley using an underhand (supinated) grip. Keeping your elbows close to your sides, extend your arms by pushing the bar down until your elbows are fully straight, then slowly return to the starting position with control. Focus on isolating the triceps and avoid swinging or using momentum.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a straight bar attached to a high pulley, using an underhand grip. Keep your elbows close to your sides and push the bar down, extending your arms until your elbows are fully straight. Slowly return to the starting position with control, focusing on isolating the triceps. Avoid swinging or using momentum, keeping the movement smooth and deliberate.

name: Single-Arm Reverse-Grip Cable Push-Down
description: Stand facing a cable machine with a single handle attached to a high pulley. Grasp the handle with an underhand (reverse) grip using one hand, elbow close to your side and forearm parallel to the floor. Keeping your upper arm still, extend your elbow to push the handle down until your arm is straight, squeezing the triceps at the bottom. Control the handle back up to the starting position without letting your elbow drift forward. Repeat for reps, then switch arms.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a single handle at a high pulley. Grasp the handle with an underhand grip using one hand, keeping your elbow close to your side. Extend your elbow to push the handle down, squeezing your triceps at the bottom. Control the handle back up to the starting position, then repeat and switch arms.

name: Cable V-Bar Push-Down
description: Attach a V-bar to a high pulley on a cable machine. Stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart and grasp the V-bar with a neutral grip, elbows close to your sides. Start with elbows bent at about 90 degrees and upper arms stationary. Push the bar down by extending your elbows until your arms are fully straight without locking out hard, squeezing the triceps at the bottom. Control the bar back up to the starting position without letting your elbows flare forward or outward. Repeat for the desired reps, keeping your torso upright and core braced throughout.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with feet shoulder-width apart. Grasp the V-bar with a neutral grip, keeping elbows close to your sides and bent at 90 degrees. Push the bar down by extending your elbows until arms are straight, squeezing triceps at the bottom. Control the bar back up to the starting position, keeping torso upright and core braced.

name: Cable Rope Overhead Triceps Extension
description: Stand facing away from a cable machine with a rope attachment set at a low pulley. Grip the rope with both hands, bring it overhead so your elbows are bent and pointing forward, then extend your arms fully to straighten the elbows, squeezing the triceps at the top. Control the weight back to the starting position without letting your elbows flare excessively.

name: BFR Underhand Straight-Bar Push-Down
description: The BFR underhand straight-bar push-down is a cable-based triceps isolation exercise performed with blood flow restriction bands placed high on the upper arms. Using a lighter load than traditional push-downs, you grip the straight bar with an underhand (supinated) grip and extend the elbows to push the bar down, emphasizing high-rep, pump-focused work for triceps size and endurance.
instructions: Place blood flow restriction bands high on your upper arms. Hold a straight-bar cable with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Extend your elbows to push the bar down, keeping your upper arms still, then return to the starting position. Focus on high-rep, controlled movements to target your triceps.

name: BFR Overhand Straight-Bar Push-Down
description: The BFR overhand straight-bar push-down is a cable-based triceps isolation exercise performed with blood flow restriction bands placed high on the upper arms. Using a straight bar and an overhand grip, you extend the elbows to push the bar down, focusing on high reps with significantly lighter loads (about 25–50% of normal working weight) to create a strong pump and stimulate triceps growth.
instructions: Attach blood flow restriction bands high on your upper arms. Hold a straight bar with an overhand grip, standing facing the cable machine. Extend your elbows to push the bar down, keeping your upper arms still, and focus on high reps with light loads. Return to the starting position, controlling the movement to target your triceps.

name: BFR Cable Rope Push-down
description: The blood flow restriction (BFR) cable rope push-down is an isolation exercise for building triceps size and strength using a cable stack, rope attachment, and BFR bands. Performed with lighter loads and higher reps, it emphasizes metabolic stress and a strong contraction at full elbow extension while keeping the upper arms fixed at the sides.
instructions: Stand facing a cable stack with a rope attachment at chest level. Wear BFR bands around the upper arms, just above the elbows. Hold the rope with palms facing down and extend the arms fully, keeping the upper arms still, then lower the rope back to the starting position in a controlled motion. Focus on squeezing the triceps at full extension.

name: Triceps Pressdown
description: Stand facing a cable machine with a handle or bar attached to a high pulley. Grip the attachment with your elbows close to your sides and upper arms stationary. Press the handle down by extending your elbows until your arms are straight, then control the weight back up without letting your elbows flare forward.
instructions: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle or bar attached to a high pulley. Grip the attachment with your elbows close to your sides and upper arms stationary. Press the handle down by extending your elbows until your arms are straight. Control the weight back up without letting your elbows flare forward.

name: Assist Machine Triceps Push-Down
description: The assist machine triceps push-down is performed on an assisted pull-up or dip station by pressing downward on the knee pad against the selected weight. It mimics the motion of a triceps cable push-down using open hands, emphasizing elbow extension to target the triceps. Typically done for moderate to high repetitions (8–12+ per set), it is commonly included in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Stand at the assist machine with knees on the pad and hands grasping the bar. Press downward on the knee pad to lift your body, then extend your elbows to lower the bar. Slowly return to the starting position, keeping control throughout the movement. Focus on extending your elbows to target your triceps.

name: Machine Triceps Dip
description: Sit in the triceps dip machine with your back against the pad and hands on the handles. With elbows bent and tucked close to your sides, press the handles down by extending your elbows until your arms are nearly straight, then slowly return to the starting position to target the triceps.
instructions: Sit in the machine with your back against the pad and hands on the handles. Bend your elbows and tuck them close to your sides. Press the handles down by extending your elbows until your arms are nearly straight. Slowly return to the starting position to complete the exercise.

name: Triceps Dip
description: The triceps dip is a bodyweight exercise performed on parallel bars or a dip station. It primarily targets the triceps while also engaging the chest and shoulders. With an upright torso, bent knees, and elbows kept close to the body, it can be used for both strength (lower reps) and hypertrophy (higher reps).
instructions: Stand in front of a dip station with hands gripping the bars. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your arms are bent at a 90-degree angle, keeping your torso upright and knees slightly bent. Straighten your arms to return to the starting position, extending your elbows fully. Keep your elbows close to your body throughout the movement.

name: Weighted Bench Dip
description: The weighted bench dip is a triceps-focused strength exercise performed with your hands on a bench behind you and your feet extended forward. Additional resistance is added by placing a weight plate on the lap, increasing the load beyond bodyweight to build size and strength in the triceps.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands grasping the edge and your feet extended forward. Place a weight plate on your lap to add resistance. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your arms are bent at a 90-degree angle. Push back up to the starting position, straightening your arms and extending your elbows.

name: Close-Grip Push-Up
description: The close-grip push-up is a bodyweight upper-body exercise that emphasizes the triceps while also working the chest and shoulders. Hands are placed close together under the chest, keeping elbows tucked near the body as you lower and press back up.
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands close together under your chest. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the ground, keeping elbows tucked near your body. Push back up to the starting position, extending your arms fully. Keep your core engaged and body straight throughout the movement.

name: Parallel Bar Dip
description: A bodyweight dip performed on parallel bars with a more upright torso and elbows kept close to the body to emphasize the triceps while also engaging the chest and shoulders. Typically done with knees bent and crossed, it can be used for both strength (lower reps) and hypertrophy (higher reps).
instructions: Stand in front of parallel bars with hands grasping the bars at shoulder width. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground, keeping your elbows close to your body. Straighten your arms to return to the starting position, extending your elbows fully. Keep your knees bent and crossed to maintain balance and control throughout the movement.

name: Ring Dip
description: The ring dip is an upper-body strength exercise performed on gymnastic rings. It emphasizes the triceps, chest, and shoulders while demanding significant core stability due to the instability of the rings. It is more challenging than standard parallel bar dips and is popular in gymnastics and CrossFit as a progression for advanced pressing strength.
instructions: Grip the rings with hands facing forward and body straight. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your arms are bent at a 90-degree angle, keeping your core engaged. Push back up to the starting position by straightening your arms, using your triceps and chest to control the movement. Keep your body stable and rings steady throughout the exercise.

name: Feet-Elevated Bench Dip
description: The feet-elevated bench dip is a bodyweight exercise that targets the triceps while also engaging the chest and shoulders. Elevating the feet increases the load on the upper body and allows additional weight to be placed on the hips if desired. If standard bench dips cause shoulder discomfort, performing the movement between two benches or chairs can reduce strain on the shoulders.
instructions: Place your hands on the edge of a bench with your feet elevated on another bench or surface. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground. Straighten your arms to return to the starting position, extending your elbows fully. Keep your core engaged and control the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Bodyweight Triceps Press
description: The bodyweight triceps press is a bodyweight pressing movement performed facing the floor with your hands on a fixed bar set at about waist to chest height. From a straight-arm plank position, you bend your elbows to lower your forehead or top of your head toward the bar, then press back to full extension, emphasizing all three heads of the triceps. Moving your feet farther back or lowering the bar increases difficulty, while stepping closer or raising the bar makes it easier.
instructions: Start in a straight-arm plank position with hands on a fixed bar at waist to chest height. Lower your forehead or top of your head toward the bar by bending your elbows. Press back to full extension, straightening your arms and emphasizing your triceps. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels throughout the movement.

name: Tricep Side Stretch
description: From a standing or seated position, raise one arm overhead, bend the elbow to reach the hand down toward the upper back, then gently pull the elbow across and slightly behind the head with the opposite hand to stretch the triceps and side of the upper arm. Hold the stretch without bouncing and repeat on the other side.
instructions: Stand or sit with good posture. Raise one arm overhead and bend your elbow to reach your hand down toward your upper back. Gently pull your elbow across and slightly behind your head with your opposite hand to stretch your tricep. Hold the stretch without bouncing and repeat on the other side.

name: Triceps Stretch
description: From a standing or seated position, raise one arm overhead and bend the elbow so your hand reaches down toward the upper back. Use the opposite hand to gently pull the elbow toward your head until you feel a stretch along the back of the upper arm. Hold, then switch sides.
instructions: Stand or sit with good posture. Raise one arm overhead and bend your elbow, reaching your hand toward your upper back. Use your opposite hand to gently pull your elbow toward your head until you feel a stretch in the back of your upper arm. Hold the stretch, then switch sides to stretch the other arm.

name: Overhead Triceps Stretch
description: From a standing or seated position, raise one arm overhead, bend the elbow to bring the hand toward the upper back, and gently pull the elbow with the opposite hand to stretch the triceps. Hold, then switch sides.
instructions: Stand or sit with good posture. Raise one arm overhead and bend your elbow to bring your hand toward your upper back. Gently pull your elbow with your opposite hand to stretch your triceps, holding for a few seconds. Switch arms to stretch the other side.

name: Russian Bar Dip
description: The Russian bar dip is an advanced variation of the standard dip that emphasizes the chest and triceps by increasing the range of motion and time under tension. Performed on parallel bars, you lower your body until your forearms rest on the bars, then transition forward and press back up to full lockout.
instructions: Grasp parallel bars with hands shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your forearms rest on the bars, stretching your chest and triceps. Transition forward, keeping your elbows close to your body, then press back up to full lockout. Control the movement throughout to avoid injury.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Straight-Arm Plank with Kick-Back
description: The straight-arm plank with kick-back is a hybrid core and upper-body exercise that targets the abs and triceps while challenging stability and cardiovascular endurance. From a high plank position, you extend one leg back in a controlled kick, maintaining a rigid torso and straight arms throughout.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands under shoulders and toes on ground. Engage core and maintain a rigid torso with straight arms. Lift one leg off ground and extend it back in a controlled kick, keeping hips stable. Lower leg back to starting position and repeat with other leg.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Banded Dip
description: The banded dip is an assisted bodyweight dip that uses a resistance band for support at the bottom of the movement. This reduces load on the shoulders in the deepest position while still challenging the chest and triceps more at the top as band assistance decreases.
instructions: Stand in front of a dip station with a resistance band anchored at the bottom. Loop the band around your hands or shoulders for support, then grip the dip bars with your hands shoulder-width apart. Lower your body until your arms are bent at a 90-degree angle, then straighten your arms to return to the starting position, squeezing your chest and triceps. Keep your core engaged and control the movement throughout the exercise.

name: Bench Dip
description: The bench dip is a bodyweight triceps exercise performed with the hands on a bench behind you and feet on the floor or another surface. Lower your body by bending the elbows, then press back up to extend the arms, emphasizing the triceps.
instructions: Sit on the edge of a bench with your hands grasping the edge and your feet on the floor. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your arms are bent at a 90-degree angle. Straighten your arms to return to the starting position, extending your elbows and emphasizing your triceps. Keep your core engaged and your body straight throughout the movement.

name: Holman Step Triceps Dip
description: Place your hands on the edge of a step or low box behind you with fingers pointing forward and legs extended or knees bent. Lower your body by bending your elbows straight back until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor, keeping your back close to the step. Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position, focusing on squeezing the triceps throughout the movement.
instructions: Place hands on the edge of a step or low box with fingers forward. Lower your body by bending elbows straight back until upper arms are parallel to the floor. Keep your back close to the step and press through palms to straighten arms. Return to the starting position, squeezing triceps throughout the movement.

name: Holman Chair Triceps Dip
description: Place your hands on the edge of a sturdy chair behind you with fingers pointing forward and feet on the floor, legs bent. Keeping your back close to the chair, lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are about parallel to the floor, then press through your palms to extend your elbows and return to the starting position, focusing on squeezing the triceps.
instructions: Sit on the floor with your hands on the edge of a sturdy chair behind you, fingers pointing forward. Place your feet flat on the floor with legs bent. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor. Push through your palms to extend your elbows and return to the starting position, squeezing your triceps.

name: Bodyweight Triceps Rope Press
description: A bodyweight triceps-focused press variation performed using a rope or similar anchor point, emphasizing elbow extension to target the triceps while maintaining a rigid core and neutral spine.
instructions: Hold a rope or anchor point with hands shoulder-width apart. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the ground. Extend your elbows to straighten your arms, squeezing your triceps. Keep your core rigid and spine neutral throughout the movement.

name: Holman Heavy Triceps Overhead Press
description: An overhead triceps press variation performed with bodyweight-style loading or improvised resistance, focusing on extending the elbows overhead to target the triceps while maintaining a stable core and neutral spine.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart. Hold weight or resistance overhead with arms extended and palms facing forward. Lower the weight behind your head by bending your elbows, then press back up to the starting position by extending your elbows and squeezing your triceps. Maintain a stable core and neutral spine throughout the movement.

name: Holman Couch Triceps Dip
description: Place your hands on the edge of a stable couch behind you, fingers pointing forward and legs extended with heels on the floor. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are roughly parallel to the floor, keeping your back close to the couch. Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position, focusing on squeezing the triceps throughout the movement.
instructions: Place your hands on the edge of a stable couch behind you, fingers pointing forward. Extend your legs with heels on the floor, keeping your back close to the couch. Lower your body by bending your elbows until your upper arms are parallel to the floor. Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting position, squeezing your triceps.

name: 30 Arms BFR Close-Grip Push-Up
description: A close-grip push-up variation emphasizing the triceps, typically performed with blood flow restriction (BFR) bands on the upper arms to increase training stimulus at lower loads. Maintain a narrow hand position under the shoulders, keep the elbows close to the body, and lower the chest toward the floor before pressing back up.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Supine Chest Throw
description: Lie on your back holding a medicine ball at chest level, then explosively throw it straight up and catch it under control before repeating. Focus on driving the ball away from your chest using a powerful pressing motion.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the ground. Hold a medicine ball at chest level with both hands. Explosively throw the ball straight up, using a powerful pressing motion, then catch it under control. Return to the starting position and repeat, focusing on proper form and technique.

name: Decline EZ-bar Skullcrusher
description: The decline EZ-bar skullcrusher is a triceps-focused pressing movement performed on a decline bench. The decline angle increases the range of motion compared to a flat bench skullcrusher and emphasizes the lateral head of the triceps. It is typically done for moderate to high repetitions in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Lie on a decline bench with your feet secured. Grasp an EZ-bar with an overhand grip, extending your arms overhead. Lower the bar to your forehead by bending your elbows, then press it back up to the starting position. Control the movement throughout to avoid injury.

name: EZ-Bar Skullcrusher
description: The EZ-bar skullcrusher is an isolation triceps exercise performed lying on a bench. Using an EZ bar to reduce wrist strain, you lower the bar toward the forehead or just behind the head, then extend the elbows to lockout, focusing on controlled reps rather than maximal loads.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with your feet planted on the floor. Grasp the EZ-bar with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Lower the bar toward your forehead or just behind your head by bending your elbows, then extend your elbows to lockout, focusing on controlled movement. Return the bar to the starting position, keeping your upper arms still and only moving your forearms.

name: Lying Close-Grip Barbell Triceps Press To Chin
description: Lie on a flat bench holding a barbell with a close, overhand grip. With elbows tucked in, lower the bar under control toward your chin, keeping upper arms relatively vertical. Press the bar back up by extending your elbows, focusing on squeezing the triceps at the top while maintaining a stable torso and neutral wrists.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench holding a barbell with a close, overhand grip. Lower the bar under control toward your chin, keeping your upper arms still and elbows tucked in. Press the bar back up by extending your elbows, squeezing your triceps at the top. Keep your torso stable and wrists neutral throughout the movement.

name: Barbell Bench Press
description: The classic chest building exercise performed on a flat bench.
instructions: Lie flat on bench. Grip barbell slightly wider than shoulder width. Lower bar to chest, then press up explosively. Keep feet flat on floor and maintain arch in lower back.

name: Incline Dumbbell Bench Press
description: Lie on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward. Press the weights up over your chest until your arms are fully extended, then lower them under control until your elbows are just below shoulder level, keeping your chest lifted and shoulders stable throughout.
instructions: Set bench to 30-45 degree incline. Press dumbbells up and together. Lower with control to stretch position.

name: Push-up
description: The push-up is a classic bodyweight strength exercise that primarily targets the chest, triceps, and shoulders while also engaging the upper back and core for stability.
instructions: Start in plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulders. Lower body until chest nearly touches floor. Push back up to start. Keep core tight throughout.

name: Leg Press
description: The leg press is a machine-based lower-body exercise performed in a seated position that targets the quadriceps while also engaging the glutes and hamstrings. It is commonly used as an accessory movement to build strength and muscle for squats and deadlifts, or as a primary compound exercise for the legs. Typically performed for moderate to high repetitions, it allows heavy loading without placing a direct load on the spine.
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine with feet shoulder-width apart on the platform. Push the platform away from you, extending your legs, then lower it back down to the starting position. Keep your back against the seat and your knees in line with your toes throughout the movement. Slowly control the weight as you return to the starting position.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Walking Lunges
description: Dynamic unilateral leg exercise.
instructions: Step forward into lunge, lowering back knee toward ground. Push through front heel to step forward into next lunge. Alternate legs.

name: Leg Curl
description: The leg curl is a machine-based hamstring isolation exercise, most commonly performed on a seated leg curl machine. It targets the hamstrings by flexing the knee against resistance and is typically done for moderate to high repetitions (8–12+ per set) as part of muscle-building or pre-exhaust work in lower-body training.
instructions: Sit at the leg curl machine with your legs extended. Hook your legs under the padded lever, ensuring your knees are in line with the machine's axis. Lift the lever by curling your legs towards your glutes, squeezing your hamstrings at the top. Slowly lower the lever back to the starting position, controlling the movement throughout.

name: Plank
description: Isometric core stability exercise.
instructions: Support body on forearms and toes. Keep body straight from head to heels. Engage core. Hold position.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Standing Calf Raise
description: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart and press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible, then slowly lower back down to stretch the calves. Maintain a controlled tempo and avoid bouncing.
instructions: Stand upright with feet hip-width apart. Press through the balls of your feet to raise your heels as high as possible. Lower your heels back down slowly to the starting position, stretching your calf muscles. Maintain a controlled tempo throughout the movement.

name: Ab Crunch
description: Basic abdominal exercise.
instructions: Lie on back with knees bent, feet flat. Place hands behind head. Lift shoulders off ground by contracting abs. Lower with control.
accessibilityRequirements: Not Suitable for Lower Back Issues

name: Leg Extension
description: The leg extension is an isolation exercise that targets the quadriceps by extending the knees against resistance from a leg extension machine.
instructions: Sit in machine with back supported. Extend legs to straighten knees, squeezing quads at top. Lower with control to starting position.

name: Machine Chest Press
description: The machine chest press is a seated, machine-based pressing exercise that targets the chest while also engaging the shoulders and triceps. It mimics the motion of a bench press with guided resistance, often allowing multiple grip options such as overhand or neutral. Typically performed for moderate to high repetitions, it is commonly used in muscle-building or pre-exhaust upper-body workouts.
instructions: Sit in machine with back supported. Grip handles at chest level. Press handles forward until arms are extended. Control the return to starting position.

name: Machine Lateral Raise
description: A shoulder isolation exercise performed on a lateral raise machine to target the middle deltoids by lifting the arms out to the sides against resistance.
instructions: Sit in machine with arms at sides. Raise arms out to sides until parallel to floor. Lower with control to starting position.

name: Machine Seated Row
description: The machine seated row is a back-focused pulling exercise performed on a seated row machine. In an upright, forward-facing position, you pull the handles toward your torso to target the lats, rhomboids, and middle and lower traps while keeping the chest supported and torso stable. It is typically performed for moderate to high reps (8–12+ per set) as part of an upper-body or back-focused workout.
instructions: Sit at machine with feet on platform. Pull handles to lower chest, squeezing shoulder blades together. Control the return to starting position.

name: Machine Bicep Curl
description: Machine-based bicep isolation exercise with guided movement.
instructions: Sit in machine with arms positioned on pads. Curl handles up by flexing biceps. Squeeze at the top, then lower with control.

name: Machine Triceps Extension
description: The machine triceps extension is an isolation exercise targeting the triceps using a selectorized or plate-loaded machine. It mimics the motion of a skullcrusher while providing more stability and easier load adjustments, making it suitable for moderate to high rep sets in upper-body or arm-focused workouts.
instructions: Sit in machine with arms positioned on pads. Extend handles down by straightening arms. Squeeze triceps at the bottom, then return with control.

name: Machine Seated Shoulder Press
description: The machine shoulder press is a selectorized or plate-loaded machine exercise that targets the deltoid muscles by mimicking the motion of an overhead press. The guided path of the machine provides stability, making it easier to control the weight and focus on pressing the handles straight up and down. It is typically performed for moderate to high repetitions, such as 8–12 reps per set, as part of an upper-body or shoulder-focused workout.
instructions: Sit in machine with back supported. Start with handles at shoulder level. Press handles overhead until arms are extended. Lower with control.

name: Lat Machine Wide Grip Pulldown
description: Sit at a lat pulldown machine with a wide overhand grip on the bar. With your chest up and core braced, pull the bar down toward your upper chest by driving your elbows down and back, squeezing your upper back and lats. Control the bar as you return to the starting position with arms extended, maintaining tension in the back throughout the movement.
instructions: Sit at machine with wide grip on bar. Pull bar down to upper chest, squeezing lats. Control the return to starting position.
